From a joint monitoring in radio (VLA) and X-ray ( Swift /XRT) in 2011, NGC 4395 seemed to follow a flat radio/X-ray correlation, i.e. L_R L_X^ 0 ( 2013ApJ...774L..25K ). Although that result was not robust due to the very limited dynamic range in both L_R and L_X , it suggested that NGC 4395 might be a source that follows the flat branch of the hybrid radio/X-ray correlation ( 2016MNRAS.456.4377X ; cf. NGC 7213, 2016MNRAS.463.2287X ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4395 as an excluded bulgeless spiral with an active nucleus, noting its blue color and that it is an outlier influencing black-hole scaling fits.

quotes.quote: Although the three bulgeless galaxies (NGC 2478, NGC 4395, and NGC 6926) could be included in our study of the M_ BH - M_ *,tot and M_ BH - M_ *,disk relations, we will use the same sample of 40 galaxies as in Davis:2018 , as this will enable a cleaner comparison of the black hole mass scaling relations for spiral galaxies.

However, it is known that some low-mass, blue, spiral galaxies possess active galactic nuclei --- for example, NGC 4395 Brok:2015 and LEDA 87300 Baldassare:2015,Graham:2016 --and therefore, we are simply probing the red end of the blue sequence. LEDA 87300 has a g^ ^ color equal to 0.41 mag Graham:2016 , which is slightly bluer than NGC 4395 with g^ ^ =0.50 mag . Here, the magnitudes are obtained from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey Data Release 6 ( http://www.sdss.org/dr6/products/catalogs/index.html ) and subsequently corrected for Galactic extinction Schlafly:Finkbeiner:2011 .

This slope is only 69 as steep as the 40-galaxy slope; its shallowness is strongly influenced by the position of NGC 4395. Such a shallow slope is uncharacteristic, given that it is not steeper than the M_ BH - M_ *,sph relation. the positions of the three excluded bulgeless galaxies from our sample and the bulgeless galaxy LEDA 87300 Graham:2016 . Notably, our extrapolated mpfitexy bisector linear regression coincides with the location of LEDA 87300, while NGC 4395 is an outlier.

sources.summary: NGC 4395 is presented as a nearby dwarf galaxy with an IMBH; VLBI, VLA and ALMA data indicate feature E is likely nuclear shocks, with no compact jet base detected.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage NGC 4395 is a dwarf galaxy at a distance of about 4.3 Mpc (scale: 0.021 pc mas ^ 1 ). It hosts an intermediate-mass black hole (IMBH) with a mass between 10 ^4 and 10 ^5 solar masses. The early radio observations of NGC 4395 with the very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) network, High Sensitivity Array (HSA), at 1.4 GHz in 2005 showed that its nucleus has a sub-mJy outflow-like feature (E) extending over 15 mas.

The feature E displays more diffuse structure in the HSA image of 2008 and has no compact substructure detected in the EVN image. Together with the optically thin steep spectrum and the extremely large angular offset (about 220 mas) from the accurate optical Gaia position, we explain the feature E as nuclear shocks likely formed by the IMBH's episodic ejection or wide-angle outflow. The VLA and ALMA observations find a sub-mJy pc-scale diffuse feature, possibly tracing a thermal free-free emission region near the IMBH. There is no detection of a jet base at the IMBH position in the VLBI maps.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4395 as a dwarf Seyfert with an intermediate-mass black hole, measuring its H lag via broad-band reverberation mapping and deriving a low central black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: NGC 4395 is a dwarf Seyfert 1 galaxy with a possible intermediate-mass black hole of several 10^4 solar masses in its center. As a well-studied object, its broad line region size has been measured via H time lag in numerous spectroscopic reverberation mapping (SRM) and narrow-band photometric reverberation mapping (PRM) campaigns. Here we present its H time lag measurement using broad-band photometric data, with the application of our newly-developed ICCF-Cut method as well as the JAVELIN and ^2 methods. utilizing the minute-cadence multi-band light curves obtained from the 2 m FTN and 10.4 m GTC telescopes in recent works, we measured its H lag as approximately 40 90 minutes from broad-band PRM.

With the H emission line velocity dispersion, we calculated its central black hole mass as M_ BH = (8 4) 10^3 M_ . These results are comparable with previous results obtained by narrow-band PRM and SRM, providing further support to an intermediate-mass black hole in NGC 4395. In addition, our study also validates the ICCF-Cut as an effective method for broad-band PRM, which holds the potential for widespread application in the era of large multi-epoch, high-cadence photometric surveys.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4395 as an Sm galaxy with a nuclear star cluster, a nearby low-luminosity Seyfert 1 AGN, and a measured black-hole mass about one-third of the cluster mass.

quotes.quote: Another well known galaxy with a NC, AGN, and MBH is NGC 4395, an Sm type galaxy at a distance of 4.3 Mpc thim04 with M_B=-17.30 paturel03 . The NC in NGC 4395 matthews99 has an M_I=-11.3 , an effective radius of 0.19 (3.9 pc), and a velocity dispersion of <30 5 km/sec filippenko03 . Based on the velocity dispersion, filippenko03 suggest a mass for the NC of 6.2 10^6 M .

Using the method described in samplesec .1 to derive NC masses in late-type galaxies, we estimate the NC mass to be 1.1 10^6 M _ . The AGN is one of the nearest and least luminous Seyfert 1 nuclei filippenko89 . The black hole has a measured mass of 3.6 1.1 10^5 M peterson05 , roughly one-third the mass of the NC.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Suzaku observations of dwarf Seyfert NGC4395, describing its low-luminosity nucleus, strong X-ray variability, hard spectrum, and unusual hard-X-ray-dominated energy output.

quotes.quote: NGC4395 is a dwarf galaxy located at the distance of 4.0 Mpc (Thim et al 2004) that is known to host a very low luminosity Seyfert nucleus (Filippenko, Ho Sargent 1993). The active nucleus is located at the centre of the galaxy, which has no obvious bulge component. Unlike LINER-like, low-luminosity AGN, the optical/UV spectrum of NGC4395 shows broad Balmer emission and high-excitation lines, reminiscent of genuine Seyfert nuclei but of higher luminosity (e.g., Ho, Filippenko Sargent 1997).

We observed NGC4395 with Suzaku to measure the spectral shape above 10 keV and characterise the flux and spectral variability in the softer band by using the X-ray CCD spectrometer over a continuous period of more than 2.5 days. NGC4395 was observed with Suzaku (Mitsuda et al 2006) on 2007 June 02 -- 05, for a total duration of 230 ks.

The distinctive SED shape of NGC4395 implies that the use of the empirical relation to estimate the bolometric luminosity from the 5100 \ luminosity, which is calibrated by the data for luminous AGN, may be inappropriate.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4395 as a dwarf Seyfert with a small black hole, rapid variability, flat X-ray continuum, and spectral changes explained by neutral absorbers.

quotes.quote: We present a new X-ray analysis of the dwarf Seyfert galaxy NGC 4395, based on two archival XMM-Newton and Suzaku observations. This source is well known for a series of remarkable properties: one of the smallest estimated black hole masses among Active Galactic Nuclei (of the order of 10 ^5 M_ ), intense flux variability on very short time-scales (a few tens of seconds), an unusually flat X-ray continuum ( 1.4 over the 2--10 keV energy range). NGC 4395 is also characterized by significant variations of the X-ray spectral shape, and here we show that such behaviour can be explained through the partial occultation by circumnuclear cold absorbers with column densities of 10 ^ 22 -10 ^ 23 cm ^ 2 .

The central regions of the dwarf spiral galaxy NGC 4395 show most of the common signatures of nuclear activity. The optical and ultraviolet (UV) spectra reveal prominent high-ionization forbidden lines on top of a nearly featureless continuum, and broad wings corresponding to gas velocities in excess of 10 ^3 km s ^ 1 are detected in the permitted lines (Filippenko Sargent 1989). Contrary to the objects of the same kind, the emission-line properties, the optical to X-ray variability pattern and the inferred accretion rate of NGC 4395 are those typical of the Seyfert class, of which this source is usually considered to represent the least luminous member.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4395 as a well-known low-mass AGN with rapid, strong X-ray variability, Seyfert-like properties, and no soft excess emission.

quotes.quote: Two well-known low-mass AGN are the spiral galaxy NGC 4395 and the dwarf elliptical galaxy POX 52. Both are seen to be highly variable. A study by Iwasawa00 noted that the rapid and strong X-ray variability of NGC 4395 was consistent with being a scaled-down Seyfert 1 galaxy.

NGC 4395 was found to be the most variable of all AGNs that were previously studied. vaughan05 had previously noted that this object's X-ray emission is the most variable of all AGNs and exceeds 100 in its fractional variability amplitude. Furthermore, its normalized excess variance was the highest value in comparison with more massive AGNs. Both NGC 4395 and POX 52 exhibited broad spectral characteristics of Seyfert 1 galaxies, but NGC 4395 lacked soft excess emission that is a known characteristic to narrow-line Seyfert 1s (NLS1s).

sources.summary: The paper measures NGC 4395's central black hole using gas dynamical modeling, describing a bulgeless Seyfert spiral with rotating molecular gas and a low-mass black hole.

quotes.quote: NGC 4395 is a bulgeless spiral galaxy, harboring one of the nearest known type 1 Seyfert nuclei. Although there is no consensus on the mass of its central engine, several estimates suggest it to be one of the lightest massive black holes (MBHs) known. We present the first direct dynamical measurement of the mass of this MBH from a combination of two-dimensional gas kinematic data, obtained with the adaptive optics assisted near infrared integral field spectrograph Gemini/NIFS, and high-resolution multiband photometric data from Hubble Space Telescope 's Wide Field Camera 3 ( HST /WFC3).

From the Gemini/NIFS observations, we derive the kinematics of warm molecular hydrogen gas as traced by emission through the H _2 1--0 S(1) transition. These kinematics show a clear rotational signal, with a position angle orthogonal to NGC 4395's radio jet. Our best fitting tilted ring models of the kinematics of the molecular hydrogen gas contain a black hole with mass M=4_ 3 ^ +8 10^5 (3 uncertainties) embedded in a nuclear star cluster of mass M=2 10^6 .

sources.summary: The paper measures an 83-minute reverberation lag in NGC 4395, deriving a roughly 10,000-solar-mass black hole and showing this bulgeless dwarf AGN remains consistent with the black-hole mass relation.

quotes.quote: Here we report an exceptionally low reverberation delay of 83 14 minutes between variability in the accretion disk and high velocity H emission from the nucleus of the bulgeless dwarf galaxy NGC 4395. Combined with the H line-of-sight velocity dispersion _ line =426 1 km s ^ 1 , this lag determines a mass of about 10,000 M_ for the black hole. This mass is among the smallest central black hole masses reported, near the low end of expected masses for heavy ``seeds'' , and the best direct mass measurement for a galaxy of this size. Despite the lack of a bulge, NGC 4395 is consistent with the M_ BH _ relation when _ is measured from the central region.

The dwarf galaxy NGC 4395 ( M_ 8 10^8 M_ ) is the least luminous active galactic nucleus (AGN) known to date , with a monochromatic luminosity at 5100 \ L_ 5100 < 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 . Prior efforts to determine the black hole mass indicate that it hosts an intermediate-mass black hole with M_ BH in the range 10^4 - 5 10^5 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper adds NGC 4395 as a low-mass, bulgeless, low-surface-brightness dwarf Seyfert comparison and derives gas and stellar masses for it.

quotes.quote: We also augmented our sample by including the dwarf Seyfert NGC 4395, as it should provide an interesting comparison as the lowest-mass AGN with a direct black hole mass constraint, hosted by a bulgeless low surface brightness galaxy.

We derived M _ GAS for NGC 4395 using the HI flux available in the All Digital HI Catalog edd2005 with Equations eq:2 and eq:3 . The 140-foot Green Bank telescope was used to observe NGC 4395 and produce the subsequent HI flux we have used here. The beam size of the 140-foot is 21 ' , large enough to encompass the angular extent of NGC 4395, therefore allowing use of Equation eq:2 .

We derived the stellar mass for NGC 4395 using the integrated B band magnitude and B-V color from , and the stellar M/L ratio from . The contamination from the AGN hosted by NGC 4395 is negligible, yet we conservatively adopt uncertainties of 0.28 mag for the color and 0.2 mag for the magnitude.

sources.summary: The paper monitors NGC 4395's very low-luminosity Seyfert 1 nucleus, describing the nearby dwarf host, weak AGN, central bar-like structure, variability, and reverberation size-luminosity behavior.

quotes.quote: We present a variability study of the lowest-luminosity Seyfert 1 nucleus of the galaxy NGC 4395 based on photometric monitoring campaigns in 2017 and 2018. Using 22 ground-based and space telescopes, we monitored NGC 4395 with a 5 minute cadence during a period of 10 days and obtained light curves in the ultraviolet (UV), V , J , H , and K/Ks bands as well as narrow-band . The root-mean-square (RMS) variability is 0.13 mag in the UVM2 and V filter light curves, decreasing down to 0.01 mag in the K filter.

The nearby galaxy NGC 4395 at a distance of 4.4 Mpc is a unique testbed for studying intermediate-mass black holes. As a Seyfert 1 galaxy, NGC 4395 hosts an active galactic nucleus (AGN) with extremely low luminosity; the bolometric luminosity is typically reported to be below 10^ 41 erg s^ 1 e.g., Filippenko&Sargent89, Filippenko+93, Lira+99, Moran+99, Filippenko&Ho03 . The host galaxy is classified as a dwarf galaxy with a stellar mass 10^ 9 \ Filippenko&Sargent89, Reines+13 , and there is no clear sign of a bulge while a bar-like central structure is identified denBrok+15 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4395 as a local Seyfert whose UV-to-X-ray lag can challenge reprocessing for a low black-hole mass and can test disk truncation scenarios.

quotes.quote: We find that our model, without the need of light echoing, can well reproduce the observed UV to X-ray lags and the optical to UV lags simultaneously in four local Seyfert galaxies, including NGC 4151, NGC 4395, NGC 4593, and NGC 5548. In our scenario, relatively larger UV to X-ray lag is expected for AGN with smaller innermost disk radius and thus more compact corona. Interestingly, for these Seyfert galaxies studied in this work, sources with relatively larger UV to X-ray lags do have broader Fe K lines, indicative of relativistic broadening due to more compact corona and smaller innermost disk radius.

Assuming a large BH mass for NGC 4395 Peterson2005 , McHardy2018 find that only in NGC 4395 the UV to X-ray lag predicted by the reprocessing model is consistent with that observed (see also the comparison between the magenta dot-dashed line and the blue open star in the bottom-right panel of Figure fig:ngc4395 ). However, if NGC 4395 holds a BH with mass as low as that reported by Woo2019 , the reprocessing model would be also challenged by the same puzzling large UV to X-ray lag of NGC 4395 (see the bottom-left panel of Figure fig:ngc4395 ). Based on our predictions shown in Figure fig:ngc4395 , future intensive and sensitive X-ray/UV/optical monitoring on NGC 4395 would help discriminating its BH mass as well as the truncated radius of the cold accretion disk.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4395 as a lopsided galaxy whose SFR infrared faintness is mainly due to compact physical size rather than low surface brightness.

quotes.quote: The star-forming regions (SFRs) in the peculiar galaxies NGC 660, NGC 1512, NGC 4395 and NGC 4618 are studied. The relationships between such characteristics of star forming regions as UV, nearand far-IR fluxes, as well as in the H and HI lines, surface brightness in these ranges, and the scatter of the radial velocities of ionized and neutral hydrogen are considered. It is shown that in all the galaxies considered, the IR fluxes from SFRs are less than in the “normal” galaxies, but for different reasons: in the galaxies with signs of recent interaction NGC 660 and NGC 1512, this is due to the low surface brightness of SFRs; in the lopsided galaxies NGC 4395 and NGC 4618, the low brightness of SFRs in the infrared range is due to their compact size.

Thus, although the asymmetry of disk galaxies is a fairly common property ( ), in two specific cases (NGC 4395 and NGC 4618) we see noticeable differences from both interacting and “normal” galaxies. It should be noted that the galaxy NGC 4395 is isolated, and the galaxy NGC 4618 although usually considered in conjunction with the galaxy NGC 4625, may also not be interacting ( ). It is obvious that the interaction is not the only reason for the formation of SFRs with a small flux in the IR range.

sources.summary: The paper monitors the active nucleus of the nearby dwarf galaxy NGC 4395, resolving its narrow-line region and revising its intermediate-mass black hole mass.

quotes.quote: We present the results of a high-cadence spectroscopic and imaging monitoring campaign of the active galactic nucleus (AGN) of NGC 4395. High signal-to-noise-ratio spectra were obtained at the Gemini-N 8 m telescope using the GMOS integral field spectrograph (IFS) on 2019 March 7, and at the Keck-I 10 m telescope using the Low-Resolution Imaging Spectrometer (LRIS) with slitmasks on 2019 March 3 and April 2. Photometric data were obtained with a number of 1 m-class telescopes during the same nights. The narrow-line region (NLR) is spatially resolved; therefore, its variable contributions to the slit spectra make the standard procedure of relative flux calibration impractical.

NGC 4395 is a nearby dwarf galaxy with a stellar mass of 10^ 9 \ Filippenko&Sargent89, Reines+13 . It contains the least luminous broad-lined active galactic nucleus (AGN) known to date, with a bolometric luminosity lower than 10^ 41 erg s^ 1 Filippenko&Sargent89, Filippenko+93, Lira+99, Moran+99, Filippenko&Ho03, Cho+20 .

sources.summary: The paper studies optical continuum reverberation in the dwarf Seyfert NGC 4395, measuring minute-scale griz lags and relating them to its low-mass black hole and compact AGN.

quotes.quote: The nearby dwarf spiral galaxy NGC 4395 contains a broad-lined active galactic nucleus (AGN) of exceptionally low luminosity powered by accretion onto a central black hole of very low mass ( 10^4-10^5 ). In order to constrain the size of the optical continuum emission region through reverberation mapping, we carried out high-cadence photometric monitoring of NGC 4395 in the griz filter bands on two consecutive nights in 2022 April using the four-channel MuSCAT3 camera on the Faulkes Telescope North at Haleakal a Observatory. Correlated variability across the griz bands is clearly detected, and the , i , and z band light curves show lags of 8.4^ +1.0 _ 1.1 , 14.2^ +1.2 _ 1.4 , and 20.4^ +2.0 _ 2.1 minutes with respect to the g band when measured using the full-duration light curves.

The active galactic nucleus (AGN) in the dwarf spiral galaxy NGC 4395 is the prototypical example of an AGN containing a very low-mass black hole (BH). First identified from the broad wings on its \ emission line Filippenko1989 , it exhibits all of the characteristics of an accretion-powered Seyfert 1 nucleus, including a high-excitation photoionized narrow-line region Kraemer1999 , highly variable X-ray emission Lira1999, Iwasawa2000, Shih2003, Moran2005 , and a compact radio core Wrobel2001 , but its bolometric luminosity is just 5 10^ 40 erg s \ Moran2005 , and the broad component of its \ emission line has a dispersion of only 600 \ Cho2021 . The mass of the BH in NGC 4395 is not precisely determined, but a variety of methods (including indirect estimates, reverberation mapping, and dynamical studies) point to a mass in the range 10^4 to a few 10^5 \ Kraemer1999, Shih2003, Filippenko2003, Peterson2005, denBrok2015, Woo2019, Cho2020 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4395 as a kinematically lopsided Sm galaxy whose velocity field resembles retrograde models, estimating its halo contribution and disc mass while noting its faint Seyfert nucleus.

quotes.quote: This characteristic asymmetry is similar to that in the velocity-map of the lopsided Sm galaxy NGC 4395 presented by Swaters et al. SwatersMN . This late-type system is one of the lowest-luminosity galaxies to have a Seyfert nucleus, which in turn is the faintest Seyfert 1 nucleus known , with M_B = -10.3 or L = 2 10^6 . Presumably a central black hole is present in the nucleus.

For example, the velocity field of the galaxy NGC 4395 looks similar to some of our retrograde models. Comparing our Fig. fig:NGC4395 to the velocity-maps of models A, B, E and F, we estimate that this galaxy has a value for between 20 and 30 . The rotation curves of these models lie always close to the curves of the isolated disc (in other words, these models are `maximum disc'); we can use the total width of the rotation curve ( v_ max v_ min ) as a measure of the disc mass. We infer a total mass for the disc of NGC 4395 of about 3.5 10^ 9 M _ within 8.5 kpc of the centre, taking Swaters' distance of 3.8 Mpc and inclination i=46^ .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4395 as a nearby dwarf spiral Seyfert 1 with no discernible bulge, a compact active nucleus, narrow lines, and an intermediate-mass black hole estimate.

quotes.quote: The better-known case is the nearby ( D 4 Mpc), late-type, dwarf spiral galaxy NGC 4395. fs89 found that NGC 4395 contains the least luminous known Seyfert 1 nucleus. Despite the fact that this galaxy has no discernible bulge, and thus might not be expected to contain a central black hole, its nucleus exhibits all of the characteristics of Seyfert activity, including broad emission lines fs89 , a compact, unresolved nonstellar continuum source in the optical and ultraviolet fhs93 , unresolved radio emission with a high brightness temperature wfh01 , and rapid X-ray variability iwa00, sif03, mor04 . The central stellar velocity dispersion is < 30 , which combined with the radius of the central star cluster (3.9 pc) gives a firm upper limit of M < 6 10^6 \ for the combined mass of the central star cluster plus black hole fh03 . The black hole itself has a likely mass of 10^4-10^5 , based on the X-ray variability timescale sif03 and extrapolation of the linewidth-luminosity-mass correlations for Seyfert galaxies fh03 .
Finding more active galaxies like NGC 4395 would be a valuable step toward understanding the demographics of black holes with M < 10^6 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4395 as a low-luminosity Seyfert 1 with a transient 396-second X-ray periodicity whose short period follows expectations from its unusually low luminosity.

quotes.quote: Moran et al. \ (2005) present analysis of the Chandra data from the low-luminosity Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 4395. A period of 396 seconds was found in the light curve with estimated global significance of 97.6 . In this case, we again found evidence that the signal is transient; no signal appears in the first half of the observation, but in the second half a strong signal appears with estimated global confidence of 99.95 at a period of 396 seconds.

Then what about NGC 4395? It is notable for being the lowest luminosity Seyfert 1, with a 2--10 keV luminosity of only 8 10^ 39 erg s^ 1 (Moran et al. \ 2005), much lower than a typical Seyfert 1 galaxy. The fact that we observe the period to scale with the luminosity in the EUVE sample suggests that NGC 4395 should have an exceptionally low period; therefore, since it is unique, luminosity-wise, it makes sense that it is unique, period-wise. On the other hand, we observe that the periodicity appears to be transient in both NGC 4395 and RX J0437.1 4711 in that in both cases it is detected only in the second halves of the light curves.

sources.summary: The paper uses a high-quality Keck spectrum of NGC 4395 to interpret its Balmer-line wings as optically thin electron scattering in broad-line-region gas.

quotes.quote: A high quality Keck spectrum of the \ line in NGC 4395 reveals symmetric exponential wings, f_v e^ v/ , with 500 km s ^ 1 . The wings extend out to 2500 km s ^ 1 from the line core, and down to a flux density of 10^ 3 of the peak flux density. Numerical and analytic calculations indicate that exponential wings are expected for optically thin, isotropic, thermal electron scattering.

The \ wings in NGC 4395 are well fit by an electron scattering model with _e=0.34 , and an electron temperature T_e=1.1 10^4 K. Such conditions are produced in photoionized gas with an ionization parameter U 0.3 , as expected in the broad line region (BLR). Similar analysis of the [ O III ] 5007 line yields _e<0.01 , consistent with the lower ionization in the narrow line region.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 4395 as an exceptionally low-luminosity AGN with a measured C IV BLR size, failed optical H-lag campaigns, and placement far below high-luminosity reverberation targets.

quotes.quote: The one exception is NGC 4395 ( L_ (5100 )=5.9 10^ 39 ergs s ^ 1 ) in which Peterson et al. (2005) measured the BLR size of its C iv emission line to be 1 0.3 light hr. This is consistent with the size expected from extrapolating the R_ BLR - L relation to lower luminosities. However, two optical campaigns to determine the H time lag of NGC 4395 were so far unsuccessful due to bad weather (Desroches et al. 2006 and contribution in these proceedings; Kaspi et al. 2007, in preparation).

NGC 4395 is four orders of magnitude lower in luminosity than those four AGNs and S5 0836+71 is 3 orders of magnitude higher. Thus, a preliminary C iv size---UV-luminosity relation over 7 orders of magnitude in luminosity can be determined.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4395 as the closest INTEGRAL AGN detection, an unusually low-luminosity Seyfert with a small black hole, strong X-ray variability, and a first reported detection above 10 keV.

quotes.quote: NGC 4395 holds the distinction of hosting the Seyfert 1 nucleus with the lowest known optical luminosity , and one of the intrinsically weakest nuclear X-ray sources observed so far . NGC 4395 is also highly unusual because it harbours a small black hole mass of 10 ^ 4-5 solar masses compared to typical values found in more typical Seyfert galaxies In addition, it shows some of the strongest X-ray variability known in radio-quiet AGN with variations of nearly an order of magnitude in a few thousand seconds. In other respects, however, such as optical and ultraviolet emission (and absorption) line properties, it resembles its more luminous counterparts .

This source has never been reported at energies above 10 keV and so the INTEGRAL detection is the first one in these high energy band. NGC4395 is detected by IBIS with a significance of 5 ; a simple power law provides a good fit to the IBIS data in the range 17-150 keV ( ^2 =8.8 for 10 d.o.f.): the photon index is flat (1.0 0.7) and the 20-100 keV flux is 1.4 10 ^ 11 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4395 as the nearby benchmark low-mass Seyfert dwarf, comparing its black hole mass, late-type morphology, spectra, and much lower Eddington ratio with POX 52.

quotes.quote: The best nearby example of a black hole in the <10^6 \ mass range is that in the dwarf Seyfert 1 galaxy NGC 4395 FilS89,fh03 . NGC 4395 is an essentially bulgeless, late-type dwarf galaxy with type 1 Seyfert characteristics such as broad emission lines FilS89, FHS93 and rapid X-ray variability Iwa00, Shih03, Moran05 . Located at a distance of only 4.3  Mpc thim04 , it is an excellent target for multiwavelength observations despite the tiny luminosity of its central engine ( 6 10^ 40 ; Moran \ 1999). A recent ultraviolet (UV) reverberation-mapping measurement found   = (3.6   1.1)   10^5   \ Pet05 .

Thus, although POX 52 and NGC 4395 have very similar black hole masses and emission-line spectra that are nearly identical in appearance, the AGN in POX 52 is 300 times more luminous than that in NGC 4395, which has an estimated / \ 10^ 3 Pet05 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies 7524 as a prototype kinematically lopsided galaxy whose two sides show different rotation-curve behavior and complex velocity-field structure.

quotes.quote: 7524 is a prototype of a kinematically lopsided galaxy. The rotation curve on the receding side continues to rise, whereas on the approaching side the rotation curve quickly reaches a flat part (see also Swaters et al. \ 1999).

7524 . This is the best resolved galaxy in our sample, yet the \ and \ rotation curves show significant differences. The velocity field of this galaxy shows a great deal of structure, including kinematic lopsidedness (see Swaters et al. \ 1999). The structure seen in the dBB curve is also seen along the major axis of the \ data.

sources.summary: The paper uses dwarf Seyfert galaxy NGC 4395 as a low-mass black-hole AGN stability test, finding its low Eddington ratio makes outbursts unexpected and uninformative.

quotes.quote: However, the exceptional object is NGC 4395, with the black hole mass of 3.6 10^ 5 M_ ( ). In this source, in principle, we could observe the variability due to radiation pressure instability. As was shown by , the outbursts for the central black hole of the mass 10^ 7 M_ , should last below 100 years, so for a mass 30 times smaller, the outbursts should last 3 yearsNo such outbursts are observed. However, this fact is actually consistent with our expectations, since the Eddington ratio in this source is only 1.2 10^ 3 . The source is thus stable with both P_ tot and P_ gas P_ tot mechanisms and provides no useful constraints for the parameterization of the viscous torque.

sources.summary: The paper monitors NGC 4395 as the lowest-mass Seyfert, finding stronger X-ray than radio variability and placing it on a steeper disk-jet luminosity track.

quotes.quote: However, as NGC 4395 is the lowest known mass Seyfert galaxy, we have used it to examine the simultaneous X-ray ( Swift ) and radio (VLA) correlation in a SMBH in a reasonably timed observing campaign. We find that the intrinsic X-ray variability is stronger than the radio variability, and that the fluxes are only weakly or tentatively coupled, similar to prior results obtained in NGC 4051. If the corona and the base of the jet are one and the same, this may suggest that the corona in radio-quiet AGN filters disk variations, only transferring the strongest and/or most sustained variations into the jet. Further, when both NGC 4395 and NGC 4051 are placed on the stellar-mass L_X L_R plane, they appear to reside on the steeper L_X L_R track. This suggests that SMBHs also follow two distinct tracks just as stellar-mass black holes do, and supports the idea that the same physical disk-jet mechanisms are at play across the mass scale.

sources.summary: The paper describes face-on NGC 4395 as hosting both a nuclear cluster and one of the least luminous Type 1 AGNs, with reverberation mapping giving low central black-hole mass estimates.

quotes.quote: NGC 4395 is oriented face-on at a distance of 4.3 Mpc thim2004 in the M94 Group. The existence of a NC in NGC 4395 was explicitly demonstrated by f&h2003 , from GALFIT analysis of a /WFPC2 I band image. The discovery of faint broad hydrogen and helium lines in its optical spectrum revealed that NGC 4395 contains one of the least luminous Type 1 AGNs known f&s1989 . A /STIS reverberation-mapping program by peterson2005 yielded an estimate of 3.6 10^5 \ for the mass of the central BH, which implies that the AGN is very underluminous and is accreting at just 1.2 10^ 3 times the Eddington rate. In a recent photometric reverberation mapping campaign, edri2012 measured an \ lag of just 3.6 hours, corresponding to a BH mass of 5 10^ 4 \ (the large discrepancy in the BH mass estimates from this study and Peterson et al. is in part due to the different assumed values of the scaling factor in the BH mass calculation, which depends on the unknown geometry and kinematics of the broad-line region).

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 4395 with a power law plus ionized absorber, requiring photon-index variation while noting absorption variability may add scatter.

quotes.quote: NGC 4395 The large quantity of Swift data of NGC 4395 allowed us to produce 22 flux-binned spectra. We find that a model consisting of a power law and an ionised absorber gives a good fit to the Swift data. The best-fitting model of the flux-binned spectra requires only the photon index to vary ( ^2_R = 1.03 ); although the model in which the ionisation state and absorbing column are free to vary does give a lower ^2 , the extra complexity is not statically required ( f = 0.31, p = 1.00 ). The spectrum has previously been modelled with multiple absorbers with varying ionisation states and absorbing columns e.g. Iwasawa2010,Nardini2011 . The lack of a need for variability in the absorption, despite previous work showing absorption variability to be present, is perhaps due to the flux-binning averaging the absorption over the period covered by the data, but this is likely to add scatter to the parameter values.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4395 as a low-redshift spiral AGN host and Seyfert galaxy, with narrow-line optical spectrum, central velocity dispersion, and a sub-million-solar-mass black hole estimate.

quotes.quote: 7 z=0.004760, mathewson96 , is an ideal source for such timing studies. 7 is a spiral galaxy, morphologically classified as SAB(rs)bc, which hosts an AGN. The optical spectrum of 7 is dominated by narrow emission lines but a weak broad wing to H has been noted by filippenko84 and hughes03 , leading to a tentative classification as a Seyfert type 1.9. However an O i Bowen fluorescence line (at =8446 ) was noted by morris85 , leading to a classification as a Seyfert type 1 galaxy. The central stellar velocity dispersion is 60 km s ^ 1 gu06 , yielding a BH-mass estimate of (0.87 0.45) 10^6 mchardy13 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4395 as a nearby bulgeless Seyfert 1.8/1.9 galaxy with a very low-mass supermassive black hole and blueshifted intermediate optical line components.

quotes.quote: NGC 4395 is a nearby, small bulgeless (Sd) galaxy, known to host one of the smallest super massive BH (10 ^4 10 ^5 M _ ) ever found. classified the nucleus of NGC 4395 as a type 1.8 or type 1.9 Seyfert using the relative intensities of the narrow and broad components. On the basis of the appearence of the Balmer lines, classified it as a type 1.8 Seyfert. The best fit of the most relevant emission lines of NGC 4395 are shown in Figure fig:NGC4395 . In the optical spectrum, besides the narrow and broad components, evidence was found of intermediate components in the [ O III ] and [ N II ] lines which are blueshifted of 259 and 291 , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4395 as a bulgeless Sd galaxy with a Seyfert 1 nucleus and a strong central active IMBH candidacy supported by emission lines, radio/X-ray activity, and low stellar velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: NGC 4395 is an Sd type, bulgeless galaxy with a Seyfert 1 nucleus. The presence of an active MBH at the center of a bulgeless galaxy is exceptional in itself (see e.g. Ferrarese et al. 2000 , Merritt Ferrarese 2001 ), however the distinctness of NGC 4395 stems from the fact that it is one of the most likely candidates for a central, active IMBH. The presence of broadened optical and UV emission lines first revealed the AGN nature of NGC 4395 . Compact radio emission , suggestive of strong jet-like outflows and highly variable and non-thermal X-ray emission (e.g. ) solidified its AGN nature. More remarkably, the source's central stellar velocity dispersion was less than 30 km/s , indicating a mass of the central MBH that is less than 10^5 M_ based on the M_ BH _ sph relation.

sources.summary: The review discusses NGC 4395 as a prototype low-mass AGN with uncertain reverberation-mapping black hole masses and line-width driven mass disagreements.

quotes.quote: The initial effort to monitor the prototype low-mass AGN NGC 4395 gave only a marginally useful constraint on the lag for H desrochesetal2006 , and preference has been given to the C IV 1549 measurement of petersonetal2005 , which led to a black hole mass estimate of = (3.6 1.1) 10^5 . This mass is consistent, within the considerable tolerance of the uncertainties, with the direct dynamical estimate of = 4^ +8 _ 3 10^5 \ by denbroketal2015 . wooetal2019 recently advocate a markedly lower value of 9100 , based on a short, 80-min lag detected from a narrow-band H reverberation mapping campaign with rapid sampling.

Even the nearby AGN NGC 4395 ( D 4 Mpc) has published reverberation mapping masses that differ by more than an order of magnitude petersonetal2005,edrietal2012,wooetal2019 . There are legitimate reasons that we do not yet know to fully interpret the reverberation masses. One problem is the f_ vir uncertainty. Beyond that, in the case of NGC 4395, the disagreement in black hole mass is mostly attributable to different line widths between the UV resonance line C IV and the optical recombination line H .

sources.summary: The paper fits flux-resolved spectra of NGC 4395 with partial-covering neutral and ionized absorbers, while noting the model complexity prevents MYTorus fitting.

quotes.quote: NGC 4395: We fit the flux-resolved spectra obtained during two \ observations and a \ observation. Our model consists of a partial covering neutral absorber and two ionized partial covering absorbers. A detailed analysis is presented in . The only difference between the current work and is that now we tie the Pexmon normalization to the one of the power law component and we let R be free. This does not alter any of the previous results.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4395 as the sample's lowest-luminosity strongly barred galaxy, notes its central oval distortion, and finds shallow O/H and N/O gradients from strong-line methods.

quotes.quote: In fact, several galaxies classified as SA in RC3 (NGC 2541, NGC 1068, NGC 5194, NGC 4395) contain a central oval distortion chapelon99,scoville88,zaritsky93,buta_atlas , while other galaxies classified as SAB (e.g. NGC 2903, NGC 925) have bars comparable in length and/or ellipticity to those in SB galaxies, or have no apparent bar, as is the case for NGC 2403.

NGC 4395: This is the lowest luminosity strongly barred galaxy in our sample and its derived radial 12+ (O/H) and (N/O) gradients are very relevant for paperII . It is not possible to derive reliably the gradients from the T_e based method. However, the analysis of the slope values derived from strong-line methods indicates a shallow gradient for both the 12+log(O/H) and the log (N/O) profiles in this galaxy: (a) The median of the seven values of _ O/H derived from strong-line methods is 0.005 dex kpc \ with a standard deviation of 0.007 dex kpc . (b) Regarding the slope of the (N/O) radial profile, the HCM and R methods yield 0.01 0.01 dex kpc \ and 0.03 0.01 dex kpc , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4395 as the lowest-mass dwarf AGN with TESS optical light curves and tests how alternative or excluded SMBH masses affect the damping-timescale relation.

quotes.quote: Finally, we include the SMBH in the dwarf AGN NGC 4395, which has the lowest mass in our sample of 10^4 M_ and high-cadence optical light curves from the Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite [ TESS, ] .
For our AGN sample, the optical light curves are irregularly sampled (except for the TESS light curve for NGC 4395), and there are seasonal gaps in multi-year light curves.

Our adopted SMBH mass measurement of NGC 4395 differs from previous measurements by an order of magnitude . If we instead adopt a SMBH mass of 3.6 10^5 M_ for NGC 4395, we derive a nearly identical correlation between the damping timescale and BH mass: _ damping = 105^ +11 _ 12 \ days \ M_ BH 10^8 \ M_ ^ 0.39^ +0.05 _ 0.05 . If we exclude NGC 4395 entirely from the sample, we also derive a nearly identical correlation: _ damping = 106^ +11 _ 13 \ days \ M_ BH 10^8 \ M_ ^ 0.38^ +0.05 _ 0.05 .

sources.summary: The paper applies photoreverberation mapping to NGC 4395, describing it as a low-luminosity Seyfert 1 AGN and measuring H emission lags of about 2.5-2.6 hours.

quotes.quote: In addition to the simulated light curves, we test our implementation of photoRM method on the observed photometric light curves of NGC 4395 obtained during the 9-night monitoring period conducted by . Observations were performed by Wise Observatory's 1m telescope using the Sloan Digital Sky Survey's g , and i bands. In this case study, we have used only the and i bands (Fig. fig3 ), since the band covers the H line which has larger contribution to the total flux than the H line covered by the g band. The i band covers the pure continuum emission. Because NGC 4395 is a low luminous Seyfert 1 galaxy, the expected time-lag is only of the order of a few hours, as confirmed by the spectroscopic RM campaigns (Peterson et al., Peterson2005 , Desroches et al., Desroches2006 ).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4395 as a bulgeless Sm spiral, adopting a Cepheid distance and estimating its central black-hole mass, globular-cluster count, luminosity, and specific frequency.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present new measurements and analyses of the globular cluster systems of the Sab galaxies NGC 3368 (M 96), NGC 4736 (M 94) and NGC 4826 (M 64), and the Sm galaxy NGC 4395. Their surface brightness fluctuations (SBF) distances are 10.4 ^ +1.1 _ 1.0 Mpc for NGC 3368, 5.0 0.4 Mpc for NGC 4736, and 7.3 0.7 Mpc for NGC 4826 tonr01,blak10 . NGC 4395 is completely devoid of a bulge, and hence lacks an SBF measurement; we adopt a Cepheid distance of 4.3 0.4 Mpc thim04 . The masses of their central supermassive black holes are (3.6 1.1) 10^5 \ M_ for NGC 4395 pete05 ; (7.5 1.5) 10^6 \ M_ for NGC 3368; 6.8 ^ +1.5 _ 1.6 10^6 \ M_ for NGC 4736, and (1.6 0.4) 10^6 \ M_ for NGC 4826 korm13a .
Hence, we get N_ GC = 35^ +20 _ 16 for NGC 3368, N_ GC = 15 7 for NGC 4395, N_ GC = 46 18 for NGC 4736, and N_ GC = 69^ +31 _ 28 for NGC 4826.

For NGC 4395, rc3 report B_ T,0 = 10.57 0.54 mag and ( B \ V ) _ T,0 = 0.46 0.08 mag; this results in M_V = -18.06 0.59 mag and S_N = 0.90 0.55 , with statistical errors only, and S_N = 0.90 0.65 if the uncertainty in the number of obscured clusters is included.

sources.summary: The paper compares its NGC 4395 break-frequency estimate with Burke et al., attributing the offset to combining TESS and ASAS-SN data and recovering agreement when using only TESS.

quotes.quote: burke20 performed a similar break frequency analysis on NGC 4395. They used the TESS sector 22 light curve to construct the PSD and fit a broken power law. They found a break frequency of ( _ br / day ^ 1 )=-0.94 0.25 , which differs from our value of ( _ br / day ^ 1 )=-0.48 0.11 . This may be due to using both TESS and ASAS-SN data. Using only the TESS PSDs, we find ( _ br / day ^ 1 )=-0.93 0.07 , which agrees with burke20 .

sources.summary: The paper adds NGC 4395 as a low-mass irregular galaxy, calculates its stellar specific angular momentum from literature data, excludes it from fitting, and notes its jet evidence.

quotes.quote: These are the MilkyWay and 2MASX J23453268-0449256 (hereafter J2345-0449) with large stellar mass, and NGC 4395 with small stellar mass were added to construct our sample.
For the Milky Way, NGC 4395, and the giant radio source J2345-0449, their j_t are calculated by using Eq. eq:jt and observable data from literature studies, which listed in Tab. tab:data .

The fitting also does not include NGC 4395 since its mass is much lower than the others and is an irregular galaxy whose gas component seem to dominate Repetto2017 . Our fitting results for three groups are shown in Fig. fig:1 and Tab. tab:fit . Here the fitting parameters for all sample are =0.529^ +0.073 _ 0.073 and =-2.638^ +0.790 _ 0.790 , which show good agreement with the study of , where =0.52 0.04 and =-2.54 0.05 for all spirals in their work. While for the group with jets, =0.607^ +0.235 _ 0.235 and = -3.575^ +2.394 _ 2.394 , which show large fitting uncertainties. The reason is that this group is concentrated in the large stellar mass range. For NGC 4395, if its j_t is calculated with the stellar mass and stellar velocity, it shows good agreement with the fitted relation. If all baryonic matters are included, the j_t deviates from the best fitting, although it is still covered by 1 fitting uncertainty. Unlike stellar composition, the gas component is not in an equilibrium state, its high velocity may be speeded up by the jet, which results in a large j_t value. After cross-matching the samples with recent radio- and x-ray observation studies e.g., 2013ApJ...774L..25K, Baldi_2021 , 13 galaxies are labeled as ones with jets. Among them, there are nine galaxies with the presence of a jet or jets in the observations, i.e., NGC 2639 2019MNRAS.490L..26S , NGC 3031 (M81) 2019MNRAS.488.4317B , NGC 4395 2013ApJ...774L..25K , NGC 4594 (M104) 2013ApJ...779....6H , NGC 2841 Baldi_2018 , NGC 3198 Baldi_2018 , NGC 7217 Baldi_2018 , J2345-0449 Bagchi_2014 and the Milky Way 2021ApJ...922..254C .

sources.summary: The paper focuses on NGC 4395, a nearby dwarf galaxy hosting an intermediate-mass black hole, using high-cadence multi-band monitoring to find departures from DRW variability and plausible timescale-dependent color variability.

quotes.quote: Here, utilizing the high-cadence multi-wavelength monitoring on NGC 4395 harboring an intermediate-mass BH, we unveil at the intermediate-mass scale for the first time, prominent departures from the DRW process at timescales shorter than hours in all three nights and bands, and plausible timescale-dependent CVs in the two longest nights of observation. Furthermore, comparing SFs of NGC 4395 to four AGNs at the supermassive scale, we suggest a new scaling relation between the timescale ( ; across nearly three orders of magnitude) and the BH mass ( M_ BH ): M^ _ BH where the exponent is likely 0.6 - 0.8 .

Recently, exploring the optical variation properties of AGNs Burke2020ApJ...899..136B,Montano2022, McHardy2023MNRAS.519.3366M has been extended to a nearby dwarf galaxy, NGC 4395 at a redshift of z = 0.001064 ; Filippenko1989,denBrok2015ApJ...809..101D , harboring an intermediate-massive BH (IMBH) with a BH mass of 10^ 4-5 M_ e.g., Filippenko&Ho2003ApJ...588L..13F, Peterson2005ApJ...632..799P, Woo2019NatAs...3..755W, Cho2021ApJ...921...98C . On one hand, using month-long and 30 minute-cadence observations of the Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite (TESS), found that the optical variation of NGC 4395 can also be described by the same DRW process as the luminous quasars. On the other hand, both Montano2022 and McHardy2023MNRAS.519.3366M mainly focus on the inter-band lags. Here, benefiting from these recent very high cadence (as short as several seconds to several minutes) and simultaneous multi-band monitoring campaigns ( Montano2022, McHardy2023MNRAS.519.3366M ), we revisit the optical variation properties of NGC 4395 and unveil at the intermediate-mass scale for the first time a prominent departure from the DRW process and a plausible timescale-dependent CV.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4395 as a CVn I galaxy observed with WFPC2, describing its stellar population or morphology and deriving a TRGB-based distance.

quotes.quote: This face-on Sd galaxy with a Seyfert 1 type nucleus also extends beyond the WFPC2 field. According to Karachentsev Drozdovsky (1998), its distance via the brightest blue stars is 4.2 0.8 Mpc. The CM diagram in Fig. 2 reveals about 21800 stars seen both in the V and I bands. The majority of the detected stars are likely RGB stars. >From the TRGB position we derive a distance of 4.61 0.57 Mpc, which agrees well with the previous distance estimate.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC4395 as the lowest-mass known AGN, whose break frequency may imply an overestimated black hole mass or different variability physics.

quotes.quote: However, the lowest mass AGN known, NGC4395, is not so easy to fit into this pattern. From the reverberation mass, it should have a high-frequency break only a factor 100 higher than that from the other objects. Yet its break is 4- 5 orders of magnitude higher than that for NGC 4258, the most obvious object to scale from in terms of similarly low L/L_ Edd , and 3 orders of magnitude from the majority of AGN in the figure. Either there is truly a difference in the variability properties of AGN and GBH, or the black hole mass in NGC 4395 is overestimated. Some evidence for the latter possibility is that this is the only object which has significantly different masses inferred from reverberation and stellar velocity dispersion estimates (refs).

sources.summary: The paper uses high-quality Keck spectroscopy of NGC 4395, the least luminous known Seyfert 1 nucleus, to constrain its compact broad-line region and rule out bloated-star cloud models.

quotes.quote: Here we report on high-quality Keck spectroscopy of the \ line in NGC 4395, which is a factor of 1000 fainter than NGC 4151, having L_ (5100 )=5.9 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 (Peterson et al. 2005). This is the least luminous known Seyfert 1 nucleus (Filippenko Sargent 1989; Filippenko, Ho, Sargent 1993), and despite its extreme low luminosity, it appears to have a rather normal broad emission-line spectrum (apparently with a lower than solar metal abundance, Kraemer et al. 1999). The low luminosity of NGC 4395 implies an expected R_ BLR of just (0.5-1.7) 10^ 14 cm, based on the most recent expression for the mean Balmer-line time lag versus L_ (5100 ) in Kaspi et al. (2005; see their Table 3). Bloated stars are predicted to have a size of 10^ 14 cm (Alexander Netzer 1994), and thus at most a handful of such stars can be accommodated in the BLR of NGC 4395. This will inevitably produce large-amplitude features in the line profile.

sources.summary: The paper uses dwarf galaxy NGC 4395 as an intermediate-mass comparison, deriving high-frequency X-ray variability consistent with its black-hole mass estimates.

quotes.quote: An interesting source for comparison turns out to be a dwarf galaxy NGC 4395, with black hole mass estimates between 0.13 and 3.6 10^5 M _ (Kraemer et al. 1999; Ho 2002; Filippenko Ho 2003; Vaughan et al. 2005; Greene Ho 2006). The luminosity is low, L/L_ Edd 0.1 (Vaughan et al. 2005). We analyzed its ASCA observations of 2000-05-24 and 2000-05-26 and found the excess variance for this source and C_M = 3.2_ 1.1 ^ +1.4 10^ 5 Hz ^ 1 . We show the range of masses and C_M for NGC 4395 in Fig. fig:mcm ( b ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4395 as a faint X-ray AGN, adopting an ASCA-derived mean spectral slope because the observation sampled most of its flux and spectral variations.

quotes.quote: NGC 4395 is a rather faint X--ray source, with an average 2-10 keV flux of 5 10^ 12 ergs cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 (Shih, Iwasawa Fabian 2003), which has not been observed by . Shih \ have estimated a mean spectral slope, _ obs , of 1.46 0.02 from the study of a 7 day long ASCA observation. Since T_ br 500 s for this source (Vaughan \ 2005), we can be reasonably confident that the ASCA observation sampled most of the flux/spectral variations that the source displays. For this reason we adopted this value as the best estimate for its average spectral shape.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4395 as a difficult luminosity-profile fitting case, describing it as a low surface brightness dwarf Seyfert galaxy that neither Sersic nor Spergel profiles fit well.

quotes.quote: For the sake of fairness we also show NGC 4395 , which resists accurate fitting by both functions. This is probably because NGC 4395 is a dwarf galaxy of low surface bightness. In fact, it is the dimmest Seyfert galaxy yet found ( ).
The two outliers correspond to the same galaxy ( NGC 4395 ) which neither profile fits especially well.

sources.summary: NGC 4395 variability is dominated by absorption; its first components match partial-covering simulations, while reflection signatures are overwhelmed in PCA.

quotes.quote: NGC 4395 The variability in NGC 4395 is dominated by absorption Nardini11 . The first two components produced here are an almost perfect match to the simulations of a partially covered power law, where the covering fraction and intrinsic source flux are both allowed to vary (see simsection ). We suggest that the weak third component is caused by changes in the column density, which requires a correction factor be added to the absorption term (see component 2 in Fig. varyingabs1 . We note that this source does show evidence of reflection features in the spectrum and a reverberation lag DeMarco13 , but any reflection features in the PCA components are completely swamped by the absorption variability.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 4395 with the baseline model plus a narrow Fe XXVI absorption line and allows variable reflected emission to avoid an unphysical reflection strength.

quotes.quote: The spectra of NGC 4395 are well reproduced with the base-line model plus a narrow absorption line at 6.90^ +0.08 _ 0.07 keV ( ^2/d.o.f = 332.7/354 ). The absorption line improves the fit by ^2 = 7.5 , and is consistent with Fe XXVI K . We obtain R_ torus = 1.67^ +1.73 _ 0.92 by assuming that the fluxes of the reflected emission varied between the Suzaku and Swift /BAT spectra (see above). We note that without this assumption we would derive an unphysically large reflection strength, R_ torus 3.5 , which is much larger than typical values of R_ torus 1 observed in local AGNs Dad08 .

sources.summary: The paper excludes NGC 4395 from one fit, then discusses it as a low-halo-mass and scaling-relation case whose central velocity dispersion may be abnormal.

quotes.quote: Here, we produce a v_ max - _0 relation for the 40 spiral galaxies with both v_ max and _0 measurements (not including NGC 4395). Knowing _0 , this relation can be used to estimate v_ max , and indirectly predict M_ DM (through Equation ( eqn:conversion )); this is useful for galaxies with velocity dispersion measurements that lack reliable rotational velocity measurements (e.g., nearly face-on disk galaxies). The bces bisector regression BCES,Nemmen:2012 https://github.com/rsnemmen/BCES yields rCl v_ max km s^ 1 & = & (0.65 0.10) _0 141 km s^ 1 && + (2.34 0.01), with _ rms = 0.08 dex and intrinsic scatter Calculated through Equation (1) from Graham:Driver:2007 . =0.07 dex, both in the v_ max direction (see Figure fig:v-sigma ). Except for NGC 4395, it is apparent from inspection of Figure fig:v-sigma that five galaxies (Circinus, NGC 613, NGC 1300, NGC 4151, and NGC 5055) are mild outliers, noticeably outside the 1 ( 1 _ rms ) scatter band.

As can be seen in Figure fig:M_BH-log_v , three of our galaxies (Circinus, NGC 1566, and NGC 4395, two of which were outliers in Figure fig:v-sigma ) have (M_ DM /M_ ) < 11.70 (i.e., v_ max < 159 km s ^ 1 from Equation ( eqn:conversion )). Our results show that 5 10^ 11 M_ is not a hard-and-fast threshold; however, given that our uncertainties on M_ DM are greater than 0.1 dex (see Table table:Sample , Column (9)), our smallest dark matter halos are only marginally below this limit. For example, based on v_ max =98 7 km s^ 1 , we predict that NGC 1566 has a quite low (M_ DM /M_ )=11.67 0.15 . However, because NGC 1566 is a Type I Seyfert galaxy Alloin:1985,Oknyansky:2019 , it is hard to imagine it not possessing an SMBH. If we track the location of the six outliers from Figure fig:v-sigma to Figure fig:M_BH-log_v , we find that the locations of NGC 613, NGC 1300, and NGC 4151 suggest that their maximum rotational velocities are anomalous, rather than their central stellar velocity dispersions or black hole masses. Circinus, NGC 4395, and NGC 5055 are not outliers in Figure fig:M_BH-log_v , indicating that they might have abnormal central stellar velocity dispersions, rather than deviant maximum rotational velocities or black hole masses.

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC07524 as a difficult photometry case with a bar-like feature and faint irregular light, comparing PHOTUTILS, AUTOPROF, and XVISTA recovery.

quotes.quote: The second column shows UGC07524 , which has a bar-like feature and a faint, irregular, light distribution. In this case PHOTUTILS is unable to model the light distribution beyond the bar feature and prematurely ends the light profile. AUTOPROF fails to capture the bar feature as well, but adequately finds the global light distribution. This galaxy also presented a challenge for XVISTA , which could only recover the bar feature, though this solution was extended to the optical edge of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 4395 as a nearby, low-luminosity Type 1 Seyfert/pure-disk system hosting a small central black hole whose mass matches expectations if its nuclear cluster substitutes for a bulge.

quotes.quote: A small, 10^ 4-5 M_ black hole is found embedded in the central star cluster of NGC 4395, one of the least luminous and nearest known Type 1 Seyfert galaxies (Filippenko Ho 2003).

Supermassive black holes do form in some pure disk systems, as shown by Filippenko Ho (2003) for the case of NGC 4395. However, these authors stress that in this galaxy the estimated black hole mass is consistent with the M_ relation of Tremaine et al. \ (2002), if the central cluster is considered in lieu of the bulge. For a =30 km s ^ 1 , a good upper limit for the velocity dispersion of central star cluster in NGC 4395, this relation predicts a M_ =6.6 10^4 M_ , consistent with the mass independently estimated from the AGN properties (Filippenko Ho 2003).

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 4395 as a nearby bulgeless late-type spiral with broad-line, radio-core, and X-ray AGN signatures, implying an unusually low-mass central black hole.

quotes.quote: The nearby ( 4 Mpc) galaxy NGC 4395 contains all the usual attributes of a respectable AGN: broad optical and UV emission lines (Filippenko Sargent 1989; Filippenko, Ho, Sargent 1993), a compact radio core (Ho Ulvestad 2001; Wrobel, Fassnacht, Ho 2001), and rapidly variable hard X-ray emission (Moran et al. 1999, 2004; Shih, Iwasawa, Fabian 2003). Contrary to expectations, however, NGC 4395 is a bulgeless, extremely late-type (Sdm) spiral, whose central stellar velocity dispersion does not exceed 30 \ (Filippenko Ho 2003).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4395 as the least luminous known dwarf type 1 Seyfert, notable for an AGN in an essentially bulgeless late-type galaxy with variable X-ray emission, a compact radio core, and a low-mass SMBH.

quotes.quote: galaxies: active --- galaxies: individual (NGC 4395) --- galaxies: nuclei --- galaxies: Seyfert The nucleus of NGC 4395 is a ``dwarf'' type 1 Seyfert active galactic nucleus (AGN), the least luminous known in its class FS89, FHS93 . Its presence in an essentially bulgeless, extremely late-type galaxy is surprising; such AGNs (and quiescent black holes) are usually found in bulge-dominated early-type systems HFS97 , with black hole mass and bulge mass strongly correlated Magorrian98, Laor98, MH03, HR04 . Despite this, the nucleus of NGC 4395 exhibits all of the hallmarks of a type 1 AGN: it is detected as a variable X-ray point source Lira99, Moran99, Moran05, Iwasawa00, Ho01, SIF03 , it has a high brightness temperature, highly compact radio core Moran99, HU01, WFH01 , and its spectral energy distribution from X-ray to radio wavelengths is similar to that of other type 1 AGNs Moran99 . NGC 4395 offers a chance to probe the low end of the supermassive black hole (SMBH) mass distribution.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4395 as a low-luminosity AGN with a reverberation-mapped BLR, comparing its small CIV BLR size, high accretion rate, and black-hole mass estimates.

quotes.quote: Notice the case of the low luminosity AGN NGC 4395 (because the size of BLRs has been measured by Reverberation Mapping technique by Peterson et al. (2005), thus we list the object individually) for which the BH mass estimated from the upper limit of stellar velocity dispersion is about 6.6 3.1 10^4 M_ (we think the upper limit of stellar velocity from Keck high-resolution spectra is more reliable than the upper limit of line width of [ NII ] ), which is about four to six times smaller than the BH mass 3.6 1.1 10^5 M_ derived recently under the assumption of Virialization and using reverberation mapping by Peterson et al. (2005).

For NGC 4395, the object mentioned above, the size of the BLRs is about 0.04 light-days derived directly from the variation of the CIV emission line, which is much smaller than the upper limit value derived from the upper limit BH masses using broad H : 1.28 light-days. However, the emission regions of high-ionization CIV should be nearer to the central black hole than the regions of low-ionization H .

According to the high accretion rate of m_ H of NGC4395, we can expect that the size of BLRs, particularly the emission regions of low-ionization braod H should be consistent with the value from the empirical relation of R_ BLRs L_ H .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4395 as the lowest-luminosity type 1 AGN anchoring an R-X relation, adopting its 4.3 Mpc distance and time-averaged X-ray luminosity because of rapid variability.

quotes.quote: The values of L_ R from Ho Ulvestad (2001) were corrected for the revised distances in Panessa et al. (2006), excluding NGC 4395, where the more recent Thim et al. (2004) value of 4.3 Mpc is used.

Since NGC 4395 shows very large and rapid X-ray variability, we have used the time-averaged absorption-corrected L _ 2-10 keV =8.8 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 , measured by Moran et al. (2005, corrected for a distance of 4.3 Mpc).

The PH AGN sample together with the PG sample, suggest a tight linear R-X relation from the lowest luminosity type 1 AGN, NGC 4395 at log L _ X 40 (left most AGN in Fig.2b), up to luminous quasars at log L _ X 45 , with a slope of unity (consistent with eq.1).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4395 as real LSB velocity-field data where a cuspy NFW halo fits better, with slit velocities resembling the triaxial mock cases.

quotes.quote: Kuzio06 and Kuzio08 find the cuspy NFW halo to provide a better fit to the rotation curve of NGC 4395 than the cored isothermal halo; they also note a misalignment of the minor axis. In Figure LSBslits , the change in velocity along the slit in NGC 4395 displays the same shape as those along the triaxial slits in Figure slits . There is an asymmetric (around = 0) overall change in velocity of 18 km s ^ 1 along the slit.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4395's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4395 is a close-by bulge-less dwarf spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 4.3 1.1 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with a Sy 1.8 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 that belongs to nine-month BAT AGN sample. This AGN is the least-luminous type I AGN known filippenko_discovery_1989, filippenko_hst_1993 and possesses one of the lowest central black hole masses in an AGN, too filippenko_low-mass_2003,peterson_multiwavelength_2005 . It is highly variable at optical, UV and X-ray wavelengths (e.g., cameron_correlated_2012 ) and features a compact radio nucleus with a subparsec-scale outflow moran_nuclear_1999,ho_radio_2001,wrobel_inner_2001,wrobel_radio_2006 .

sources.summary: The paper emphasizes NGC 4395 as a nearby, faint Seyfert nucleus in a Magellanic spiral host, with a very small supermassive black hole.

quotes.quote: NGC 4395 (S1.8) This galaxy (Fig. f4 ) holds several records: it is the nearest and faintest Seyfert 1 nucleus Filippenko89 , and it is one of the intrinsically weakest nuclear X-ray sources observed to date Lira99,Moran99 . NGC 4395 is also highly unusual as an AGN host because of its late Hubble type: it is classified as a Magellanic spiral Sm; Ho01 .

sources.summary: The review identifies NGC 4395 as an early dwarf-galaxy IMBH case, with AGN spectral evidence, hard X-rays, a compact radio jet, and a dynamical black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: The first observational evidence for IMBHs in dwarf galaxies were identified in the late 80s in the spiral galaxy NGC 4395 (M _ * 1.3 10^ 9 M _ ; Fig. mosaic ) and the elliptical galaxy Pox 52 (M _ * 1.2 10^ 9 M _ ; Fig. mosaic ) by the finding of high-ionization narrow emission lines and broad Balmer emission lines in their optical spectrum ( 1987AJ.....93...29K ; 1989ApJ...342L..11F ). These, together with the detection of hard X-ray emission, indicate the presence of an AGN with a BH mass of M_ BH 3 10^ 5 M _ estimated from the width of the broad emission lines under the assumption that the gas is virialized ( 2003ApJ...588L..13F ; 2004ApJ...607...90B ; 2005AJ....129.2108M ; 2005ApJ...632..799P ; 2005MNRAS.356..524V ; 2008ApJ...686..892T ). NGC 4395 has, in addition, a compact radio jet ( 2006ApJ...646L..95W ) and is one of the few dwarf galaxies for which a dynamical BH mass measurement has been possible ( M_ BH = 4^ +8 _ 3 10^ 5 M _ ; 2015ApJ...809..101D ).

sources.summary: The appendix finds good UFO evidence in NGC 4395 despite Tombesi10, attributing aligned blue-shifted line peaks to absorption changes rather than ionization response.

quotes.quote: NGC 4395 is an example where we find good evidence for a UFO while Tombesi10 does not. This is because the strength of the Fe xxv line is comparatively weaker than the distinct peaks from 3 keV to 5 keV, which align very well with the overall blue-shifted emission lines. The variability here is more likely to be caused by changes in absorption rather than a response to changes in ionization.

sources.summary: The paper singles out NGC 4395 as the only low-luminosity barred galaxy in the sample, with low central N/O and a shallow metallicity gradient across strong-line methods.

quotes.quote: The central (N/O) abundances, as traced by the y-intercepts of the radial fits, b_ N/O , range from 1.3  dex for NGC 1313 and NGC 4395 to 0.25  dex, considering the complete sample of galaxies for which the N/O radial profile could be derived from HCM and the R calibration.

We are aware that the number of low luminosity barred galaxies in our sample is low. In fact there is only one barred galaxy with M _B 18.5 , NGC 4395. We analysed the gradient of this galaxy carefully in paperI , and showed that the metallicity gradient is shallow with all strong-line methods, yielding an average value of _ O/H =-0.005 0.007  dex kpc ^ 1 , that is considerably distant from the range of values covered by unbarred galaxies of similar M _B , that goes from  -0.06 to 0.10 dex kpc ^ 1 (ignoring methods based on the O3N2 indicator, in which the range of observed slopes is much smaller).

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 4395 as a bulgeless dwarf galaxy with a very small SMBH, confirmed by intranight reverberation mapping and short BLR delays.

quotes.quote: This was used in case of NGC 4395, which is a bulgeless dwarf galaxy with one of the smallest SMBH of only 10,000 solar masses (Woo et al. 2019). This SMBH mass was confirmed with the international intranight RM campaign done by more than a dozen of telescope across the world including the 1.4m Milankovic telescope (Cho et al. 2020). The observed time delays of the BLR were only 1-2 hours, and it was important to show that this case of very low-luminosity AGN follows the radius-luminosity scaling relations (Bentz et al. 2013).

sources.summary: The paper reviews NGC 4395 as an IMBH-candidate host with a radio-quiet nucleus, a sub-pc elongated outflow-like structure, an EVN nondetection, and VLA/HSA components interpreted as ejection activity.

quotes.quote: Observations of NGC 4395 with the High Sensitivity Array (HSA) at 1.4 GHz reveal a radio quiet nucleus and an elongated sub-pc scale structure, indicative of an outflow 2006ApJ...646L..95W . The recent study of NGC 4395 by 2022MNRAS.514.6215Y finds an undetected source from European VLBI Network (EVN) 5 GHz high-resolution observations. Very large array (VLA) 15 GHz observations detect a core (coincident with the Gaia optical position) and eastern (E) components; a new HSA imaging confirms the E component that is interpreted as a propagating shock originating from episodic ejection or outflow activity.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4395 as a loose Sc/Irr galaxy containing the faintest nearby Seyfert 1 nucleus, with a compact candidate radio counterpart in its outskirts.

quotes.quote: The galaxy NGC 4395 has a loose and disconnected appearance containing the faintest and nearest Seyfert 1 nucleus currently known and classified as Sc/Irr type. The proposed counterpart of X2 in NGC 4395 is a very compact and faint radio source in the peripheral outskirts of the galaxy (electronic Fig. ngc4395 ). With the present data it is difficult to assess whether the object is associated to NGC 4395 itself.

gmw_00000212.name: NGC 4418
gmw_00000212.overview: NGC 4418 is a nearly edge-on Sa-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:0705.2947, arXiv:1310.2770] classified as a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1403.3782] with a deeply dust-enshrouded, Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2004.01273]. This compact obscured nucleus drives decelerating bilateral outflows [arXiv:2402.17590] and is undergoing a minor interaction with the neighboring galaxy VV 655 [arXiv:2004.09534].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4418 as a deeply obscured luminous galaxy with rich dense-gas chemistry, unusually bright HC3N emission, and line ratios consistent with a young starburst plus PDR component.

quotes.quote: NGC 4418 is a nearly edge-on Sa-type galaxy with deep mid-infrared silicate absorption features suggesting that the inner region is enshrouded by large masses of warm (85 K) dust (e.g. Spoon \ 2001, Evans \ 2003). Due to the high obscuration, it is difficult to determine the nature of the activity that is driving the luminosity. The infrared luminosity to molecular gas mass ratio - L_ IR / M(H _2 ) = 100 is high for a non-ULIRG galaxy (Sanders, Scoville, Soifer 1991).

We find a rich molecular chemistry in NGC 4418 and luminous HC _3 N line emission which in our Galaxy is associated with warm, dense starforming cores. In this context, the elevated HCN/HCO ^+ 1--0 line ratio found by Imanishi \ (2004) may instead indicate young starformation. As discussed in section 3.2, we therefore advice caution in using the HCN/HCO ^+ 1--0 line ratio as a diagnostic tool for XDR activity since its interpretation is ambiguous. It is clear that further XDR modelling is required, including a continued dicussion of the HCN/HCO ^+ line emission ratio, as well as modelling of HC _3 N abundances in XDRs. For NGC 4418, we tentatively suggest that the line emission can be fitted to a combination of a warm, dense gas component not strongly affected by UV or cosmic rays (hot-core like chemistry) supplying abundant HC _3 N and HCN --- with an additional PDR component (providing CN, HCO ^+ and HNC line emission).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4418 as a dust-enshrouded luminous infrared galaxy with unusually strong HC3N emission, high central HC3N abundance, warm dense gas, and a nucleus resembling a hot core.

quotes.quote: The edge-on, Sa-type galaxy NGC 4418 has one of the highest luminosities in HC _3 N (relative to HCN) found for an external galaxy so far nascent,monje08 . Its unusual HC _3 N emission was first reported by nascent . The inner region of NGC 4418 is deeply dust-enshrouded spoon_01 with mid-IR intensities that are indicative of dust temperatures of 85 K evans03 inside a radius of 50 pc. The IR luminosity-to-molecular gas mass ratio is high for a non-ULIRG galaxy indicating that intense, compact activity is hidden behind the dust.

In the inner 300 pc of NGC 4418, we estimate a high HC _3 N abundance, of the order of 10 ^ 7 . The excitation of the HC _3 N molecule responds strongly to the intense radiation field and the presence of warm, dense gas and dust at the center of NGC 4418. The intense HC _3 N line emission is a result of both high abundances and excitation. The properties of the HC _3 N emitting gas are similar to those found for hot cores in Sgr B2, which implies that the nucleus ( < 300 pc) of NGC 4418 is reminiscent of a hot core.

sources.summary: The paper resolves NGC 4418's compact obscured nucleus into eight radio features, arguing their morphology and inverted spectrum disfavor an AGN-only power source and may indicate extreme clustered star formation.

quotes.quote: The galaxy NGC 4418 (IRAS12243-0036) is a luminous ( L_ FIR >10^ 11 L_ ) infrared galaxy (LIRG) with an infrared flux density more than 5 times larger than expected from the linear radio to far-infrared relation. find only ten such objects in a sample of 1809 galaxies, which makes NGC 4418 a very unusual object. The galaxy has one of the deepest mid-IR silicate absorption features ever detected towards an external galaxy indicating a very deeply obscured nucleus. The deep absorption and high IR brightness of NGC 4418 was first noted by who proposed that this galaxy hides either an active galactic nucleus (AGN) or a compact nuclear starburst.

The nuclear structure of NGC 4418 visible with EVN and MERLIN consists of eight compact (<49 mas i.e. <8 pc) features spread within a region of 250 mas, i.e. 41 pc. We derive an inverted spectral index 0.7 ( S_ ^ ) for the compact radio emission. Brightness temperatures >10^ 4.8 K indicate that these compact features cannot be HII-regions. The complex morphology and inverted spectrum of the eight detected compact features is evidence against the hypothesis that an AGN alone is powering the nucleus of NGC 4418.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4418 as a nearby luminous infrared galaxy with a compact obscured nucleus, a kpc-scale dusty superwind, and a distinct 10 Myr circumnuclear starburst powerful enough to drive the outflow.

quotes.quote: We report our optical spectroscopic study of the nucleus and its surrounding region of a nearby luminous infrared galaxy NGC 4418. This galaxy has been known to host a compact obscured nucleus, showing distinct characteristics such as a very compact ( 20 pc) sub-mm and mid-infrared core and dusty circumnuclear region with massive molecular gas concentration. We detected dusty superwind outflow at 1 kpc scale along the disk semiminor axis in both shock-heated emission lines and enhanced interstellar Na D absorption. This superwind shows basic characteristics similar to those of the prototypical superwind in the starburst galaxy M82, such as a kpc-scale extended structure of gas and dust along the disk minor axis, outflowing components (multiphase gas and dust), physical conditions of the ionized gas, and monotonically blueshifting radial velocity field with increasing distance from the nucleus on the front side of the superwind.

We also detected a moderately extinct starburst population in the SDSS nuclear spectrum with the burst age of 10 Myr and stellar mass of 1 10^7 \ M_ . It is powerful enough to drive the superwind within the dynamical age of the superwind ( 10 Myr). On the basis of comparison between this starburst--superwind scenario and the observations in terms of the burst age, stellar mass, infrared luminosity, and obscuration in the optical bands, we argue that this superwind-driving starburst is separate from the sub-mm core even if the core is a very young star cluster. Therefore, this galaxy hosts both the enshrouded compact core and the superwind-driving circumnuclear starburst.

sources.summary: The paper presents MUSE observations of the CON galaxy NGC4418, finding optical structures, multiple outflows, AGN-ionized knots, and evidence that its compact obscured nucleus is AGN-powered.

quotes.quote: Here, we present results from MUSE for the confirmed local CON galaxy NGC4418. For the first time we spatially map the spectral features and kinematics of the galaxy in the optical, revealing several previously unknown structures. In particular, we discover a bilateral outflow along the minor axis, an outflowing bubble, several knot structures and a receding outflow partially obscured by the galactic disk. Based on the properties of these features, we conclude that the CON in NGC4418 is most likely powered by an AGN.

The detection of multiple outflow structures in NGC4418 is consistent with the galaxy existing in an early phase of a feedback cycle in which dust is being expelled from the nuclear region via outflows. Based on the velocity and structure of the observed outflows, we suggest that it is unlikely that the CON in NGC4418 is powered solely by starburst activity. Furthermore, the knots detected in the [ O III ] emission are consistent with having been ionized by an AGN. We therefore conclude that the CON in NGC4418 is likely undergoing an AGN phase.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 4418 with MUSE, linking its compact obscured nucleus to decelerating bilateral outflows, a southern ionized tail, a northern bubble, [O III] knots, and likely AGN power.

quotes.quote: This work analyses the galaxy-scale optical properties of the local CON NGC 4418 ( z =0.00727). The key aims of the study are to understand the impact of nuclear outflows on the host galaxy and infer the power source of its CON. Through the mapping of the galaxy spectra and kinematics, we seek to identify new structures in NGC 4418 to ultimately reveal more about the CON's history, its impact on the host, and, more generally, the role CONs play in galaxy evolution.

We confirm the presence of a previously postulated, blueshifted outflow along the minor axis of NGC 4418. We find this outflow to be decelerating and, for the first time, show it to extend in both directions from the nucleus. We report the discovery of two further outflow structures: a redshifted southern outflow connected to a tail of ionised gas surrounding the galaxy and a blueshifted bubble to the north. In addition to these features, we find the [ O iii ] emission reveals the presence of knots across the galaxy, which are consistent with regions of the galaxy that have been photoionised by an AGN.
Based on the properties of these features, we conclude that the CON in NGC 4418 is most likely powered by AGN activity.

sources.summary: The paper detects mid-infrared absorption features in NGC 4418, attributes them to ices and carbonaceous grains, and argues that a deeply enshrouded AGN likely powers the nucleus.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of absorption features in the 6--8 m region superimposed on a featureless mid-infrared continuum in NGC 4418. For several of these features this is the first detection in an external galaxy.
