We compare the absorption spectrum of NGC 4418 to that of embedded massive protostars and the Galactic centre, and attribute the absorption features to ice grains and to hydrogenated amorphous carbon grains. From the depth of the ice features, the powerful central source responsible for the mid-infrared emission must be deeply enshrouded. Since this emission is warm and originates in a compact region, an AGN must be hiding in the nucleus of NGC 4418.

The spectrum of NGC 4418 shows absorption features at 6.0, 6.8, 7.3, 7.6, 10 and 18 m (Table optdepths ). The 6.0 m feature in NGC 4418 is similar to that in the Galactic sources but with a perhaps more pronounced long wavelength wing. The 6.85 m feature is considerably narrower than the molecular cloud feature but is similar in width to the diffuse ISM feature. The band at 7.3 m is substantially broader than the molecular cloud and diffuse ISM features. The absorption band near 7.6 m is similar to that observed locally. The presence of ice along the line of sight toward NGC 4418 is suggested by the identification of the 7.6 m band with CH _ 4 (Boogert et al. , ) and by the presence of the 6.0 m band due to H _ 2 O ice (Keane et al. ; Chiar et al. ).

sources.summary: The paper argues NGC 4418 hosts a deeply buried AGN: near-infrared and millimeter diagnostics show weak surface star formation plus compact, X-ray-heated, high-density nuclear gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 4418, at z = 0.007, is one of the closest and strongest buried AGN candidates. It has an infrared luminosity of L _ IR 9 10 ^ 10 L _ (= 10 ^ 44.5 ergs s ^ 1 ; see Table 1), and shows no clear Seyfert signatures in its optical spectrum arm89,leh95 . The 5--23 m mid-infrared spectrum of NGC 4418 has the typical shape of an obscured AGN roc91,spo01 . Based on an 8--23 m mid-infrared spectrum, dud97 argued that the energy source of NGC 4418 is very compact and more centrally-concentrated than surrounding dust, as is expected for a buried AGN.

We investigated the nuclear energy source of the infrared luminous galaxy NGC 4418 through near-infrared K and L band spectroscopy, and millimeter observations at 3.4 mm using both a single dish telescope and an interferometric array. Various previous observations have suggested that this galaxy has a deeply buried AGN at the center. In the near-infrared spectra, we detected clear PAH emission and CO absorption features, suggesting that the observed near-infrared flux comes mostly from star-forming activity. The magnitude of this detected star-forming activity was measured from the observed PAH emission luminosity and was found to account for only 1/50 of the infrared luminosity of this galaxy.

sources.summary: NGC 4418 shows highly excited molecular absorption, compact warm nuclear continuum, redshifted outer-region lines suggesting inflow, and chemically dense nuclear gas affected by X-rays or cosmic rays.

quotes.quote: NGC 4418 and Arp 220, observed as part of the SHINING key programme, reveals high excitation in H _2 O, OH, HCN, and NH _3 . In NGC 4418, absorption lines were detected with E_ lower > 800 K (H _2 O), 600 K (OH), 1075 K (HCN), and 600 K (NH _3 ), while in Arp 220 the excitation is somewhat lower. While outflow signatures in moderate excitation lines are seen in Arp 220 as have been seen in previous studies, in NGC 4418 the lines tracing its outer regions are redshifted relative to the nucleus, suggesting an inflow with M 12 \ yr ^ 1 . Both galaxies have compact and warm ( T_ dust 100 K) nuclear continuum components, together with a more extended and colder component that is much more prominent and massive in Arp 220.

A chemical dichotomy is found in both sources: on the one hand, the nuclear regions have high H _2 O abundances, 10^ 5 , and high HCN/H _2 O and HCN/NH _3 column density ratios of 0.1 0.4 and 2 5, respectively, indicating a chemistry typical of evolved hot cores where grain mantle evaporation has occurred. On the other hand, the high OH abundance, with OH/H _2 O ratios of 0.5, indicates the effects of X-rays and/or cosmic rays. The nuclear media have high surface brightnesses ( 10^ 13 /kpc ^2 ) and are estimated to be very thick ( N_ H 10^ 25 cm ^ 2 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4418 as a sparsely constrained Seyfert 2 candidate, probably Compton-thick, with MIR-X-ray predictions far above observed X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: NGC 4418 Little information is available about the X-ray properties of the Sy 2 candidate NGC 4418. Apparently it remained undetected during observations in 1994. published data from 2003 in which the nucleus is faintly detected, insufficient for a detailed spectral analysis. They conclude that NGC 4418 is probably Compton-thick and infer an intrinsic 2-10 keV luminosity of 10^ 41.2 erg/s based on the assumption of one per cent reflection sensitivity for a non-stated object distance. In addition, data were taken in 2006 but remain unpublished so far.

The observed MIR--X-ray ratio supports the CT scenario predicting an X-ray column density of = 25.85 0.44 . The intrinsic 2-10 keV luminosity estimated from the MIR--X-ray correlation would be = 43.49 0.37 so four orders of magnitude higher than what is observed. However, owing to the large uncertainty in the X-ray properties, the MIR--X-ray correlation can not be used to verify the AGN nature of NGC 4418.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4418 as a lenticular LIRG with unusual nuclear gas and dust, arguing its interaction with VV 655 likely shaped its nucleus and tidal arm.

quotes.quote: NGC 4418 stands out among nearby LIRGs due to its dense central concentration of molecular gas and the dusty, bi-polar structures along its minor axis suggestive of a wind driven by a central starburst and possible nuclear activity. We seek to understand the consequences of the ongoing minor interaction between VV 655 and NGC 4418 for the evolution of the LIRG. Specifically, we consider the origin of the gas supply responsible for the unusual nuclear properties of NGC 4418. We investigate the structural, kinematic, and chemical properties of VV 655 and NGC 4418 by analyzing archival imaging data and optical spectroscopic observations from the SDSS-III and new spectra from SALT-RSS.

The gas-phase metallicity in NGC 4418 significantly exceeds that in VV 655. No kinematic disturbances in the ionized gas are observed along the minor axis of NGC 4418, but we see evidence for ionized gas outflows from VV 655 that may increase the cross-section for gas stripping in grazing collisions. A faint, asymmetric outer arm is detected in NGC 4418 of the type normally associated with galaxy-galaxy interactions.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4418 as an SED-identified AGN candidate undetected by NuSTAR, with obscured-AGN indicators, estimated N_H=26.2, and possible heavy obscuration or starburst emission.

quotes.quote: SED-identified three AGN candidates Yamada2023 identified, on the basis of SED decomposition, three AGN candidates (ESO 350 38, ESO 374 032, and NGC 4418), which had been observed in X-rays, specifically with NuSTAR in the 8--24 keV band, but not been detected presumably due to insufficient exposures ( 50 ks) (see Section sub2-1-GOALS ). Their WISE color criteria Stern2012 and equivalent widths of the 6.2 m polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon features suggest that they are obscured AGNs e.g., Yamada2021 . Using Equation 7 in Asmus2015 in combination with their 12- m AGN fluxes and observed 2--10 keV fluxes, we estimate their N_ H to be 25.3 1.8 (ESO 350 38), 25.6 1.9 (ESO 374 032), and 26.2 1.9 (NGC 4418).

The 3 upper limits of intrinsic 2--10 keV luminosities are then estimated to be L_ 2-10 ^ (lim) = 42.42 , 42.69, and 41.49, respectively, from the 3 upper limit of their 8--24 keV luminosities under the assumption of N_ H = 25 Yamada2021 . Comparison between their X-ray and bolometric AGN luminosities yields 2--10 keV bolometric correction factors of 20--160. The values are roughly consistent with the typical one among local AGNs with a wide range of _ Edd = - 3--0 Vasudevan2007 . Thus, these three sources are expected to be heavily obscured AGNs with N_ H 25 , or pure starbursts.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4418 as a warm FIR-color case where the model fits SCUBA measurements but fails to reproduce the IRAS fluxes, preventing firm physical conclusions.

quotes.quote: However, we can not fit well the observed FIR spectrum of six galaxies in our sample, namely, MCG+02-04-025, NGC 1614, NGC 4418, IC 860, NGC 7714 and MRK 331. The ratio L_ 60 /L_ 100 in these galaxies is higher than 0.8. This is the maximum flux ratio value that can be explained by the fixed template we use for the dust emission from HII regions. This is the main reason for the failure of the model to provide a good fit to the FIR spectra of these galaxies. In the bottom panel of Fig. fig:goodbad , we show a plot of the observed FIR spectrum and the best fitting model SED in the case of NGC 4418.

Although the model fits well the SCUBA measurements, it fails to fit well the IRAS fluxes. Comparison of the observed FIR SEDs between the two galaxies shown in Fig. fig:goodbad reveals clearly the difference in the IRAS colors of NGC 958 and NGC 4418, with the second being significantly ``warmer".

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4418 as [CII]-deficient, compact in infrared/radio emission, and a likely hidden Seyfert with a highly extinguished nucleus.

quotes.quote: In the extreme cases of NGC 4418, IC 0860 and CGCG 1510.8+0725, [ CII ] emission is undetected at the 3 level of 2cii =5-0.5 10^ 4 . Emission in the other prominent PDR cooling line [ OI ] at 63 is undetected at similar levels, as are [ OIII ] ( 88 ) and [ NII ] ( 122 ). In Figure 1 we show 3 upper limits on the flux in the [ CII ] 158 transition. The absence of [ CII ] emission in these galaxies is very striking because the upper limits on the in these galaxies are up to 40 times smaller than the narrow range in measured for two-thirds of the sample. Yet these galaxies have normal atomic and molecular gas components.

The mid-infrared images at 7 and 15 are unresolved at 7 resolution of ISOCAM (Silbermann et al. 1997) and they are compact in radio continuum at 1.4 GHz (Condon et al 1990): NGC 4418 measures 0.5'' 0.3''(32 ) , CGCG 1510.8+0725 measures 0.5'' 0.5'' (85 ) and IC 860 0.4'' 0.3 '' (75 ) . NGC 4418 shows many indications of being a hidden Seyfert nucleus (Roche et al. 1986, Kawara, Nishida Philips 1989), and has the distinction of having the most 'extinguished' nucleus with A_V > 50 (Roche et al. 1986) as seen in the 9.7 absorption feature.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4418 as a dust-enshrouded FIR-excess galaxy whose chemistry suggests young starburst or buried AGN activity, with high abundances challenging an XDR explanation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4418 is a luminous, edge-on, Sa-type galaxy with a deeply dust-enshrouded nucleus spoon01 . NGC 4418 is a FIR-excess galaxy with a logarithmic IR-to-radio continuum ratio ( q ) of 3 roussel03 . This excess may be caused by either a young pre-supernova starburst or a buried AGN aalto07b, roussel03, imanishi04 . Unusually luminous HC _3 N line emission aalto02,aalto07b,costagliola10 has been interpreted as a signature of young starburst activity.

For NGC 4418 however, large 3n \ abundances are inconsistent with the XDR interpretation. An alternative possibility is that H _3 O ^+ forms through \ evaporating off dust grains and reacting with \ in warm, dense gas.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4418 as the first external galaxy with vibrationally excited HCN J=4-3, a nearby luminous infrared galaxy with a buried AGN and infrared-pumping evidence.

quotes.quote: This vibrationally excited HCN v _ 2 =1 J=4--3 (l=1f) emission line was recently detected in the external galaxy NGC 4418 sak10 . NGC 4418 is a very nearby luminous infrared galaxy (L _ IR 10 ^ 11 L _ ) at z=0.007, and a luminous buried AGN-type energy source with high emission surface brightness was found spo01,eva03,ima04,sak13 . However, based on only one example, determining whether the vibrational excitation of HCN in NGC 4418 was an exceptional case or not was impossible.

The HCN J=4--3 v _ 2 =1 (l=1f) to v=0 flux ratio is 0.04, which is a factor of 5 smaller than the ratio observed in NGC 4418 sak10 . In NGC 4418, the infrared 14 m HCN absorption feature was detected lah07 . sak10 estimated that the energy of the HCN v _ 2 =1 J=4--3 (l=1f) emission line was smaller than that of the 14 m absorption line and concluded that the observational results could be explained by the infrared radiative pumping mechanism.

sources.summary: The paper uses H I absorption toward the NGC 4418 nucleus to estimate foreground line opacity and show how diffuse gas can suppress far-infrared line emission.

quotes.quote: To illustrate the effect of foreground absorption, we have used the recent H I absorption observations towards the NGC 4418 galaxy nucleus costagliola to evaluate the [ ] line opacity associated with the foreground absorption. The HI spectrum presents two components with total column densities of 5.8 10^ 21 \ and 1.5 10^ 22 . Assuming that the gas producing the HI absorption is diffuse, it is expected to produce an absorption signal in [ ] as well.

We can estimate the magnitude of this absorption from the HI column densities using a carbon to hydrogen abundance ratio of x_C = 1.4 10 ^ 4 . These numbers lead to expected integrated line opacities of 5.8 and 15 for the [ ] line associated with each velocity component. To further estimate the impact of this absorption, we use the measured line widths of 130 and 160 \ to derive the relative emission I_ rel ( C II ) as defined in Eq. eq:1 , and obtain -0.04 and -0.11.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4418 as an extremely compact, warm far-infrared source whose HCN brightness requires high infrared surface density and saturated vibrational emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 4418 : an extremely compact and warm source of far-IR emission was identified from the strong and very excited molecular lines of H _2 O, OH, and other species including HCN gon12 . The radius of this source was estimated to be 10 pc at an adopted D=29 Mpc, i.e. =1.6 10^ 2 arc sec ^2 . We have used these two values of and a flux of the HCN line of 16 Jy km s ^ 1 sak10 to give F_ HCN 2 3-2 / =760-1000 Jy km s ^ 1 arc sec ^ 2 . Only the maximum value of _ IR =2.2 10^8 L _ pc ^ 2 can account for this brightness (Fig. hcngreenhousecolH2 ), yielding a source luminosity of D^2 _ IR =(0.9-1.3) 10^ 11 . This estimate agrees with the conclusion that the bulk of the galaxy luminosity emerges from such a compact region gon12,sak13 .
The HCN _2=1^f J=4-3 to J=3-2 flux ratio of 1.9 0.3 sak10 is also consistent with strongly saturated HCN vibrational emission.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4418 as a heavily obscured LIRG/Compton-thick AGN example, citing infrared, submillimeter, and Chandra evidence plus red mid-infrared colour.

quotes.quote: NGC 4418 is a LIRG with a known dust embedded nuclear CT source. The nature of this source has been subject to many observations at different wavelengths and the presence of a CT AGN is highly favoured. Infrared observations Roche1986,Spoon2001,Roche2015 of NGC 4418 show a deep silicate absorption at 10 m and indicate a very heavily obscured, CT AGN. Submillimeter observations of NGC 4418 at high spatial resolution are also consistent with the presence of a CT AGN Sakamoto2013 . It's Chandra /Advanced CCD Imaging Spectrometer (ACIS) observations show a flat hard X-ray spectrum and imply the presence of a CT AGN, but due to the limited photon statistics this identification is considered as tentative Maiolino2003 .
Since 9 m - 22 m colour of NGC 4418 is 3.32, we consider to take [ 9 m - 22 m ] > 3.0 as a more reliable region that would avoid unobscured AGN selection.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4418 as an extremely chemically complex luminous infrared galaxy with a compact obscured nucleus, warm dense molecular gas, and likely young AGN/starburst activity.

quotes.quote: Our spectral scan confirms that the chemical complexity in the nucleus of NGC 4418 is one of the highest ever observed outside our Galaxy. The similar molecular abundances observed towards NCG 4418 and Arp 220 are consistent with a hot gas-phase chemistry, with the relative abundances of SiO and CH _3 OH being regulated by shocks and X-ray driven dissociation. The bright emission from vibrationally excited species confirms the presence of a compact IR source, with an effective diameter < 5 pc and brightness temperatures > 350 K. The molecular abundances and the vibrationally excited spectrum are consistent with a young AGN/starburst system. We suggest that NGC 4418 may be a template for a new kind of chemistry and excitation, typical of compact obscured nuclei (CON).

sources.summary: NGC 4418 shows OH79 absorption with OH163 emission, a buried Seyfert 2 nucleus, the sample's second deepest silicate absorption, and prominent CO(16-15) emission.

quotes.quote: There are three objects where OH79 shows absorption while OH163 shows emission: NGC 3079, NGC 4418, and NGC 4945. All three of these galaxies have buried nuclei, indicated by their deep 9.7 m silicate absorption. NGC 4418 and NGC 4945 have the second and third strongest silicate absorption in the entire 178 galaxy sample.

CO(16-15) is too weak to be detected in the majority of the sample; however, some examples of galaxies where the transition is clearly visible are NGC 253, NGC 1068, NGC 3079, NGC 4418, and NGC 4945. The transition is very prominent in NGC 4418, which has a deeply buried Seyfert 2 nucleus and the second deepest silicate absorption in the sample ( S_ sil = 4.00), while it is not so strong in NGC 253, which is a starburst.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4418 as a compact obscured nucleus with unusually warm infrared colors, possible dominant nuclear infrared luminosity, deep silicate absorption, and a compact molecular outflow.

quotes.quote: We note also that one of the CONs, NGC 4418, stands out with a substantially higher f_ 25 /f_ 60 ratio of 0.22 . This could be due to a larger fraction of mid-infrared radiation leaking out in this source, either due to a lower column density or a more clumpy medium, or to radiation from surrounding structures as, for example, the super star clusters suggested by var14 based on radio VLBI observations. It could also be that the difference is due to different fractions of the total L_ IR coming from the CON and the host galaxy.

In NGC 4418, a compact molecular outflow detected in low- J transitions of CO has been reported by flu19 and lut20 but its geometry is not known. The final CON, IRAS 17578-0400, does not have any published outflow signatures, but our HCN observations reveal a low-velocity elongation along its kinematic minor axis (Fig. fig:outflow_I17578 ). This may be a signature of a minor axis outflow which is directed almost perpendicular to our line of sight toward the galaxy. Possible signatures of noncircular motions are also seen in the ground state HCN and HCO ^ + lines in the form of asymmetric line profiles in some CONs. In most of them, the absorptions are stronger on the blueshifted side: Zw 049.057 aal15b , IRAS 17208-0014 (Fig. fig:detections ), and Arp 220 sak09,aal15b,mar16 or close to the line center: IRAS 17578-0400 (Fig. fig:detections ), and NGC 4418 sak13 .

sources.summary: The paper finds no APEX detection of vibrational HCN in NGC 4418, but says its elevated HCN/HCO+ ratio indicates strong infrared pumping without making it a correlation outlier.

quotes.quote: found strong detections of HCN v _2 =1 J=3 2 , J=4 3 in NGC 4418 and Arp 220 by ALMA observation, indicating somewhat impact on the HCN rotational line. All of our spectra do not show any detections of HCN v _2 =1 \ including NGC 4418 and Arp 220, possibly due to limited S/N of APEX observation. Both NGC 4418 and Arp 220 show elevated L'_ HCN /L'_ HCO^+ ratio, especially among SF-dominated galaxies (see Figure lineratio ), indicating possible influence of IR pumping. NGC 4418 and Arp 220 are the most heavily influenced galaxies by infrared pumping in our sample. However, both NGC 4418 and Arp 220 do not show outliers in correlations of L'_ dense L_ IR and _ L'_ dense _ L_ IR , indicating limited enhancement towards .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4418 as a highly obscured nuclear template, noting its silicate optical-depth profile and CON-like continuum in decomposition tests.

quotes.quote: For the silicate profile we use a template derived from a highly obscured source, namely NGC 4418, because the composition of silicates is likely different (resulting in a different profile) compared to extinction laws derived from the MW e.g. Tsuchikawa2021 or the line of sight is more complex than towards the galactic centre. It is also work noting that the optical depth peak of the silicate profile of NGC 4418 is 4.4 from the Spitzer spectroscopy whereas ground based observations with a smaller beam find a higher peak 7 Roche2015 . This means that the silicate profile used is still contaminated by some amount of star-formation and so the nuclear optical depth values inferred should be taken as lower limits for the true optical depth.

We use NGC 1797 for the star-forming component as this shows a typical spectrum of a star-forming galaxy with some silicate absorption, and the nucleus of NGC 4418 as the CON template. The star-forming continuum is normalised to the integrated continuum flux between 5 and 14 m. The PAH and emission lines from NGC 1797 are also included as part of the star-forming component and are normalised by the same factor as the continuum. The CON continuum from NGC 4418 is normalised by its integrated flux over the same wavelength range.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4418 as a deeply obscured type 2 comparison galaxy, noting its historic 6 micrometer water-ice detection and very deep nuclear silicate absorption.

quotes.quote: The first detection of 6 m water ice in extragalactic sources occurred in NGC 4418 ( Spoon01 ).
For comparison with the absorption bands in our sample, we use the Spitzer/IRS data of the deeply obscured type 2 NGC 4418 (see Appendix ngc4418 ).

First, in Fig. gatos_zoom (left) we compare the 6 m water optical depth of the sources with N _ H ^ X-ray (cm ^ 2 )>24 where the water ice absorption is prominent (i.e. NGC 5728, ESO 137-G034) with that of the extremely obscured nucleus of luminous infrared galaxy NGC 4418. We find that the water ice absorption band are similar when normalized to that of the 6 m feature (see right panel of Fig. gatos_zoom ). The very deep silicate absorption band observed in the nuclear region of NGC 4418 implies that there is a low contribution of nuclear warm material ( Roche15 ).

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4418 as a strongly obscured Seyfert II whose SED can be fit with an AGN-heated optically thick dust cloud and central power-law source.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert II NGC 4418 exhibits strong extinction along a nuclear diameter of less than 50 pc (FWHM, as estimated from the VISIR observations). Using radiative transfer models (Siebenmorgen et al. 2004), the SED can be fit by an AGN heated optically thick dust cloud of A_ V =86 mag. Extinction is computed from the outer radius r_ out =450 pc to the dust evaporation zone at 1 pc and for simplicity a constant dust density profile is assumed. The model includes a central power--law heating source of 10^ 11.8 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4418 as HC3N-rich and extreme, noting high abundance, vibrationally excited HC3N, the highest HC3N/HCN and IRAS 60/100 ratios, and the lowest C II/FIR upper limit.

quotes.quote: Recently, HC _3 N was found in high abundance in the highly obscured galaxy NGC 4418 aalto07a , as well as the ULIRG Arp 220 aalto02 . Rotational lines of vibrationally excited HC _3 N have recently been discovered in a few galaxies ( NGC 4418 costagliola10a , Arp 220 martin10 , and IC 860 costagliola10b ), therefore showing that it is important to take both radiative and collisional excitation into consideration when interpreting HC _3 N line emission from IR luminous galaxies. NGC 4418 , the object with the highest HC _3 N/HCN ratio in the sample is also the galaxy with the highest IRAS 60 m/100 m ratio. Also in this case, NGC 4418 is the most extreme galaxy, with the lowest upper limit on the C ii /FIR flux ratio.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 4418 as a strong far-infrared molecular-absorption case, with the highest C II deficit, deep high-lying absorption, strong OH65 absorption, and O I absorption.

quotes.quote: G-C11 also showed that the deficits are better correlated with L_ FIR /M_ gas than with L_ FIR , while PACS observations of three (U)LIRGs with strong line deficits, NGC 4418, Arp 220, and Mrk 231, showed deep absorption in high-lying molecular lines hereafter G-A12 and G-A14 gon12,gon14 . NGC 4418 is a case in point, as it shows the highest [ C ii ] deficit, a moderate L_ IR 1.5 10^ 11 \ but a high L_ FIR /M_ gas 400 / \ (G-C11), and the highest-lying \ absorption among all galaxies with full FIR spectra (G-A12).

While most ULIRGs are strong in OH65, some of them (IRAS 09022-3615, UGC 5101, IRAS F10565+2448) are weak and, conversely, there are three sources with moderate luminosities that are strong in OH65 (NGC 4418, Zw 049.057, and IRAS F11506-3851).
In Arp 220 and NGC 4418, where the [ O i ] 63 m line is observed primarily in absorption, a significant fraction of the Si absorption is likely produced in extended regions (G-A12). .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4418 as a local compact-nucleus LIRG with far-infrared inflow signatures, using its [O I] inverse P-Cygni profile as a SPICA detectability example.

quotes.quote: Inflow signatures are found in all observed (in the far-IR) local LIRGs (with (1-4) 10^ 11 ) that host a very compact and warm nucleus (NGC 4418, Zw 049.057, Arp 299a, and IRAS 11506 3851), which may represent galaxies in an early stage of merging, or accreting metal-rich gas from the intergalactic medium via efficient ``cold'' inflows. We show in Fig. inflows the inverse P-Cygni observed in the [ O i ] 63 m line towards NGC 4418 ( L_ IR 10^ 11 , z 0.007 ) smoothed to a resolution of R=1000 . The errorbars indicate the uncertainty expected for SPICA /SAFARI in 5 hours of observing time at z=0.2 . For a LIRG with L_ IR 4 10^ 11 , this type of line shape would be detected up to z 0.4 .

sources.summary: The survey presents NGC 4418 as a very red, heavily obscured nucleus with controversial MIR emission and no strong star-formation signature in IRS spectra.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, two of the remaining galaxies, NGC 4418 (aka NGC 4355) and 2MASX J04282604-0433496, show no signs of strong star formation, judging from their /IRS spectra asmus_subarcsecond_2014 . Instead, NGC 4418 hosts a highly obscured nucleus with the obscuration probably causing the red colour (see e.g., roche_silicate_2015 ). In fact, both objects are among the reddest in terms of colour ( >5 ) of all galaxies in the LASr-GPS. While, the nature of the dominating MIR emitter in NGC 4418 is still somewhat controversial (e.g., sakamoto_submillimeter_2013, varenius_radio_2014 ), the case of object makes clear that also heavy obscuration can lead to very red colours See also the similarly mysterious Arp 220; e.g., martin_unbearable_2016, paggi_x-ray_2017, sakamoto_resolved_2017, yoast-hull_-ray_2017 ).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4418 as an archetypal compact obscured nucleus template, noting absent 11.3 m PAH detection and a deeper nuclear silicate minimum than Spitzer spectra show.

quotes.quote: However, for the archetypal CON NGC 4418, the 11.3 m feature was not detected in its subarcsecond angular resolution ground-based mid-IR spectrum Roche15 . These authors also found a deeper silicate absorption band (i.e. lower flux level of the nuclear silicate absorption band minimum) compared to that measured in the total Spitzer/IRS spectrum. This suggests that in deeply embedded sources, the bulk of the observed PAH emission originates primarily in their circumnuclear regions and, therefore, is likely to experience substantially lower levels of extinction compared to the compact core.

This idea is demonstrated in Fig. Synth_con where we generate the IR spectra of galaxies containing CONs by using two template spectra: the first one is from NGC 4418, a galaxy whose SED is dominated by a very deep silicate absorption feature (black solid line, representing the nuclear CON) and the other is the host galaxy template from Marshall18 , cicumnuclear PAH spectrum (green solid line representing the host galaxy providing the circumnuclear emission).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4418 as a non-merger with known inclination and a high CN bright/faint ratio, plus an enshrouded compact core lacking a luminous AGN.

quotes.quote: The galaxies which are not mergers are NGC 4418, ESO 320-G030, and NGC 1365 Stierwalt2013 . For reference, the inclination angles of these three galaxies are i=62^ for NGC 4418 Sakamoto2013 , i=43^ for ESO 320-G030 Pereira2016 , and i=40^ for NGC 1365 Sakamoto2007 .
There are two galaxies with (CN bright)/(CN faint) > 2 , NGC 1068 and NGC 4418, for which using this calculation method would mean 1 .
An optical spectroscopic study of NGC 4418 found an enshrouded compact core with no luminous AGN Ohyama2019 .

sources.summary: The paper finds tentative Chandra evidence for a Compton-thick AGN in NGC 4418 and notes infrared support plus optical line widths more consistent with LINERs than Seyferts.

quotes.quote: NGC 4418 also shows evidence for a flat spectrum emission component which may imply the presence of a Compton-thick AGN, but the limited photon statistics make this identification somewhat tentative. We note, however, that infrared observations also suggest the presence of a heavily obscured AGN in this galaxy spoon01,dudley97 . Also in this case, an absorbed powerlaw with frozen to 1.9 gives a significantly worse fit.
However, at least in the case of NGC 4418, for which lehnert95 present data with higher spectral resolution, the width of the lines is more consistent with LINERs than Seyferts.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4418 as a very extinguished galaxy with deep silicate absorption, ice and hydrocarbon features, and compact infrared luminosity requiring unusual nuclear conditions.

quotes.quote: The second unusual spectrum is that of the nucleus of NGC 4418, originally classified by as a ``very extinguished galaxy'', for its very deep 10 m silicate absorption feature. Instead of the commonly detected PAH emission features, the 5.5--8 m ISO spectrum of its nucleus is dominated by absorption features of water ice (6 m), hydrocarbons (6.85 m and 7.25 m) and methane ice (7.67 m), reminiscent of the line of sight towards our own Galactic Center ( ). Supporting groundbased observations indicate that most of the infrared luminosity (L _ IR = 10 ^ 11 L _ ) from NGC 4418 is produced in a compact nucleus with a radius of less than 80 pc ( ). If powered entirely by star formation, the conditions of the nuclear gas and dust within this environment must be exceptional not to give rise to emission features commonly associated with star formation.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4418 as an infrared galaxy with an especially low [C II]/FIR ratio, low molecular gas content, and [O I] 63 micron absorption.

quotes.quote: At the same time, the scatter in the ratio increases by almost a factor of two: some high redshift galaxies do not show a [ C 2 ] deficit, even if their far-infrared luminosities are higher than 10^ 12 , and the source with the lowest [ C 2 ] / FIR ratio is NGC 4418 , a galaxy with 10^ 11 .
With this new representation, high redshift galaxies overlap with their low redshift counterparts and NGC 4418 is shifted to the right of the figure due to its relatively low molecular gas content.
The [ O 1 ] 63 \ line was detected in absorption at some velocities in NGC 4418 , Arp 220 and IRAS 17208-0014 .

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4418's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4418 is an infrared-luminous inclined spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0073 ( D 37.8 Mpc) with a highly obscured active nucleus optically classified as a Sy 2.0 veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . The nucleus might also contain a compact massive starburst (e.g., imanishi_near-infrared_2004,aalto_luminous_2007 ); see sakamoto_submillimeter_2013 for a recent detailed study). Owing to the high obscuration, the AGN is difficult to verify at X-ray wavelengths maiolino_elusive_2003 .

sources.summary: The paper groups NGC 4418 among strong HCN-vib emitters, reporting a high AGN fraction, possible lower-velocity outflow signatures, and HCN-vib luminosity only a few times below the brightest sources.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, at least two other sources with strong HCN-vib emission, NGC 4418 and UGC 5101, have AGN fractions 50 .
Possible outflow signatures are also found in Zw 049.057 fal18 as well as in NGC 4418 and IRAS 22491-1808 flu18 , although at lower velocities.
However, it is still less luminous than in the ULIRGs Arp 220 W and IRAS 17208-0014, and only a factor of two or three brighter than in the LIRGs Zw 049.057, IC 860, and NGC 4418.

gmw_00000213.name: NGC 4438
gmw_00000213.overview: NGC 4438 is a highly disturbed Virgo Cluster spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0503153, arXiv:1310.2770] hosting a central active nucleus classified as a Seyfert 2 or LINER [arXiv:1001.3597, arXiv:2306.04513]. It has been heavily affected by tidal interactions with neighboring galaxies [arXiv:1001.0980, arXiv:2306.04513] and ongoing ram pressure stripping [arXiv:1204.0430, arXiv:0901.2770], which have displaced its gas and dust into extraplanar regions [arXiv:1005.2331, arXiv:0701.610].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4438 as a tidally disturbed, ram-pressure-stripped galaxy with molecular-dominated gas, shock-contaminated extraplanar emission, and reduced molecular-gas star formation efficiency outside the disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 4438 (Fig. fig:plot_n4438 ) had a strong gravitational interaction 100 Myr ago (Vollmer et al. 2005, Kenney et al. 2008). It now undergoes strong active ram pressure stripping (Vollmer et al. 2005) leading to double line profiles in the strong western extraplanar CO emission. The gas content of NGC 4438 is dominated by the molecular phase.

The star formation contours follow the gas contours only at the northern tip of the extraplanar region. The reason for this behavior is that most of the H emission in the extraplanar region stems from shock-ionized gas and not from H ii regions (Kenney Yale 2002, Vollmer et al. 2009). We therefore rely on the star formation map based on UV emission. At molecular gas surface densities > 15 M _ pc ^ 2 the star formation rate is proportional to the molecular gas surface density. At smaller molecular gas surface densities the correlation steepens. The star formation efficiency with respect to the molecular gas SFE_ mol = _ * / _ mol decreases by about a factor of 3 between the disk and the extraplanar regions.

sources.summary: The paper presents VCC 1043 as a strongly disturbed Virgo disk galaxy affected by tidal interaction and ram pressure, with tails, displaced gas clouds, ionized filaments, and mapped optical gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: The Virgo cluster galaxy \ (VCC 1043) is a prototype of a disk interacting within a dense environment. Its main properties are listed in Tab. galax . It is one of the closest objects to the cluster core at only 1 \ in projection (or 280 kpc) from M87 and 28 \ (140 kpc) from M86. Its morphology is the most perturbed among the Virgo galaxies, showing prominent stellar tails from North-East (NE) to South-West (SW) of the disk and a huge dust lane West of the disk (Fig. imtot ), molecular and neutral gas clouds displaced up to 1 (4.7 kpc) West of the disk (Combes et al. 1988; Cayatte et al. 1990), very extended (up to 2 or 9.4 kpc) ionized optical and X-ray emitting gas filaments (Kenney et al. 1995, hereafter referred to ; Machacek et al. 2004) and an elongated region of 1.4 GHz continuum emission (Hummel et al. 1983). It is one of the most \ deficient disks in the cluster (Cayatte et al. 1990, 1994).

Using Fabry-Perot interferometry data at an effective angular resolution of 2 , the optical velocity field of the galaxy was mapped for the first time. \ is one of the most disturbed Virgo spirals which has probably undergone a tidal interaction with the massive companion , and which is probably undergoing the effects of ram pressure stripping by the hot ICM in the cluster core. The \ velocity field reveals the complex gas kinematics within the galactic disk, the nuclear shells and the external filaments.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4438 as a strongly perturbed Virgo galaxy with tidal and ram-pressure effects, extraplanar CO, well-mixed H i and H2, and no significant offset H i.

quotes.quote: NGC 4438 has a strongly perturbed stellar disk with a prominent stellar tidal arm pointing to the north. This perturbation is due to a rapid and close gravitational interaction with its companion S0 galaxy NGC 4435 (Combes et al. 1988, Vollmer et al. 2005). The gas disk is heavily truncated and almost exclusively molecular. Extraplanar CO with a mass of 5 10^8 M _ is detected to the west of the galactic disk. Detailed modelling of the interaction including the gravitational interaction, ram pressure, and an ISM-ISM collision showed that ram pressure (together with the tidal interaction) is the most important ingredient to reproduce the observed CO emission distribution and kinematics (Vollmer et al. 2005).

We find a remarkable resemblance between the two spectra tracing the molecular and the atomic hydrogen. The only differences are that (i) the ratio between the H i peak flux density at v=0 km s ^ 1 and v>0 km s ^ 1 is larger than that of the CO data and (ii) the CO peak at v>0 km s ^ 1 extends further to high velocities. This might indicate that both gas phases are well mixed.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4438 as an AGN candidate with disputed nuclear X-ray detections, possible Compton-thick obscuration, and circum-nuclear star formation contaminating MIR luminosity.

quotes.quote: NGC 4438 claim the detection of an X-ray nucleus embedded into diffuse emission in the AGN candidate NGC 4438 from observations, which was then verified by using the same data. The derived observed X-ray luminosity is = 39.02 . On the other hand, and claim that the nucleus is not detect again from the same data. However, their upper limit is consistent with the above value.

Finally, argue that the AGN might in fact be Compton-thick obscured based on the X-ray spectral slope, the low X-ray-- ratio, and the high upper limit on the equivalent width of any Fe K line. While this scenario would be consistent with the MIR--X-ray correlation putting NGC 4438 very close to it, one has to note that relatively strong circum nuclear star formation is present in this object, which might easily also dominate the subarcsecond MIR luminosity (see also mason_nuclear_2012 ). Better high angular resolution MIR data are required to distinguish between AGN and starburst dominance.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4438 as a perturbed Virgo spiral whose multiphase ISM is shaped by both a past tidal encounter and ongoing ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: Ram pressure stripping of the multiphase ISM is studied in the perturbed Virgo cluster spiral galaxy NGC 4438. This galaxy underwent a tidal interaction 100 Myr ago and is now strongly affected by ram pressure stripping. Deep VLA radio continuum observations at 6 and 20 cm are presented.

The strongly H i deficient spiral galaxy NGC 4438 is a unique case in the Virgo cluster. The tidal arms of this highly inclined galaxy are evidence of its tidal interaction 100 Myr ago with NGC 4435 (Combes et al. 1988, Vollmer et al. 2005) or M 86 (Kenney et al. 2008). The disk of interstellar matter is severely truncated and extraplanar gas is found to the west of the galaxy center.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC4438 as a strongly tidally disturbed Virgo galaxy and evaluates whether the nearby plume traces interactions with NGC4435, M86, or IC3355.

quotes.quote: NGC4438 (Arp 120) is the most dramatic example of a tidally disturbed galaxy in the Virgo cluster. Dynamical models suggest that NGC4438 has been perturbed by a high-velocity ( 800 ) interaction with the companion S0 NGC4435 (e.g., vollmer05 ). However, recent H observations favour a different scenario in which NGC4438 has collided with the giant elliptical galaxy M86 kenney08 .

If at the distance of the Virgo cluster (16.5 Mpc, mei07 ), the plume would have a physical size of 33 kpc (in both right ascension and declination), extending up to 70 kpc from NGC4438. Its and CO recessional velocities ( 14 ) are roughly consistent with the velocity of NGC4438 ( V_ = 104 2 and W_ 50 = 244 5 , giovanelli07 ) but significantly different from the redshift of NGC4435 ( V 801 ). This apparently rules out a gravitational interaction between NGC4438 and NGC4435: given their large velocity difference, we should expect the stripping material to lie at a velocity intermediate between the two systems.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4438 as a disturbed Virgo spiral interacting with M86; cool X-ray gas and H filaments between them likely originated in a previous collision.

quotes.quote: Two other galaxies are detected in the X-rays nearby: NGC 4388 and NGC 4438. These spiral galaxies have absolute redshifted velocities of 2566 ^ 1 NGC 4388, Rines2008 and 71 ^ 1 NGC 4438, Kenney1995 , respectively.

While all four of the galaxies lie near one another in projection, the X-ray data combined with absolute velocity measurements along the line-of-sight suggest that only NGC 4438 and M86 are interacting with one another in any substantial way.

The origin of this 10^ 4 phase of gas spatially coincident with soft X-ray emission between M86 and NGC 4438 is likely a previous collision between the two. The existing multiwavelength data are all consistent with this scenario, although further data will be required to rigorously confirm this. The X-ray emitting gas in this filament region appears poorer in metals than the gas detected at the center of M86, implying that this cool X-ray emitting gas likely originated in NGC 4438.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4438 as a ram-pressure-stripped, tidally disturbed Virgo spiral with gas-gas collisions, extraplanar CO, strong radio continuum, and reduced star formation.

quotes.quote: The CND and AGN tori have spatial extents of about 10 pc and rotation timescales of 10^ 4 yr, whereas the relevant scales and timescales in NGC 4438, the Taffy galaxies, and Stephan's Quintet are of the order of tens of kpc and 100 Myr. The common property of all systems is that they are undergoing gas-gas collisions with high energy injection rates. In these collisions, one gaseous body is the turbulent clumpy multi-phase ISM, while the other can be of different mean density and temperature (e.g. ISM, intragroup or intracluster gas): NGC 4438 is affected by ongoing ram pressure caused by its rapid motion through the Virgo intracluster medium (Vollmer et al. 2005, 2009), the Taffy galaxies recently experienced a head-on ISM-ISM collision (Condon et al. 1993, Vollmer et al. 2012), and the intragroup gas of the Stephan's Quintet is compressed by a high-velocity intruder galaxy.

NGC 4438 had a tidal interaction 100 Myr ago (Combes et al. 1988) probably associated with an ISM-ISM collision (Kenney et al. 1995). In addition, the galaxy undergoes strong on-going ram pressure stripping leading to extraplanar CO emission and a characteristic double-line profile in the extraplanar region (Vollmer et al. 2005). Strong radio continuum emission is associated with the extraplanar gas (Vollmer et al. 2009).

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4438 was heavily affected by M87 residual sidelobes, requiring restricted L-band imaging and matched Q/U, total-intensity, and spectral-index products.

quotes.quote: Three Virgo galaxies: NGC 4192, NGC 4388 and NGC 4438 These galaxies are highly affected by contamination from strong sources in the field, such as M87, rendering higher than normal rms noise and artifacts hard to clean out. This may have an effect on both spectral index results and polarization.

Also NGC 4438 is strongly affected by residual side lobes from M87. Despite attempts of self calibration and peeling, the rms could not be brought down to lower than almost three times the expected value. L-band observations of NGC 4438 differ from the other galaxies, in that only the upper half of the frequency range (spectral windows 16-31) was used for imaging, with a central frequency of 1.77 GHz. Q and U images were made over the same upper half of the frequency range in order to match the total intensity image, as well as the spectral index image.

sources.summary: The paper identifies VCC1043 as a centrally active Virgo galaxy with extended, extraplanar non-jet radio emission, but notes that subtracting central FIRST flux removes its radio excess and that it also had a tidal interaction.

quotes.quote: All four galaxies are classified as centrally active (VCC0836 (NGC 4388): Sy2, VCC1043 (NGC 4388): Sy/LINER, VCC1632 (NGC 4552): LINER/HII, VCC1727 (NGC 4579): Sy2/LINER). In addition, VCC0836, VCC1043, and VCC1727 galaxies show extended radio emission. In the case of VCC1043 the radio emission, which is not due to a jet, is even extraplanar (Kotanyi et al. 1983). But as can be seen in Fig. fig:radio_fir (e), when one subtracts the flux density of the FIRST central point sources, there is no more radio excess observed.

The only possible case is VCC1043 (NGC 4438), but there the galaxy had also a strong tidal interaction (Combes et al. 1988). A clearer case is the edge-on galaxy VCC1516 (NGC 4522) where high column density extraplanar H i is detected (Kenney et al. 2003, in prep.). Both galaxies do not show an enhanced, asymmetric radio continuum emission.

sources.summary: The paper presents Herschel SPIRE observations of NGC4438, finding extra-planar dust aligned with stripped gas and arguing that its disturbed Virgo-cluster environment perturbs gas and dust together.

quotes.quote: We present Herschel SPIRE observations of the perturbed galaxy NGC4438 in the Virgo cluster. These images reveal the presence of extra-planar dust up to 4-5 kpc away from the galaxy's disk. The dust closely follows the distribution of the stripped atomic and molecular hydrogen, supporting the idea that gas and dust are perturbed in a similar fashion by the cluster environment. Interestingly, the extra-planar dust lacks a warm temperature component when compared to the material still present in the disk, explaining why it was missed by previous far-infrared investigations. Our study provides evidence for dust stripping in clusters of galaxies and illustrates the potential of Herschel data for our understanding of environmental effects on galaxy evolution.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4438 as a Seyfert 2 with strong nuclear radio emission, prominent extraplanar continuum, shell-like polarized emission, and increasing polarization toward the synchrotron boundary.

quotes.quote: NGC 4438 harbors a Seyfert 2 nucleus with an associated strong nuclear radio source (Hummel Saikia 1991). In addition, we observe prominent extraplanar total power emission at 20 cm and 6 cm extending further than emission at other wavelengths (Fig.  fig:n4438 ). The 6 cm polarized extraplanar emission has a shell-like distribution with a pronounced maximum in the south. We also observe polarized emission from the galactic disk south from the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4438 as probably in Virgo, highly distorted, gas-deficient from intracluster-medium interaction, and blue because of a tidal companion.

quotes.quote: NGC 4438 This seemingly foreground galaxy is very probably a member of the Virgo cluster. suggest that the deficiency of this highly distorted galaxy is caused not by the tidal companion, which should increase the line-width, but by interaction with the intracluster medium of the Virgo cluster. Moreover, this galaxy has a very small radial extent of its \ gas, as attested by the VLA map of . Interestingly, at the distance of Virgo, this galaxy would be very blue, which is easily explained by the presence of its tidal companion.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4438 as having an off-center hard source embedded in elongated diffuse emission, with radio and broad H evidence supporting an AGN interpretation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4438 (L1, S0/a) has only one point source, which is close to the center but not exactly centered (within 2 ). It has a hard X-ray spectrum and is embedded in soft diffuse emission with an elongated morphology. The spectrum of the central source is well fitted by a thermal plasma model or a plasma component plus a power-law with a total luminosity of L_ 0.5-10 keV 7.7 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 . Since the source is deeply embedded in diffuse emission, it is possible that it is an AGN.

sources.summary: NGC 4438 is described as a tidally disturbed Virgo spiral interacting with NGC 4435, with CO, H, and X-ray evidence of collision plus a strong central radio/LINER source.

quotes.quote: The asymmetrically disturbed optical morphology of NGC 4438 as well as observations in CO (Combes et al. , Vollmer et al. ), H (Kenney et al. , Chemin et al. ) and X-rays (Machacek et al. ) clearly suggest tidal interactions with the companion galaxy NGC 4435. Furthermore, the X-ray properties of the gas outside the nuclear regions of these galaxies trace their past collision (Machacek et al. ). NGC 4438 is the only galaxy in our small sample showing a strong central source; as it is also of earliest Hubble type (Sa), it has the strongest mass concentration (hence deepest gravitational potential minimum) in the centre.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4438 as a spiral near M86 whose gas was probably stripped during a high-speed encounter traced by large-scale H + [NII] filaments.

quotes.quote: Suggestive evidence for gravitational heating comes from new observations of the Virgo galaxy M86 (figure fig:m86 ) kenney_spectacular_2008 , where we have discovered a remarkable set of large scale H + [ NII ] filaments connecting the giant elliptical to NGC 4438, a spiral galaxy 23' ( 120 kpc) away. Spectroscopy of the filaments show a fairly smooth velocity gradient between the galaxies, strongly suggesting a recent gravitational interaction between them. The data suggest that as NGC 4438 passed by M86 at high velocity, its cold gas was probably stripped by the ISM of the elliptical, causing the spiral to become HI-deficient.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4438 as the nearby spiral whose tidal collision with M86 stripped cold gas, leaving debris observed between the two galaxies.

quotes.quote: More recently, Kenney et al. \ (2008, hereafter K08) detected strong H features extending from the nearby spiral NGC 4438 at 23 away, to within 1 of the nucleus of M86. The distribution and velocity of the ionized gas provides clear evidence for tidal interaction between these two giants (K08, Fig. 1 Cortese et al. \ 2010). In this scenario, we are observing debris from the spiral left in the wake of the collision with M86 with 10^9 M_ of cold gas removed from NGC 4438 (K08).

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC4438 as the nearby spiral interacting with M86, linking ionized gas and spatially correlated dust emission to stripped material between the galaxies.

quotes.quote: The discovery of atomic gas offset from the center of M86 and decoupled from its stellar disk supports a tidal interaction (Li van Gorkom 2001), confirmed when Kenney et al. \ (2008) detected strong H features extending from M86 to the nearby spiral NGC4438. The distribution and velocity of the ionized gas provide clear evidence for tidal interaction between these two giants. SPIRE observations of M86 and NGC4438 showed that the dust emission is spatially correlated with the ionized gas between the two galaxies (Gomez et al. \ 2010; Cortese et al. \ 2010a) implying that the dust is material stripped from the nearby spiral.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4438's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4438 is a highly-inclined disturbed spiral galaxy in the Virgo cluster at a distance of D= 13.7 2.2 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with an active nucleus, which has initially be classified as a broad-line LINER ho_search_1997-1 . However, point out that the LINER-like emission is actually not coming from the nucleus, which rather shows optical emission line ratios like an H II nucleus. Studies of the high-resolution X-ray emission regarding the existence of an AGN are not conclusive machacek_chandra_2004,satyapal_link_2005,gonzalez-martin_x-ray_2006,gonzalez-martin_fitting_2009,flohic_central_2006 .

sources.summary: NGC 4438 is highlighted as a disrupted Virgo galaxy whose interactions produce filaments, a LINER-like central source, inner lobes, and a western radio bubble.

quotes.quote: VCC 1043 (NGC 4438) NGC 4438 is a unique object in the Virgo cluster which shows strong disruption of the stellar component. This was caused by a gravitational interaction with NGC 4435 and NGC 4406 (M 86), to which it is connected by filaments visible in H emission Kenney2008AEllipticals . NGC 4438 shows peculiar radio emission - a central point source corresponds to the LINER-type AGN Decarli2007TheCluster and unresolved inner lobes Hota2007NGCWavelengths , accompanied by a radio-bubble in the west Wezgowiec2007TheSpirals,Vollmer2009Astrophysics4438 .

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC4438 as underpredicted by 4-7 orders of magnitude, notes outflow bubbles implying angular momentum, and finds the adjusted model within about one order.

quotes.quote: Sources that were under-predicted 4-7 orders of magnitude are: NGC1291, NGC1316, NGC1553, NGC4261, NGC4438, NGC4594, NGC4697 and IC4296.

NGC1291 is an early type spiral, NGC1316 is disturbed early type elliptical, NGC 1553 has a spiral feature passing threw a center, NGC 4261 core is surrounded by a disk of gas and dust, NGC4438 has an outflow bubbles which also may indicate angular momentum to dominate the accretion flow, NGC4594 is a spiral galaxy, the last two sourced NGC 4697 and IC4296 are elliptical radio galaxies.

Other sources (NGC1291, NGC1316, NGC4438, NGC4472, M87, NGC4594, NGC4697, IC1459, IC4296) differ from their observational counterparts of about 1 order of magnitude, lots of them even less than order of magnitude (see Table 1.)

sources.summary: The paper calls NGC 4438 the most surprising object in its polarized-radio sample, linking strong tidal arms, a southeastern disk/bulge maximum, and extraplanar polarized emission coincident with gas detections.

quotes.quote: NGC 4438 has strong tidal arms and shows the most surprising distribution of polarized radio continuum emission. Within the disk/bulge region we observe a maximum to the south east. However, most of the polarized emission is extraplanar and coincides with detections of extraplanar CO (Combes et al. 1988, Vollmer et al. 2005), H i (Cayatte et al. 1990) and H (Kenney et al. 1995).

gmw_00000214.name: NGC 4457
gmw_00000214.overview: NGC 4457 is a nearly face-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0406243] hosting a LINER nucleus [arXiv:1310.2770] and a weak bar [arXiv:0808.1841]. It features a truncated hydrogen disk with asymmetric star-forming regions [arXiv:2201.05080, arXiv:astro-ph/0406243], a radio jet [arXiv:2201.05080], and a faint, circular outer ring [arXiv:0808.1841, arXiv:astro-ph/0406243].

sources.summary: The paper explains NGC 4457's faint, nearly circular outer ring and absent R1 ring as possibly resulting from a very weak bar whose pattern speed increased after growth.

quotes.quote: Dissolving the R1 ring and NGC 4457 We found in section 2.2 that the R1 ring dissolves when the bar pattern speed increases by more than 8 after bar growth.

NGC 4457, an ()SAB(s)0/a galaxy, appears to be lacking an R1 ring. According to laurikainen04 NGC 4457's bar has Q_T=0.09 , which corresponds to a very weak bar. It is therefore unlikely that loss of the R1 ring is due to the strength of the bar, as we find that strong bars dissolve the R1 ring and weakening bars can leave behind a double ring. It is possible, however, that the lack of an R1 ring is the result of a bar that has increased in pattern speed.

For this reason, in Figure mon4457.eps we compare NGC 4457 with the last frame ( t=25P_ b,0 ) of simulation 5, which has a bar that is speeding up after bar growth, d _b/dt=0.0004 . NGC 4457's outer rings are very faint in the B-band image, yet it does appear that the outer ring is circular and no R1 ring is evident. It is possible that the pattern speed of this galaxy's bar has increased since the bar finished growing, thereby destroying any R1 ring that would have formed shortly after bar growth.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4457 as having asymmetric H-alpha and H I distributions, extended asymmetric radio emission, steep eastern-arm spectral index, and broadly detected 6 cm polarization.

quotes.quote: NGC 4457 NGC 4457 has an asymmetric H distribution with a prominent arm in the southern galactic disk. The H i extent is smaller than the optical disk and quite asymmetric with the peak coinciding with the prominent H arm (Chung et al. 2009). The radio continuum emission distribution is extended and asymmetric with an arm structure to the east. This arm extends beyond the H i distribution. While the spectral index is shallow ( 0.5 ) in the starforming regions, it is steep ( 0.9 ) in the eastern arm structure.

sources.summary: The atlas describes NGC 4457 as a face-on Disky galaxy with many star-forming regions, a known radio jet, rotating gas and stars, and disturbed non-circular gas motions.

quotes.quote: NGC 4457 (see Fig. Figure_AB6 ): This face-on galaxy has many star forming regions, clearly visible in our H image (classified as Disky ), found only in the inner 30 Cortes2015 . This galaxy is known to host a radio jet, although the PA is not reported in the literature see and references therein Nemmen2014 . Both the stellar and the ionised gas components are rotating, but non-circular motions have been detected. This is seen in the gas velocity map, that is more disturbed than that of the stars, specially along the stellar kinematic minor axis Cortes2015 . In our image it is clearly visible an H arm in the approaching side of the galaxy, as in previous works Chemin2006,Cortes2015 .

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4457 has a LINER AGN candidate with circumnuclear star formation and a nuclear X-ray point source, but MIR contamination weakens the result.

quotes.quote: NGC 4457 The LINER AGN candidate in NGC 4457 shows significant circumnuclear star formation in the MIR and X-rays but also a nuclear X-ray point source satyapal_link_2005 . Its observed X-ray luminosity is = 38.89 flohic_central_2006,gonzalez-martin_x-ray_2009 , while argue that the source has = 40.57 assuming that it is Compton-thick obscured owing to the low X-ray spectral index and X-ray-- ratio. The expectations from the MIR--X-ray correlation on and are consistent with this scenario. However, the nuclear MIR luminosity used might still be affected or even dominated by star formation, so this result is not robust (see also mason_nuclear_2012 ).

sources.summary: The paper finds a bright hard central source in NGC 4457, interprets its spectrum and radio detection as an embedded AGN, and classifies the LINER as composite.

quotes.quote: NGC 4457 (L2, S0/a) has one bright central source with a hard spectrum, embedded in soft diffuse emission. The spectrum of the central source is well fitted by a power-law plus plasma model or a two-temperature plasma model ( L_ 0.5-10 keV =1.9 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 ). This can be interpreted as the spectrum of an embedded AGN and is confirmed by the detection of this source at 1.49 GHz C87 . From this detection we infer R_ X = -1.6 , which means that this source is radio loud.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4457's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4457 is a low-inclination spiral galaxy in the Virgo cluster at a distance of D= 17.4 3.5 Mpc tully_nearby_1988 , hosting a LINER nucleus ho_search_1997-1 . The detection of a hard X-ray point source supports the presence of an AGN, while additional diffuse soft emission indicates an AGN/starburst composite nature satyapal_link_2005,gonzalez-martin_x-ray_2006,flohic_central_2006 . A compact radio source at 6 cm wavelength embedded within a halo with an extent of 10 840 pc was detected in the nucleus of NGC 4457 (PA 177 ; van_der_hulst_structure_1981 ) but remained undetected at 2 cm nagar_radio_2002 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4457 as a severely truncated, nearly face-on galaxy with a regular R morphology, a strong but truncated H disk, and H emission concentrated in one peculiar spiral arm.

quotes.quote: The severely truncated galaxy NGC 4457 (image given in PI) has a regular R morphology and a nearly circular outer ring, which suggests we are viewing the galaxy close to face-on. The galaxy has a relatively strong but truncated H disk with much of the H emission arising from one peculiar spiral arm. While this arm may resemble those seen in ICM-ISM simulations (Schulz Struck 2001), NGC 4457 is located 9 \ from M87 where the ICM is thought to be very tenuous.

gmw_00000215.name: NGC 4472
gmw_00000215.overview: NGC 4472, also known as M49, is a giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1611.04435, arXiv:0904.0526] that dominates a Virgo Cluster sub-clump [arXiv:1007.5322, arXiv:2108.07341]. It features an intricate system of debris shells [arXiv:1611.04435, arXiv:1004.1473] and is currently interacting with the dwarf galaxy VCC 1249 [arXiv:1611.04435, arXiv:1205.3095].

sources.summary: The survey finds M49 hosts debris shells and little surrounding ICL, with present-day accretion dominated by dwarf stripping and future cluster-core infall likely to disperse its shells.

quotes.quote: The giant elliptical M49 (981 ; Cappellari 2011) hosts an intricate system of debris shells, described in detail in our earlier papers (J+10, Mihos 2013a), and seen also in deep imaging by the NGVS and VEGAS teams (Arrigoni Battaia 2012 and Capaccioli 2015, respectively). Figure  M49streams shows the more extended field around M49, where the NW and SE shells can be seen in M49's outer halo. At lower surface brightness we find a stream of extremely diffuse light in the field northeast of M49, along a line roughly connecting to the spiral galaxy NGC 4519 (1241 ).

Aside from these two features and the shell system surrounding M49, we find no extended diffuse light or small tidal streams in the field brighter than =29.0. Deeper inside M49's halo itself, the only ongoing accretion event involves the stripping of the dwarf irregular VCC 1249 (McNamara 1994; Lee 2000; Arrigoni Battaia 2012). This is in marked contrast to the high density fields around M87 and M86/M84 and suggests that the current rate of ICL production around M49 is lower than that in the cluster core, as might be expected if ICL production traced either galaxy density or cluster potential well.

Meanwhile, M49 itself is thought to be falling into the Virgo Cluster from the south, as evidenced by the X-ray bow shock to the north of M49 (Irwin Sarazin 1996, Kraft 2011). When the galaxy enters the denser environment of the Virgo Cluster core, its delicate system of shells will likely be disrupted due to encounters with other galaxies and the cluster potential itself, adding to Virgo's growing ICL component.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes NGC 4472 globular-cluster data and argues that its apparent blue tilt depends on magnitude cuts and does not prove different galaxy formation histories.

quotes.quote: However, both restrict the analysis of blue tilts in individual galaxies to the three most luminous (massive) candidates M49 (NGC 4472), M87 (NGC 4486) and M60 (NGC 4649). Consequently these are also the most GC rich galaxies in the sample, although NGC 4649 and NGC 4472 have fewer than half the number of GCs as M87 within the ACS field of view.

On the other hand both claim that NGC 4472 does not show a `blue-tilt', with Mieske et al. (2006) measuring a slope of -0.008 0.024. This has been suggested as evidence of differences in the formation mechanisms of these galaxies, and has been argued as proof that the `blue-tilt' is real. However, in a recent study of a different NGC 4472 data set Lee et al. (2008) claim to find evidence of a `blue-tilt' in this galaxy. In order to explore this apparent discrepancy we re-analyzed the GO-9401 images of NGC 4472 studied by Strader et al. (2006) and Mieske et al. (2006) in a manner identical to the M87 study described above.
Restricting the `blue-tilt' analysis to the same z=22 mag of the M87 study we indeed find a `blue-tilt' in this galaxy with a d(g-z)/dz slope of -0.025, similar to that of NGC 4649.

sources.summary: The paper uses M49 with M87 as a massive Virgo central galaxy whose gravity shapes the cluster potential and hot-gas environmental parameter.

quotes.quote: However, here we define a gravitational potential index ( ) to quantify the cluster environment instead of the simple cluster-centric distance, because there are two massive galaxies M87 and M49 in the central area. Both galaxies are quite dominant in the and thought to significantly affect the cluster members. It is the normalized sum of the gravitational potentials from the two galaxies, as follows: =< m_ 87 r_ 87 + m_ 49 r_ 49 >, where m_ 87 and m_ 49 are the masses of M87 and M49 The mass of M87 is 4.2 times larger than that of M49 fer12 . , respectively, and r_ 87 and r_ 49 are projected distances from M87 and M49, respectively. For convenience, normalization is carried out so that 1 is the value at the point on the line connecting M87 with M49, where the summed gravitational potential is minimum between the two galaxies.

Third, the parameter considers only the gravitation of M87 and M49, despite the existence of more relatively massive galaxies in the , such as M60 or M86. This is because we focused on the most massive galaxies that significantly affect the distribution of the intracluster medium in the . As shown in Figure gp , the hot gas distribution (traced by the X-ray map) is determined mainly by M87 and M49, whereas the importance of the other galaxies seems to be relatively weak.

sources.summary: The paper maps M49's planetary-nebula population to 155 kpc, finding a higher outer-halo PN specific frequency, a PNLF distance of 18.1 Mpc, and signs of a localized accretion-related population.

quotes.quote: We identify 738 PNe out to a radius of 155 kpc from M49's centre from which we define a complete sample of 624 PNe within a limiting magnitude of m_ 5007,lim =28.8 . Comparing the PN number density to the broad-band stellar surface brightness profile, we find a variation of the PN-specific frequency ( parameter) with radius. The outer halo beyond 60 kpc has a 3.2 times higher parameter compared to the main galaxy halo ( _ 2.5, inner ^ M49 =(3.20 0.43) 10^ 9 PN L^ 1 _ , bol ), which is likely due to contribution from the surrounding blue IGL.

We use the Planetary Nebulae Luminosity Function (PNLF) as an indicator of distance and stellar population. Its slope, which correlates empirically with galaxy type, varies within the inner halo. In the eastern quadrant of M49, the PNLF slope is shallower, indicating an additional localised, bright PN population following an accretion event, likely that of the dwarf irregular galaxy VCC1249. We also determined a distance modulus of _ PNLF =31.29^ +0.07 _ 0.08 for M49, corresponding to a physical distance of 18.1 0.6 Mpc , which agrees with a recent surface-brightness fluctuations distance.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4472 as the observed basis for a representative single-phase elliptical galaxy, contrasting its central cooling and AGN cycles with NGC 5044.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we carry out 3D adaptive mesh refinement (AMR) simulations to study AGN feedback and multi-phase gas in two idealized elliptical galaxies based on the observations of NGC 5044 and NGC 4472. NGC 5044 is a multi-phase galaxy (MPG) in Werner2014 , and NGC 4472 is a single-phase galaxy (SPG).

Both of them are central dominant galaxies of low-mass groups. NGC 5044 has extended multi-phase gas and NGC 4472 is observed to be a single-phase elliptical galaxy. We choose them as representatives of multi- and single-phase galaxies, and refer to them as MPG and SPG throughout the paper instead of using their actual names.

As in the case of the MPG, runaway cooling also happens first in the center of the galaxy, and triggers AGN feedback, but this does not lead to a major precipitation event with the formation of extended multi-phase gas. Instead, after the cold gas is quickly accreted onto the SMBH and AGN is turned off, condensation occurs again in the center of the galaxy, with no spatially extended multi-phase gas present.

sources.summary: The paper studies M49 as Virgo B's dominant brightest galaxy, using outer globular-cluster kinematics to trace halo assembly, stellar shells, an accretion signature, and interaction with VCC 1249.

quotes.quote: We present the first results of an MMT/Hectospec campaign to measure the kinematics of globular clusters (GCs) around M49---the brightest galaxy in the Virgo galaxy cluster, which dominates the Virgo B subcluster. The data include kinematic tracers beyond 95 kpc ( 5.2 effective radii) for M49 for the first time, enabling us to achieve three key insights reported here. First, beyond 20 30 ( 100-150 kpc), the GC kinematics sampled along the minor photometric axis of M49 become increasingly hotter, indicating a transition from GCs related to M49 to those representing the Virgo B intra-cluster medium. Second, there is an anomaly in the line-of-sight radial velocity dispersion ( _ ,los ) profile in an annulus 10-15 ( 50-90 kpc) from M49 in which the kinematics cool by _ ,los 150 relative to those in- or outward. The kinematic fingerprint of a previous accretion event is hinted at in projected phase-space, and we isolate GCs that both give rise to this feature, and are spatially co-located with two prominent stellar shells in the halo of M49.

Third, we find a subsample of GCs with velocities representative of the dwarf galaxy VCC 1249 that is currently interacting with M49. The spatial distribution of these GCs closely resembles the morphology of VCC 1249's isophotes, indicating that several of these GCs are likely in the act of being stripped from the dwarf during its passage through M49's halo.

sources.summary: The paper evaluates whether ngVLA astrometry of NGC 4472 could detect or exclude a putative supermassive black-hole binary linked to past galaxy mergers.

quotes.quote: For the early-type galaxy NGC 4472, we study how astrometry with a next-generation Very Large Array (ngVLA) could be used to monitor the reflex motion of the primary SMBH of mass M_ pri , as it is tugged on by the secondary SMBH of mass M_ sec . Casting the orbit of the putative SMBH binary in terms of its period P , semimajor axis a_ bin , and mass ratio q = M_ sec / M_ pri 1 , we find the following: (1) Orbits with fiducial periods of P = 4 yr and 40 yr could be spatially resolved and monitored. (2) For a 95 accuracy of 2 as per monitoring epoch, sub-parsec values of a_ bin could be accessed over a range of mass ratios notionally encompassing major ( q > 1 4 ) and minor ( q < 1 4 ) galaxy mergers. (3) If no reflex motion is detected for M_ pri after 1(10) yr of monitoring, a SMBH binary with period P = 4(40) yr and mass ratio q > 0.01(0.003) could be excluded.

We focus on the early-type galaxy NGC 4472, the dominant galaxy in Virgo Subcluster B e.g., jan10,arr12 . Throughout we assume a distance of 16.7 Mpc, where 81.0 pc subtends 1 bla09 . NGC 4472 may have experienced one or more galaxy mergers, as suggested from its overall slow rotation and its stellar core, which exhibits a substantial mass deficit, counter rotation and tangentially-biased orbits e.g., bet01,kra11,ems11,tho14 .

sources.summary: The proceeding describes M49’s extended blue halo and PN populations, using photometry and ePN.S kinematics to separate the main halo, intragroup light, and VCC 1249 interaction.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, the environment around M49 is characterised by a very extended halo, with a significant colour gradient, reaching blue colours of B-V = 0.65 at 100 kpc 6.25 R_ eff , 2013apj...764l..20m . Due to the age-metallicity degeneracy, one cannot directly infer the stellar population properties from the observed colours; therefore, the outer halo of M49 may be blue due to a relatively young (and metal-rich), or a metal-poor (and old) stellar population (or a combination of the two). A young stellar population 2 Gyr, 2013apj...764l..20m would have to be due to a recent merger with a relatively massive star-forming galaxy, but a recent merger is not visible in the deep photometry 2013apj...764l..20m, 2015a&a...581a..10c, 2017apj...834...16m .

Combining the data from the deep SuprimeCam narrow-band survey discussed above with that from the ePN.S survey allowed us to identify three distinct PN populations in the halo of M49 2018A&A...616A.123H . Two of them were already discussed previously, namely the main halo of M49 and the IGL of the Virgo Subcluster B. However, the decomposition of the line-of-sight velocity distribution (LOSVD) into halo and PN components was not straightforward due to the presence of a third component, namely the dwarf irregular galaxy VCC 1249. It is interacting with M49 and dominates the blue wing of the LOSVD due to a velocity difference of 500 km s ^ 1 between the two galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper uses M49 to test globular-cluster formation efficiency, comparing its stellar, gas, and globular-cluster density profiles against M87 and NGC 1399.

quotes.quote: M87, M49, and NGC 1399 are ideally suited to a closer examination of these issues, for three reasons: (1) They are relatively nearby, making it possible to observe internal trends within the systems in the first place.

(2) Each is a giant elliptical (M87 and NGC 1399 are actually type cD), and is therefore accompanied by a large GCS: N _ tot 13 500 for M87; 6 900 for M49; and 4 700 in NGC 1399 (e.g., har91 Harris 1991; see also 5 below). And, most importantly, (3) even this small sample of three galaxies suffices to bring out all aspects of the two specific frequency problems summarized in 2.2: M87, as is well known, suffers from both; M49 does not show the first, but has long been thought to exhibit the second; and NGC 1399 has been claimed as an example of the first problem, but not the second. Because of their proximity, these systems have been well studied in all respects of importance here.

These measurements are easily converted to projected stellar mass density profiles _ stars (R_ gc ) , as mass-to-light ratios have been obtained from a spectroscopic analysis applied uniformly to all three systems ( vdm91 van der Marel 1991). X-ray observations, which have been made with several different satellites, yield gas densities over still larger areas around each galaxy. Here, use will be made of the ROSAT data for M87 (taken from nul95 Nulsen B ohringer 1995); of ROSAT data for M49 ( irw96 Irwin Sarazin 1996); and of Einstein observations in NGC 1399 ( kil88 Killeen Bicknell 1988).

sources.summary: The paper describes M49 as the massive elliptical whose hot halo and tidal field interacted with VCC1249, stripping HI, shaping tails, and producing UV/HII features between the galaxies.

quotes.quote: Owing to a recent interaction between the dwarf galaxy VCC1249 and the halo gas of the elliptical galaxy M49, neutral hydrogen has been displaced from the interstellar medium of this dwarf into the Virgo intracluster medium. Motivated by recent near-ultraviolet (NUV) imaging from the GALEX Ultraviolet Virgo Cluster Survey (GUViCS) of the VCC1249/M49 system that shows significant ongoing/recent star formation in the compact regions, we aim to constrain the origin of these outlying HII regions with a multi-wavelength approach. Moreover, we analyze the color and luminosity profile of the galaxy to investigate its recent interaction with M49. Our new observations indicate that VCC1249 underwent a recent interaction with M49 in which both ram-pressure stripping and tidal interaction occured.

VCC1249 is a dIrr (Nilson et al. 1973) low-surface brightness galaxy, located 5.6 arcmin to the southeast of the giant elliptical galaxy M49 (Kumar Thonnard 1983), the brightest member of Virgo Cluster B, approximately 4 degrees (1.19 Mpc projected distance) south of M87 (Virgo Cluster A). This is not particularly surprising, since at the projected distance between M49 and VCC1249 of about 30 kpc, the X-ray emission is dominated by the hot ( 10^7 K Forman et al. 1985) gas belonging to the halo of M49 with a density of more than 10^ 3 cm ^ 3 (Fabian 1985).

sources.summary: The paper compares M49 with M87 to test whether nova rates depend on globular-cluster specific frequency, finding 29 candidates in each and no enhancement in M87 relative to M49.

quotes.quote: More recently, the idea that globular clusters may play a role in the formation of nova binaries gained further support by the observation that the nova rate in M87 (which has a cluster specific frequency, S_N=14 ) appeared to be 3 times higher than that observed for the more luminous Virgo elliptical M49, which is characterized by a cluster specific frequency of only S_N=3.6 sha00, fer03, bro06 . To better test the dependence of nova rate on cluster specific frequency, and thereby the idea that a significant fraction of novae are formed in a galaxy's globular cluster system, we initiated a survey with the MegaCam on the CFHT 3.6 m reflector to determine the relative nova rates in M49 and M87 using the same telescope, instrument, and filter, and on the same nights with the same temporal sampling sha13a . In a total of 4 epochs spanning 2 years of observation we discovered a total of 29 nova candidates in each galaxy.

The spatial distributions of the nova candidates from our preliminary analyses of M49 and M87 are shown in Figure 3. The distribution for M87 shows an excess of nova candidates at a distance beyond 10 ' from the nucleus. Clearly, there is no evidence from our data that the nova rate in M87 is enhanced relative to the rate in M49, particularly if we restrict our analysis to nova candidates that are clearly associated with the galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4472 as a brightest cluster galaxy in the sample, adjusts its X-ray luminosity catalog, and notes limited ACS coverage.
