The high angular resolution Chandra image (see Fig. 6) shows symmetric, 8 kpc long, arm-like features in the X-ray halo surrounding NGC4636. The leading edges of these features are sharp and are accompanied by temperature increases of 30 , as expected from shocks propagating in a galaxy atmosphere. Although the sharpness of the edges of the NE and SW arms appears similar to the sharp edges found along ``fronts'' in clusters (see discussion and references above), the cluster ``fronts'' are cold, while those in NGC4636 are hot.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 4636's X-ray halo, confirming a cavity, arm-like shock features, and a metal-rich plume interpreted as gas entrained by AGN activity.

quotes.quote: observations of NGC 4636 show disturbances in the galaxy X-ray halo, including arm-like high surface brightness features (tentatively identified as AGN driven shocks) and a possible cavity on the west side of the galaxy core. We present \ and \ spectral maps of NGC 4636 which confirm the presence of the cavity and show it to be bounded by the arm features. The maps also reveal a 15 kpc wide plume of low temperature, high abundance gas extending 25-30 kpc to the southwest of the galaxy.

The cavity appears to be embedded in this plume, and we interpret the structure as being entrained gas drawn out of the galaxy core during previous episodes of AGN activity. The end of the plume is marked by a well defined edge, with significant falls in surface brightness, temperature and abundance, indicating a boundary between galaxy and group/cluster gas. This may be evidence that as well as preventing gas cooling through direct heating, AGN outbursts can produce significant gas mixing, disturbing the temperature structure of the halo and transporting metals out from the galaxy into the surrounding intra-group medium.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4636 as an FIR-extended elliptical whose excess dust is too extended and old-stellar-population dominated to fit a recent dusty merger explanation.

quotes.quote: We find that the observed fluxes for the two remaining FIR-extended galaxies -- NGC 5044 and NGC 4636 -- are very much larger than predicted by our steady state model for the FIR spectral energy distribution. We believe that the dust in these galaxies has an entirely different origin. The large and extended FIR emission from NGC 5044 and NGC 4636, which is related to the enormous range of FIR luminosities in Figure 3, will be discussed in a separate forthcoming publication.

We adopted this explanation previously in our discussion of the excess dust in NGC 4636 (Temi, et al. 2003), which we interpreted as a dusty merger that occurred within the last 3 10^8 yrs, the estimated sputtering time in the hot gas in this galaxy. However, it has since been determined that the mean stellar age in NGC 4636 is very old (10.3 Gyrs, Sanchez-Blazquez et al. 2006), and this strongly argues against a merger 3 10^8 yrs ago with a dusty, star-forming galaxy. Moreover, we observe that the additional dust in NGC 4636 is extended (FW0.1M = 54.4 ^ in Table 1) and appears to be distributed rather evenly about the center unlike the irregular distribution that might be expected to result from a recent merger undergoing dynamical friction with accompanying ram-stripping and tidal disruption.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4636 as a dark-matter-rich elliptical galaxy, using globular-cluster velocities to recover rising mass-to-light ratios and a highly concentrated inner dark halo.

quotes.quote: We present the density structure of dark matter in the outer parts (to about 7 effective radii) of the elliptical galaxy NGC 4636, from the radial velocities of 174 globular clusters, using the non-parametric, inverse algorithm CHASSIS. We find the galaxy to be rich in dark matter, with R band mass-to-light ( M/L ) ratios rising to about 30, at nearly 4 R_e ; the K band M/L at about 3 R_e is found to be nearly 10. The result does not depend on applying the method to the red and blue globular clusters separately. This estimate of M/L is higher than the previous analysis from the same kinematic data. We also find that the dark matter distribution is highly concentrated towards the inner halo.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4636 as a nearby dominant elliptical whose twisted double radio source and X-ray spiral structures trace AGN interaction with group gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 4636 NGC 4636 is the dominant elliptical of a nearby poor group on the periphery of the Virgo cluster. The kinematics of globular clusters reveal an extended dark matter halo for the dominant elliptical (Chakrabarty Raychaudhury 2008), in agreement with mass estimates from Chandra and XMM-Newton observations (Johnson et al. 2009). Our GMRT image at 610 MHz has been presented in Baldi et al. (2009b), who investigated the X-ray morphology and temperature structure of the group core using a long Chandra exposure. Here we report the 610 MHz image, overlaid on the POSS-2, in the left panel of Fig. fig:ngc4636 , and present the new 235 MHz contours superimposed on the Chandra image in the right panel. The radio source structure is visible in better detail in the high-resolution image at 610 MHz.

sources.summary: The paper finds velocity broadening in NGC 4636, notes tension with resonance-scattering limits, and attributes method discrepancies partly to its complex morphology.

quotes.quote: Although there are significant systematic errors associated with the technique ( 150 ), we find broadening at the level of 280 to 500 in A 3112, NGC 1399 and NGC 4636.

We note that for NGC 4636, the 430-580 broadening we measure is apparently inconsistent with the 100 upper limit inferred from the presence of resonance scattering Werner09 . However, these values may still be in agreement if the broadening we measure is the result of ordered motion.
The object with the largest difference between the methods is NGC 4636. This target has a complex morphology so the Gaussian assumption may not be good in this case.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4636 as an optically normal, group-centered elliptical with excess, spatially extended 70 micron emission attributed to AGN-driven dust transport.

quotes.quote: However, and to our initial surprise, many optically normal E galaxies -- e.g. NGC 4636 and 5044 -- have L_ 70 that are 10-30 times brighter than our steady state prediction. This infrared excess indicates the presence of excess cold interstellar dust (of mass 10^5 M_ ) that cannot have come from local mass-losing stars.

Small open green circles in Figures 6 and 7 show the approximate computed locations of two galaxies -- NGC 5044 and NGC 4636 -- using our steady-state dust evolution model in which dust ejected from galactic stars is destroyed locally by sputtering. The green arrows illustrate the large additional (excess) contribution to L_ 70 which we interpret as dust in gas heated by AGN feedback events and then transported outward from galactic cores.

If our interpretation is correct, group-centered galaxies, like NGC 5044 and NGC 4636, move back and forth along the bottom of the banana in Figure 1 on timescales determined by the grain sputtering lifetime and the interval between AGN outbursts, which may be roughly comparable, 10^7 yrs.

sources.summary: The paper finds no six-month XMM-Newton spectral variability for NGC 4636, but reports low-confidence hard-band short-term variation and UVW1 variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 4636 : SMF0 is used to fit XMM -Newton data, i.e., variations are not found over six months period. Note that ^2_r 2 . Unfortunately, none of the proposed models are good enough to improve the final fit. From the analysis of the light curves, short term variations in the hard band are obtained from one XMM -Newton observation at 1.4 confidence level. In the UV, variations of 28 are obtained from the UVW1 filter.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4636 to show altered O VII line ratios, low resonant-to-forbidden ratios, cool absorbing gas, and H emission in its core.

quotes.quote: In some objects the O VII resonant-to-forbidden line ratio is decreased by either resonant scattering or charge-exchange boosting the forbidden line, as we show for NGC 4636.
We found (/f) line ratios lower than 1.25 in the RGS spectra of NGC 4636, M 89, and NGC 1404 as already shown in Pinto2014 .

We multiplied the two cie emission components for a collisionally-ionized absorbing model ( hot model in SPEX) and re-fit the RGS spectrum of NGC 4636. We obtained a column density of 1.24 0.30 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 with a temperature of 0.23 0.03 keV, which is lower than the 0.43 0.07 keV value measured for the cie component responsible for the O VII emission. The presence of such cool gas is suggested by the detection of a large amount of H emission in the core of NGC 4636 Werner2014 .

sources.summary: NGC 4636 is flagged as a low-dispersion outlier with unusually steep entropy, a plausibly precipitation-limited atmosphere, and very low central entropy.

quotes.quote: NGC 4636 NGC 4636 has _v = 199.5 2.7 , _K (1--10 kpc) = 1.00 0.02 , (t_ cool /t_ ff ) = 10.79 0.36 , and multiphase gas that does not extend beyond 2 kpc. It is an outlier because its entropy slope is unusually large among the galaxies with _v < 240 km s^ 1 . Voit2015b showed that this galaxy's atmosphere is plausibly precipitation-limited, and Voit2019 pointed out that its inner entropy profile slope is consistent with that of a pure cooling flow (i.e. _K = 1 ). At 0.1 kpc, its entropy level reaches 1 keV cm ^2 , considerably below the level expected from 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 of intermittent kinetic feedback power (see Voit2019 for details).

sources.summary: The paper confirms previous abundance-gradient results for NGC4636, describing decreasing Mg and Si abundances with radius and a nearly constant element-to-iron ratio across the galaxy.

quotes.quote: We analyzed nine X-ray bright Virgo early-type galaxies observed by both ASCA and ROSAT. Through spatially resolved spectroscopy, we determined the radial temperature profile and abundances of Mg, Si and Fe for six galaxies. The temperature profiles are consistent with isothermal temperatures outside of a cooler region at the galaxy center. We present new evidence for iron abundance gradients in NGC4472 and NGC4649 and confirm the previous results on NGC4636.

Abundance profiles of Mg, Si and Fe for NGC4472, NGC4636, NGC4649, NGC4261, NGC4374 and NGC4552 are presented in Figs. virgo-fe , virgo-si and virgo-mg and in Table table-ab . The central Fe abundance is less than 0.7 solar, when meaningfully constrained (this excludes NGC4365; see our abundance table definition). In most galaxies, the central iron abundance is higher than in the outskirts of the galaxy, where the abundance is always below 0.3 solar and in half the galaxies in this sample, below 0.1 of solar. Except for NGC4636 whose abundances decrease with radius, the radial abundance profiles of Mg and Si are consistent with being flat with averaged values of 0.5 solar for NGC4472 and lower for the rest of the galaxies.

The ratio of elements to iron is nearly constant with radius for NGC4261, NGC4374, NGC4552 and NGC4636, while this ratio increases with decreasing Fe abundance in NGC4472 and NGC4649. Fe abundance gradients, although clearly established in NGC4636, NGC4472, and NGC4649, may not be a common feature.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4636 using photometric data because significant rotation is absent; its triaxiality is poorly constrained, and its ellipticity variation is noted in the sample handling.

quotes.quote: NGC 1600, NGC 4374, NGC 4636 and NGC 4839. Because of the lack of any significant rotation in these galaxies, photometric data alone is used to estimate their shape likelihood distributions. The triaxialities of these four galaxies are therefore poorly constrained. They are included on the grounds that omitting them could bias our results away from strongly triaxial systems, most of which are probably slowly rotating. In each case, the data are averaged from the center out to the largest radius for which kinematic data is available (see Table tbl-1 ). The ellipticity of NGC 1600, NGC 4374 and NGC 4636 varies by about 0.10 in this interval, but the ellipticity of NGC 4839 rises steadily from 0.20 near the center to 0.50 at 32 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Chandra data to limit nuclear emission in NGC 4636, notes a possible near-nuclear X-ray source, and finds its observational limit consistent with the ADAF prediction.

quotes.quote: We investigate this extreme quiescence using Chandra X-ray Observatory observations of the giant elliptical galaxies NGC 1399, NGC 4472, and NGC 4636. The unique Chandra imaging power enables us to place upper limits of 7.3, 15 and 28 10^ 9 the Eddington luminosity for the 10^8-10^9 M _ black holes in NGC 1399, NGC 4472, and NGC 4636, respectively.

Only in the case of NGC 4636 is there a possible coincidence of the nucleus with a Chandra X-ray source (just to the southwest of the nuclear position indicated in Figure 1c), with a 0.3--7 keV count rate 0.004 cts s ^ 1 (see below).

The resulting predicted 2--10 keV luminosities, L_ ADAF , are 2 10^ 41 , 10^ 40 , and 10^ 36 ergs s ^ 1 for NGC 1399, NGC 4472, and NGC 4636, respectively (Table 1). Our observational upper limits are thus significantly below the ADAF predictions for NGC 1399 and NGC 4472, but consistent for NGC 4636.

sources.summary: NGC4636 is described as a nearby X-ray luminous elliptical with a hot gas corona and Chandra arm-like halo structures interpreted as shocks from nuclear outbursts.

quotes.quote: NGC4636 is one of the nearest and most X-ray luminous ``normal'' elliptical galaxies ( L_X 2 10^ 41 ergs s ^ 1 ). The first X-ray imaging observations of NGC4636 from Einstein showed that, like other luminous elliptical galaxies, NGC4636 was surrounded by an extensive hot gas corona . The Chandra observation of NGC4636 shows a new phenomenon -- shocks produced by nuclear outbursts (see Jones et al. for details of the Chandra observation of NGC4636 ). The high angular resolution Chandra image (see Fig. n4636_image ) shows symmetric, 8 kpc long, arm-like features in the X-ray halo surrounding NGC4636. The leading edges of these features are sharp and are accompanied by temperature increases of 30 , as expected from shocks propagating in a galaxy atmosphere.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4636 by fitting its globular-cluster content and inner-cored stellar light profile, then comparing the globular-cluster and galactic-light distributions.

quotes.quote: NGC 4636 The GC content of this galaxy has been studied by Kissler Patig et al. (1994). The modified core model fit has _0=77.66 arcmin ^ 2 , r_c=0.823 arcmin and =0.691 as optimal parameter values. The galactic light profile shows an inner flat distribution (a core with radius r_b 0.0573 arcmin (Lauer et al. 2007)), while for >r_b the light distribution is well fitted by the power law fit _s() ^ 1.5 (Kissler Patig et al. 1994).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC4636 as a Virgo galaxy with dust features, unusual X-ray morphology from recent AGN outbursts, and excess dust likely accreted in a recent merger.

quotes.quote: NGC4636 has dust features and an unusual X-ray morphology (Temi et al. \ 2007b). The origin of this morphology is thought to be recent outbursts from the central AGN (Jones et al. 2002). Temi et al. (2003) used ISO observations to show that the dust mass for NGC4636 is far in excess of that expected from stellar mass loss and proposed that the dust was accreted in a very recent merger with a dusty, gas-rich galaxy (similar to M86, Gomez et al. \ 2010). NGC4636 is part of the Virgo Cluster.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4636 as having knotty soft diffuse emission and embedded point sources, with optical broad H suggesting a possible heavily absorbed LLAGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 4636 (L1, E1/S0) has bright knotty, soft, diffuse emission. There are five point sources deeply embedded in the diffuse emission. One of them is at the center but this is not the brightest source and its spectrum is soft. This source did not have enough counts to justify fitting its spectrum with a variety of models, but its luminosity was evaluated to be L_ 0.5-10 keV =8.7 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The review treats NGC 4636 as an L* Virgo-outskirts elliptical with extensive GC velocities, a rich GCS, high X-ray luminosity, and group-scale dark halo evidence.

quotes.quote: The first large GC kinematics study of an L^* elliptical involves NGC 4636, at the Virgo outskirts. Here UT2+FORS2 has been used to obtain 437 GC velocities out to 45 kpc ( ; Y. Schuberth, this vol).

NGC 4636 is a typical L^* elliptical, except that its rich GCS and high X-ray luminosity suggest that it is at the center of a group halo. Its GCS kinematics imply a group-scale dark matter halo---but less massive than inferred from the (disturbed) X-ray-emitting gas .

sources.summary: NGC 4636 is described as a southern Virgo galaxy with a central radio core and two lobes, plus a highly disturbed X-ray morphology suggesting a past large AGN outburst.

quotes.quote: NGC 4636 NGC 4636 (also known as NGC 4624) lies in the southern regions of the Virgo cluster, some 2.6 from NGC 4486. The radio data show a central core and two clear lobes. The X-ray morphology is highly disturbed and suggests that a much larger AGN outburst has occurred in the past (see also Jones02, OSullivan05, Baldi09 ).

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4636's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4636 is one of the X-ray brightest elliptical galaxies in the Virgo cluster at a distance of D= 15.6 2.7 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median). It hosts a broad-line LINER ho_search_1997-1 . An X-ray point source consistent with the nuclear position is present, but appears very weakly and with a soft spectrum, which disfavours an AGN unless highly obscured gonzalez-martin_x-ray_2006,gonzalez-martin_fitting_2009,flohic_central_2006,baldi_unusual_2009 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4636 as a poor-group central early-type galaxy with compact weak jet-like radio emission and X-ray cavities indicating AGN interaction.

quotes.quote: NGC 4636 NGC 4636 is a central early-type galaxy within a poor group in the far outskirts of the Virgo Cluster. \ VLA A configuration images, as presented in Figure fig:chandra_vla_c , have been previously published and discussed by . The additional archival C configuration VLA data from 2017 revealed a more diffuse, but still quite compact and weak radio-jet-like emission with a total extent of 3.5 kpc.

sources.summary: The review cites NGC 4636 as a giant elliptical where resonant scattering was found, later detailed with low velocities and turbulent pressure, and illustrated with RGS spectra showing AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: Resonant scattering was found in the giant elliptical galaxy NGC 4636 . This galaxy was examined in more detail with four other systems , finding low ( <100 ) velocities in these systems and turbulent pressure support <5 .
Fig. fig:ngc4636_res shows RGS spectra from different regions NGC 4636 and an image demonstrating the strong effect of AGN feedback on the gas distribution .

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes Ginga observations of NGC 4636, treating it as a non-active elliptical whose earlier 2-10 keV spectra suggested a significant extended hard X-ray component.

quotes.quote: Three non-active ellipticals -- NGC 1399, NGC 4472, and NGC 4636 Ik92,Aw91 - were observed with the Ginga LAC that has a larger >2 keV collecting area. Initial (pre- ASCA / ROSAT ) studies fit the 2-10 keV spectra with one-component models, and the higher temperatures and 2-10 fluxes as compared to subsequent ASCA and ROSAT results are suggestive of the presence of a significant extended hard component. We re-analyzed the background-subtracted Ginga top-layer spectra for these galaxies, extracted from the LEDAS data base.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 4636 as an elliptical whose Chandra observations are consistent with a central dark-matter cusp, unusually high central dark-matter density, and an improved two-component dark-matter fit.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, a recent paper discussing Chandra observations of the elliptical galaxy NGC 4636 finds no discrepancy between the Chandra data and the existence of a central dark matter cusp. The averaged central dark matter density inferred for this galaxy is about two orders of magnitude larger than for dwarf spirals and low-surface brightness galaxies (where the core-cusp problem seems most notable). Also intriguing, their fit significantly improved when using a two-component dark matter model rather than just a single component.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4636 as a close comparison system, noting similar temperature structure, resonant scattering measurements, and a low cutoff temperature in cool surrounding gas.

quotes.quote: Xu et al. ( ) detected resonant scattering effects in the 15.0 \ line, from the RGS observations of the core of the elliptical galaxy NGC 4636. This galaxy has a temperature structure similar to that of NGC 5044.

Among these, our result along with that of NGC 4636 by Xu et al.( ) is unique because 1) the quality of the observed spectra is among the best, 2) the observed T_ cut value is significantly lower than for other sources studied, and 3) the galaxy is surrounded by a relatively cooler (and lower pressure) intracluster medium (ICM) than in other cases.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC4636 initially stays below its X-ray limit, notes a spiral arm in the core, and later finds the model slightly exceeds the limit.

quotes.quote: Three limitation values in case of NGC821, NGC1399, NGC4636 are not exceeded.
In NGC4636 we see 'spiral arm in the core'.
In case of NGC 4636 is also exceeded, but it is very close to its limit.

gmw_00000221.name: NGC 4698
gmw_00000221.overview: NGC 4698 is an early-type spiral galaxy in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:1310.2770, arXiv:1511.05416] that hosts a low-luminosity Seyfert 2 or LINER nucleus [arXiv:1309.5908, arXiv:1603.08837]. It features a peculiar, slightly triaxial bulge and a nuclear stellar disk that rotate almost perpendicularly to its main disk [arXiv:1502.00265, arXiv:1511.05416].

sources.summary: The paper portrays NGC4698 as a Virgo Sa with a classical bulge, low-surface-brightness disk, offset UV rings, and perpendicular bulge and disk rotation axes suggesting later disk acquisition.

quotes.quote: NGC4698 is a bulge-dominated Sa galaxy in the Virgo E cloud. The Carnegie Atlas sandage94 describes it as an elliptical-like bulge in which there is no evidence of recent star formation or spiral structure, plus a low-surface-brightness disk with spiral arms become prominent only in the outer part of the disk. HST observations reveal that the bulge is a `classical bulge' drory07 , consistent with a major merger/accretion event in the past. Once again, UV images reveal that star formation is segregated into two `almost perfect' rings: one within and the other outside the optical radius. Interestingly, the two rings are not concentric: the inner ring is centered on the nucleus, whereas the outer one is offset 20 arcsec to the north.

The peculiarity of NGC4698 becomes more evident when the dynamical properties of the bulge and disk component are compared. showed that the rotation axis of the disk and of the bulge are almost perpendicular. They interpret this unusual geometry as an evidence of a later formation of the disk through the acquisition of material by a completely formed spheroid.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 4698 as a double-stage spiral/Seyfert system with an E-like bulge, tightly wound arms, two outer rings, and patchier H-alpha than UV emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 4698 This galaxy was classified in the RC3 as SA(s)ab. R. J. Buta et al. 2013 (in prep) classify the galaxy as (R)SA(rr)0/a/E2, a double-stage spiral, described by Vorontsov-Velyaminov1987 as cases where an inner spiral pattern gives a different type than the outer spiral pattern. In this case, we find a E-like bulge where the spiral arms arise.

This galaxy presents two outer rings visible in both the UV and H . There is a third not-so-well-defined ring in between the other two, which can hardly be seen in H (panel g in Fig. fig1 ) but which can be recognised in the UV image (panel i in Fig. fig1 ). Here, as in NGC 160, the H emission is more patchy than the UV emission. NGC 4698 is a low-luminosity Seyfert 2 galaxy ( Ho1995 ), and the nuclear emission is therefore strong.

sources.summary: The paper detects NGC 4698 as an unresolved, marginal L-band radio source and finds an inverted radio spectral index from its multi-frequency spectrum.

quotes.quote: We detected radio emission in six out of ten sources: NGC 3185, NGC 3941, NGC 4477, NGC 4639, NGC 4698 and NGC 4725. Images of the detected sources (contours and coloured maps) are shown in Figs fig:contourMaps3185 fig:contourMaps4725 . A source is considered detected if the peak intensity is 3 . However, a source exhibiting a peak intensity 3 S_ P < 6 is considered as marginal detection, following the same criterion as in HU01 . Following this criterion, the detection rates are 5/10 in the L-band (with NGC 4477 and NGC 4698 as marginal detections), 6/10 in the C-band (with NGC 3941 as marginal detection), 5/10 in the X band (with NGC 3185 as marginal detection) and 4/10 in Ku band. As can be seen from images shown in Figs fig:contourMaps3185 fig:contourMaps4725 , four out of six sources (NGC 4477, NGC 4639, NGC 4698 and NGC 4725) are are not resolved in our VLA observations, and for them we could only set an upper limit to their sizes which correspond to half of the beam size, in analogy with HU01 .

We have built radio spectra for the sources which have been detected in two or more frequencies: NGC 3185, NGC 3941, NGC 4477, NGC 4639, NGC 4698 and NGC 4725 (Fig. fig:radiospectra upper limits are represented as arrows), considering all the available data points. We performed a weighted linear least squares fit using the Sci-Py scipy routine curve fit , weighting for the uncertainties in the flux densities. In three cases, NGC 4477, NGC 4639 and NGC 4698, the targets were found to fit a typical power-law S_ ^ I ^ to the spectrum We define a steep spectrum as one having 0.5, and a flat one as 0.5, following PG13 , with S_ ^ . We also define an inverted spectrum as one having 0 . For NGC 4639 we considered only the 5.5, 9 and 14 GHz data points, as the 1.5 GHz data point seems to deviate with respect to the power-law behaviour, being probably associated with a different emitting component which dominates at lower frequencies. We found an inverted spectral index for NGC 4639 and NGC 4698 of = 0.40 0.01 and = 0.13 0.07, respectively, and a steep spectral index of = +0.52 0.09 for NGC 4477.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4698 as a Milky Way analog with an HI short-tail signature suggesting minor merger history, LINER activity, and elevated dark matter halo mass.

quotes.quote: We note that NGC 4579 and NGC 4698, both well within the selection criteria that resulted in our MW analog sample, have some peculiarities that are worth mentioning. In the case of NGC 4579, the HI extension is quite limited, not reaching R _ 25 . This is most likely due to the moderately HI-deficiency ( def_ HI 1 ), as confirmed by 2009AJ....138.1741C . For MW analog NGC 4698, a short tail signature in the HI (see Figure  fig:plot_Barolo ) may indicate a recent history of a minor merger, also suggested by 2009AJ....138.1741C . We also note that these galaxies have been identified as hosting AGN activity, both of them being classified as LINER galaxies 2000ApJ...532..323P,2003ApJ...594..704G . However, given that these galaxies fit well within our sample selection criteria ( subsec:sample criteria ) and that their RCs, DM halo masses, nor concentration parameters stand as outliers, we do not exclude them from our study.

However, our most HI-poor galaxies (NGC 4579 and NGC 4698) indeed exhibit higher DM halo masses compared to the rest of the sample. The confirmed presence of an AGN in these two galaxies e.g., 2000ApJ...532..323P,2003ApJ...594..704G could explain this finding, with the AGN impacting the HI content e.g., 1998A&A...331L...1S, 2006MNRAS.365...11C .

sources.summary: The paper portrays NGC 4698 as an early-to-intermediate Sa with a low-luminosity Seyfert 2 nucleus, a perpendicular kinematically decoupled central disc, little gas, and old populations.

quotes.quote: NGC 4698 might be considered the example of the early-to-intermediate Sa galaxy (Sandage Bedke 1994). It contains a low-luminosity Seyfert 2 nucleus (Ho et al. 1997). The stellar kinematics is rather unusual (Paper VII), for example because of the presence of a kinematically decoupled central disc, in which the stars rotate perpendicularly to the stars elsewhere in the central regions ( Pizzella et al. 2002, Sarzi et al. 2000). The galaxy has little ionised gas, and its stellar populations from the SAURON absorption lines are predominantly old. A stellar population analysis from STIS spectra also shows that the central stellar populations are old.

sources.summary: The review highlights NGC 4698 as a spiral with a polar bulge and aligned polar nuclear stellar disc, finding a slightly triaxial vertically elongated bulge.

quotes.quote: In fact, to date NGC 4698 bertola99 , NGC 4672 sarzi00 , and UGC 10043 matthewsdegrijs04 are the only spiral galaxies known to host a prominent bulge sticking out from the plane of the disc. The case of NGC 4698 is particularly intriguing since it hosts also a polar nuclear stellar disc aligned with its polar bulge and thus perpendicular to the main disc. This galaxy was recently revisited by corsini12 and its intrinsic shape was derived using the methodology proposed by mendezabreu10 . They found a slightly triaxial polar bulge elongated along the vertical direction with axis ratios Q = 0.95 and F = 1.60. This result agrees well with the observed kinematics presented in bertola99 and with a model where the nuclear disc is the end result of the acquisition of external gas by the pre-existing triaxial bulge on the principal plane perpendicular to its shortest axis and perpendicular to the main disc of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4698 as a type 2 Seyfert/LINER-classified galaxy with radio counterpart, atypical Seyfert properties, nearby point sources, and no spectral variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 4698 This galaxy was optically classified as a type 2 Seyfert see their spectra in Appendix multiimages ho1997 . classified it as a LINER, but re-confirmed the type 2 Seyfert classification using optical observations with the NOT /ALFOSC/Gr7. A radio counterpart was found by at 6 cm with VLA data. stated that this is an atypical Seyfert 2 galaxy because it showed no absoption and lacks the broad line region. The Chandra image revealed point-like sources around the nucleus which can be ultraluminous X-ray sources (ULX), the closest located at 30 from the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper reports a weak broad H component in NGC 4698, improving the fit despite previous non-detections and yielding ambiguous line-ratio classification.

quotes.quote: For three galaxies (NGC 4374, NGC 4552 and NGC 4698; Fig. Panel_NGC4374 , Panel_NGC4552 , Panel_NGC4698 , respectively) we derive a very broad component ( > 1300 km s ^ 1 ) that C15 do not report in their analysis. According to our modeling, the fit is improved in the three galaxies by adding a very broad H component to the H [ N II ] complex.

More specifically, we have found that, even though the [ S II ] lines are perfectly modeled with a single narrow component, the addition of a weak broad component improves the ^ 2 of the fit a 17 (see Fig. Panel_NGC4698 ). The lack of detection of this component in previous works could be due to its low contribution to the global fit (39 , Table T_kin ) flux in comparison with the narrow component (see Sect. C15 ).
For the case of NGC 4698 line ratios correspond to the transition region in both diagrams for the HST data, so this results in an ambiguous classification.

sources.summary: The atlas classifies NGC 4698 as Core-halo and describes perpendicular nuclear gas and stellar rotation, a likely ancient-merger origin, and non-planar ionized-gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 4698 (see Fig. Figure_AB7 ): Our H image has a morphology that we classified as Core-halo . included this object in their sample, where they also obtained H imaging with ALFOSC/NOT. They were only interested in the double-ring structure seen at large scales, hence the comparison of the nuclear region with the published H image is not possible. reported that the nuclear disc of gas and stars are rotating perpendicularly with respect to the galaxy main disc, most probably as a result of acquisition of external gas Corsini2012 . spatially studied resolved stellar and ionised gas kinematics, and suggested that this Virgo cluster galaxy is the product of an ancient merger.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4698 as a bright nearby Seyfert 2 whose close ULX candidate was identified as a rare pure absorption-line BL Lac quasar.

quotes.quote: NGC 4698: The central galaxy here is a bright nearby Seyfert 2 galaxy. Foschini et al. (2002c) identified a ULX within only 73 arcsec of the nucleus of the galaxy. If it was an object at the same distance as the galaxy it would have a luminosity of L_X = 39.5 erg s ^ 1 . This ULX, however, turned out to be a quasar of z = 0.43. In fact it turned out to be a pure absorption line BL Lac quasar which is a very rare type of object.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4698 X-ray data are dominated by off-nuclear emission, leaving its possible Compton-thick AGN highly uncertain and unconstrained by MIR-X-ray diagnostics.

quotes.quote: NGC 4698 According to , the data of NGC 4698 are dominated by off nuclear emission (but see cappi_x-ray_2006 ). The low X-ray-- ratio based on observations indicates that the AGN might be Compton-thick obscured according to . Therefore, is highly uncertain, and NGC 4698 is excluded from the reliable sample. The MIR--X-ray correlation diagnostic does not constrain the nature of the AGN in this object.

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 4698 as a misaligned kinematically decoupled component case whose inner twist is thought to reflect an intermediate merger event.

quotes.quote: However, there are a few cases in our sample where the rotation axis of the decoupled component is misaligned with respect to the main rotation axis of the galaxy (e.g., NGC 4698, see Figure 1 bottom panel). In this case the decoupled component can be identified as a twist of the zero velocity curve in the inner regions.

This model is preferred to explain the presence of misaligned components such as the one seeing in NGC 4698. This galaxy is a well-studied case in which the formation of the component is thought to be the result of an intermediate merger event (Bertola et al. 1999).

sources.summary: NGC 4698 is presented as a low-luminosity Seyfert 2 possibly shaped by a merger, with regular minor-axis rotation and inner parts rotating perpendicular to the main body.

quotes.quote: NGC 4698 is a low-luminosity Seyfert 2 galaxy ho95 that is thought to have experienced a major or intermediate-mass merger ( bertola99 , but see also sarzi00 ). The \ stellar velocity field displays regular rotation around the minor axis. The inner parts however (i.e. 5 ) appear to be rotating perpendicularly to the main body of the galaxy, consistent with a nuclear disc in our unsharp-masked image see also pizzella02 . We note, however, that no clear evidence for such decoupled inner component is found in either the velocity dispersion or h_3 maps.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4698's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4698 is an inclined early-type spiral galaxy in the Virgo Cluster at a distance of D= 24.4 6.7 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) that hosts a Sy 2.0 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . The AGN is considered a ``true''-Seyfert 2 candidate ( pappa_x-ray_2001,georgantopoulos_chandra_2003 ; but see shi_unobscured_2010 ). However, the hard X-ray point source in its centre might in fact be Compton-thick gonzalez-martin_fitting_2009 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC4698 as an Sb-Sc spiral with high fitted bulge index, large bulge-to-disc size ratio, and a peculiar bulge elongated perpendicular to the disc.

quotes.quote: The five Sb--Sc galaxies with n_ b >4 are NGC5430, NGC2841, NGC3521, NGC3642 and NGC4698, with n_ b = 4.2 , 5.7 , 6.7 , 6.8 and 7.1 , respectively.
Consequently, in Fig fig:r_montage we find seven galaxies black: IC3653 and NGC4458; red: NGC4452; green: NGC3521, NGC3642, NGC3893 and NGC4698. with r_ e,b /r_ e,d >1 .
NGC4698 SA(s)ab A spiral galaxy with peculiar structure: the bulge is elongated perpendicular to the main disc.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4698 as one of the rare known polar-bulge galaxies, with a morphologically and kinematically decoupled bulge, and notes its Virgo-cluster environment.

quotes.quote: The polar bulges are extremely rare. In a recent review devoted to the shape of galactic bulges, M endez-Abreu (2015) mentions only three such galaxies: NGC 4698 (Bertola et al. 1999; Corsini et al. 2012), NGC 4672 (Sarzi et al. 2000), and UGC 10043 (Matthews and de Grijs 2004). One of the reasons for the small number of known polar bulges is that they can be identified only in the case where the galactic disk is close to the edge-on orientation to the line of sight and therefore, the bulge inclination to the disk plane can be easily detected. If this is not the case, then the bulge looks like a bar and does not attract the attention of researchers. Given the small number of known objects, the systematics of properties and the origin of the polar bulges remain puzzles.

In two of the three galaxies (NGC 4698 and NGC 4672) the morphologically decoupled bulges are known to be related to the kinematically decoupled subsystems in their cores that rotate almost orthogonally to the galactic disks (Bertola et al. 1999; Sarzi et al. 2000).

UGC 10043 ( 7 in Table 1) is a member of a group, and it interacts with the galaxy MCG+04-37-035 (Aguirre et al. 2009). Two other galaxies (NGC 4672 and NGC 4698; see Introduction) are not isolated either. NGC 4672 is a member of a group of galaxies (Garcia 1993); NGC 4698 is a member of the Virgo cluster of galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4698 as an anomalous Seyfert 2 whose X-ray luminosity and Eddington ratio resemble LINERs, while its optical classification is disputed and it is non-variable.

quotes.quote: Note that only one Seyfert 2, namely NGC 4698, appears in luminosity and R_ Edd with a typical value of what it is found for LINERs. The optical classification of this object has been controversial in the current literature, being classified as a Seyfert 2 by and , but also classified as a LINER by . However, since it is not a variable source, its presence in any of the two families does not change our conclusions concerning the variability patterns.

gmw_00000222.name: NGC 4746
gmw_00000222.overview: NGC 4746 is an edge-on spiral galaxy located at a distance of approximately 33.5 Mpc [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a little-studied, possibly active nucleus that has been classified as a borderline LINER or H II region [arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4746's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4746 is an edge-on spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 33.5 5.5 Mpc (NED redshift-independent) with a little-studied possibly active nucleus, which has been classified as a borderline LINER/H II decarli_census_2007 . No pointed X -ray observations are published and no compact source was detected with subarcsecond-resolution radio observations irwin_high-resolution_2000 . We conservatively treat NGC 4746 as an uncertain AGN.

gmw_00000223.name: NGC 4785
gmw_00000223.overview: NGC 4785 is an inclined barred spiral galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z=0.0123 [arXiv:1310.2770]. It hosts a little-studied Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1408.4453, arXiv:1310.2770] that has been identified as a heavily obscured, Compton-thick source [arXiv:1408.4453].

sources.summary: The paper presents first pointed X-ray observations of NGC 4785, identifying its Seyfert nucleus as a bona fide Compton-thick AGN with strong Fe K emission and heavy obscuration.

quotes.quote: We present X-ray observations of the active galactic nucleus (AGN) in . The source is a local Seyfert 2 which has not been studied so far in much detail. It was recently detected with high significance in the 15--60 keV band in the 66 month /BAT all sky survey, but there have been no prior pointed X-ray observations of this object.

With , we clearly detect the source below 10 keV, and find it to have a flat continuum and prominent neutral iron fluorescence line with equivalent width 1 keV. Fitting the broadband spectra with physical reflection models shows the source to be a bona fide Compton thick AGN with \ of at least 2 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 and absorption-corrected 2--10 keV X-ray power L_ 2-10 few times 10 ^ 42 erg s ^ 1 .

We have presented the first pointed X-ray observations of . The source has long been known as a relatively nearby Sy 2, but little is known of the intrinsic AGN properties. With , in combination with /BAT, we show the source to be a bona fide CTAGN.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4785's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4785 is an inclined barred spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0123 ( D 59 Mpc) with a very little-studied Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . No high-angular resolution X-ray and radio observations have been published. It was not detected with HEAO1 polletta_multiwavelength_1996 .

gmw_00000224.name: NGC 4941
gmw_00000224.overview: NGC 4941 is an inclined barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1310.2770] classified as a Seyfert 2 [arXiv:2111.13508, arXiv:1310.2770] with a heavily obscured active nucleus [arXiv:2111.13508]. The galaxy hosts a nuclear bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0505407], an extended optical narrow-line region [arXiv:2107.00244], and a compact double radio source [arXiv:1310.2770].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4941 as a Seyfert 2 low-luminosity AGN, finding strong X-ray obscuration, similar hard/soft variability, and a heavily obscured nucleus.

quotes.quote: We present the results obtained from timing and spectral studies of the highly obscured low luminosity active galactic nucleus NGC 4941 using data obtained from the Nuclear Spectroscopic Telescope Array and the Neil Gehrels Swift Observatories. We find similar variability in 3-10 keV and 10-60 keV energy ranges with fractional rms variability of 14 . We investigate broad-band spectral properties of the source in 3-150 keV range, using data from NuSTAR and Swift /BAT, with phenomenological slab model and physically motivated mytorus model. From the spectral analysis, we find heavy obscuration with global average column density of the obscured material as 3.09^ +1.68 _ 1.01 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 .

NGC 4941 is classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy (Veron-cetty et al. 2006) with a morphological class of Sa (Fisher et al. 2008). Over the past years, NGC 4941 was observed with several X-ray observatories, such as ASCA (Terashima et al. 2002), BeppoSAX (Maiolino et al. 1998), Chandra (Bottacini et al. 2012), Suzaku (Kawamuro et al. 2013) and NuSTAR (Georgantopoulos et al. 2019, Garcia-Burillo et al. 2021). The ASCA , BeppoSAX , Suzaku and NuSTAR observations revealed a heavily obscured nucleus with line of sight column density N_ H >10^ 23 cm ^ 2 and a strong Fe K emission line in the spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4941 as a spiral with centrally confined anomalous gas kinematics, high inner gas velocity dispersion, and a nuclear bar.

quotes.quote: NGC 4941 has been found by us in a galaxy sample we studied not for this purpose.

The non-zero gas velocities are confined in the central regions along the minor axis of NGC 4424 and NGC 4941, where the gas velocity curve shows a steep gradient. The gas velocity increases to a maximum of 10 \ in the inner few arcsec, and further out it decreases to zero. Our measurements along the minor axis of NGC 4424 are in agreement within errors with those by Kenney et al. (1996) which are characterized by a lower spatial sampling. A zero velocity gradient is observed for the ionized-gas component along the minor axis of NGC 4178. In all the galaxies the gas velocity dispersion remains low ( 50 ), except for NGC 4941 where it reaches 100 \ in the inner 5'' .

Moreover NGC 4941 hosts a nuclear bar, which is almost aligned with the galaxy major axis ( PA 15^ ) and confined into the region where the kinematic peculiarities are measured (Greusard et al. 2000). Therefore the observed kinematics is consistent with the presence of gas moving onto anomalous orbits in the tumbling potential of the nuclear bar. This is also supported by the presence of gas in retrograde motion relative to the stars along the disk minor axis (i.e., in a direction close to the bar minor axis), where the anomalous orbits are expected to be highly inclined with respect to the rotation axis of the bar (see Friedli Benz 1993 for details).

sources.summary: The paper reports no extended mid-IR emission in NGC 4941 despite an optical NLR, and places it at low column density and Eddington ratio where dusty winds are not launched.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies, NGC 6814, NGC 7314, and NGC 7465, only show evidence of faint extended emission at 3-5 _ bk levels. Finally, the PSF-subtracted images of NGC 4941 and NGC 7213 do not show evidence of extended emission.

NGC 4941, NGC 7465 and NGC 7314 have extended optical NLRs, which are detected with optical IFU observations and/or HST imaging see and Sousa et al. in preparation, respectively ErrozFerrer2019, Ferruit2000 . In NGC 6814, the NLR and the coronal line region are rather compact and there is evidence for ionized outflows on these scales 1 , see MuellerSanchez2011 . In these four galaxies we do not detect extended mid-IR emission.

Finally the low nuclear column density and Eddington ratio of NGC 4941 amd NGC 7213 place these galaxies in a region in Fig. fig:dustywinds where dusty winds are not launched. This is in agreement with the unresolved mid-IR morphology.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4941 as a source whose LECS and marginal PDS data favor a transmission model, though the nature of its spectrum remains uncertain.

quotes.quote: NGC 4941 The MECS data of NGC 4941 were also presented in Salvati et al. ( ). In this paper we include the LECS data and a marginal PDS detection in the 30--60 keV range. However, the latter datum was not used during the fitting procedure because of its low statistical significance (though it is plotted in Fig. fig_spectra_b ). The transmission model (N _H =4.5 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ) provides the best fit to the data above 3 keV. The Compton thick warm scattering model is ruled out at a high confidence level, and the Compton thick, cold reflection model is also worse than the transmission model (though at a lower confidence level: above 3 keV ^2 =4 with only one degree of freedom less). The EW(Fe _ 6.4keV ) is high, but consistent with 300 eV at the 90 level once the effects of absorption are taken into account. Summarizing, although the transmission scenario is preferred, we consider the nature of this spectrum still uncertain.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 4941's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 4941 is an inclined barred spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 21.2 6.7 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with a Sy 2.0 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . At radio wavelengths, a compact double source was detected, which in total extends for 0.5 50 pc along a PA 25 schmitt_jet_2001 . The emission is compact with a weak extended halo pogge_circumnuclear_1989 .

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4941 is reproduced by the baseline plus a thermal component, differs from earlier modeling by omitting disk reflection, and gives consistent parameters.

quotes.quote: The base-line model plus an apec model well reproduces the spectra with ^2/d.o.f = 33.1/43 . The broadband X-ray spectra were already analyzed by , and the difference from the previously used model is the absence of the disk-reflection component. The obtained parameters are consistent with each other.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4941 has a rotating stellar velocity field, a maximum-amplitude radius beyond the observed 200 pc region, and a mostly flat central dispersion map.

quotes.quote: NGC 4941 A rotation pattern is again observed in the stellar kinematics (central right panel of Fig. painel-4941 ). The rotation curve (top right panel of Fig. painel-4941 ) indicates that the maximum amplitude is located beyond the observed region, which corresponds to a radial distance from the nucleus of only 200 pc for this galaxy. The velocity dispersion map (bottom left panel of Fig. painel-4941 ) is almost flat within 100 pc from the nucleus, with central values 135 km s ^ 1 , decreasing to 100 km s ^ 1 at the edges of the field ( 200 pc).

gmw_00000225.name: NGC 4945
gmw_00000225.overview: NGC 4945 is a nearby, edge-on barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1808.05617, arXiv:2110.09690] that hosts a coexisting starburst and Type II Seyfert nucleus [arXiv:1808.05617, arXiv:1811.07935]. This highly obscured, Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0304122, arXiv:1008.5164] drives molecular outflows [arXiv:2109.10437] and exhibits water megamaser emission [arXiv:1608.00258, arXiv:1808.03024].

sources.summary: The paper reports H91 and H92 radio recombination line emission from NGC 4945, models its nuclear ionized gas and star formation, and finds a flat central rotation curve.

quotes.quote: We searched for the RRLs H91 and H92 with rest frequencies of 8.6 GHz and 8.3 GHz in the nearby southern Seyfert galaxy NGC 4945 using the Australia Telescope Compact Array with resolution of 3'' . This yielded a detection from which we derived conditions in the starburst regions.

We detected RRLs from the nucleus of NGC 4945 with a peak line strength integrated over the source of 17.8 mJy, making it the strongest extragalactic RRL emitter known at this frequency. The line and continuum emission from NGC 4945 can be matched by a model consisting of a collection of 10 to 300 H II regions with temperatures of 5 000 K, densities of 10^3 cm ^ 3 to 10^4 cm ^ 3 and a total effective diameter of 2 pc to 100 pc. The Lyman continuum production rate required to maintain the ionization is 6 10^ 52 s ^ 1 to 3 10^ 53 s ^ 1 , which requires 2 000 to 10 000 O5 stars to be produced in the starburst, inferring a star formation rate of 2 M_ yr ^ 1 to 8 M_ yr ^ 1 .

We resolved the rotation curve within the central 70 pc region and this is well described by a set of rotating rings that were coplanar and edge on. We found no reason to depart from a simple flat rotation curve. The rotation speed of 120 km s ^ 1 within the central 1'' (19 pc) radius infers an enclosed mass of 3 10^7 M_ , and an average surface density with the central 19 pc of 25 000 pc ^ 2 , which exceeds the threshold gas surface density for star formation.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4945 as a Seyfert 2/starburst system and Compton-thick AGN, reporting a significant Fermi/LAT detection with a power-law GeV spectrum.

quotes.quote: NGC 4945 is also a Seyfert 2 galaxy at z=0.001908 exhibiting starburst activity in its central region 1993ApJ...409..155I,1994ApJ...429..602M . Its emission extends up to soft rays 2009A+A...507..549P , as observed by INTEGRAL /SPI. It is one of the brightest hard X-ray AGN see and references therein, and Ricci et al., in prep. 2008PASJ...60S.251I . NGC 4945 was found to be a Compton thick AGN, based on GINGA 1993ApJ...409..155I and INTEGRAL observations 2009A+A...505..417B .

We followed the same procedure for the analysis of Fermi /LAT data in the region of NGC 4945, for the source 1FGL J1305.4 4928. The likelihood analysis on NGC 4945 results in a TS of 85.3 in the 100 MeV--100 GeV band, equivalent to a 9.2 detection. Assuming a power-law shape on the source energy spectrum, a photon index of = 2.31 0.10 and a flux of F_ 100 MeV-100 GeV = \ are found. The highest energy photon detected is 20.7 GeV, located at 0 22 from NGC 4945.

sources.summary: The paper detects Fermi/LAT emission from NGC 4945, models it with a power-law spectrum, and uses its broadband SED to support a starburst origin.

quotes.quote: NGC 4945, another Seyfert 2 galaxy at z=0.001908 , also exhibits starburst activity within its core. Its emission was detected up to soft rays, as observed with INTEGRAL /SPI, and was found to be Compton thick . This source was reported as a ray emitter in the 1FGL catalogue, although the authors did not conclude on the starburst or AGN origin of the emission.

The likelihood analysis on NGC 4945 results in a TS of 85.3 in the 100 MeV--100 GeV band, equivalent to a 9.2 detection. The nominal position of the ray emission, located at _ J2000 = 13^ h 05^ m 33^ s , _ J2000 = -49^ 26' 44'' with an error circle radius of 3.2' (68 CL), is only 1.6' away from the position of NGC 4945. Assuming a power-law shape on the source energy spectrum, a photon index of = 2.31 0.10 and a flux of F_ 100 MeV-100 GeV = \ are found.

Figure fig-NGC4945_SED shows the SED of NGC 4945 including archival data, as well as the and INTEGRAL spectra. We also show for comparison the SED of the starburst galaxy NGC 253. The two sources clearly have very similar broadband SEDs, strengthening the idea that the high energy ray emission from NGC 4945 is of starburst origin.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4945 as a nearby Sc galaxy with the coldest dust in the sample, compatible free-free and synchrotron SFRs, and possible 30 GHz AME.

quotes.quote: NGC 4945 NGC 4945 ( 13^ h 05^ m 27.5^ s , -49^ 28^ m 06^ s J2000.0), also known as G305.27+13.34, is an Sc galaxy at a distance of 3.6 Mpc 2002Karachentsev with optical dimensions of 19.9 3.8 arcmin 1991deVaucouleurs and observed 857 GHz dimensions of 6.82 3.98 arcmin (partially resolved).

We find a free-free EM of NGC4945:freefree cm ^ 6 pc with a synchrotron index of = NGC4945:alphasync , once more steeper than lower frequencies (without considering free-free, the best fit synchrotron index is still steep at = -0.95 0.12 ). The dust is the coldest of the three galaxies, with T_ dust = NGC4945:Tdust K and = NGC4945:Betadust . Fixing =1.65 results in T_ dust = NGC4945:Tdustfixed K, however this significantly over-predicts the emission at 143 GHz, requiring no contribution to the spectra from free-free emission and hence a much flatter synchrotron index that does not fit these low frequency data. The calculated SFR based on the non-thermal flux density at 1.4 GHz is 2.7 ; using the free-free model fitted here returns a thermal SFR of 2.9 , i.e. consistent within the uncertainty.

For NGC 4945, the expected 30 GHz AME based on the 100 m flux density would be at the level of 0.4 Jy. Between 23 and 94 GHz, the weighted mean of the residuals is NGC4945:residuals Jy, giving a 3 upper limit of NGC4945:residuals:3sig Jy. However, the 23-41 GHz measurements of NGC 4945 suggest the possibility of a 30 GHz-peaked curve (the high 60.6 GHz data point from is likely due to CMB contamination: the entry in the CMB-subtracted catalogue is 0.5 0.2 Jy compared with 1.0 0.2 Jy from the main catalogue).

sources.summary: The GHOSTS analysis treats NGC 4945 as one of the nearest sample galaxies, measuring median RGB halo colours per HST field and finding a rather flat minor-axis stellar-halo colour profile.

quotes.quote: For the three nearest galaxies (NGC 0253, NGC 3031, and NGC 4945) we obtain a median colour measurement per HST field. These galaxies are at a distance of 4 Mpc and the size of their HST fields extends over roughly the same linear distance, covering 3.5 and 2.8 kpc on a side of the ACS and WFC3 field of view (FoV), respectively.

We assume that the stars observed along the minor axis fields located at galactocentric distances R> 5 kpc for the edge-on galaxies (NGC 0253, NGC 0891, NGC 4565, NGC 4945) and R > 10 kpc for the highly inclined galaxies (NGC 3031, NGC 7814) represent halo stellar populations. Figure fig:colprof_minmaj shows the minor axis stellar halo colour profile of each galaxy. In order to give a rough quantitative estimate of the magnitude of colour variation with radius, we fit a linear colour gradient to the data. Such a function has no particular physical relevance or motivation, and a variety of radial profile shapes are predicted by models e.g. Cooper10, Font11, Tissera14 . Other parameterizations are possible, but additional complexity seems unwarranted given the number of data points and their uncertainties.

Half of the galaxies (NGC 4565, NGC 0891, and NGC 7814) show fits consistent with stellar halo colour gradients whereas the remaining three galaxies (NGC 0253, NGC 3031, and NGC 4945) have rather flat colour profile.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4945 as a gamma-ray starburst whose data are consistent with calorimetry, though lack of TeV measurements leaves its cosmic-ray parameters uncertain.

quotes.quote: We now apply our model to five individual starbursts NGC 253, M82, NGC 4945, NGC 1068, and the Circinus galaxy, as well as the ULIRG Arp 220. The input parameters and best-fit results are listed in Table tab:parameter and Table tab:result . For each galaxy we adopt an observed star-formation rate (SFR), and then calculate the pionic flux E_ ^2dN_ /dE_ dAdt for each point in ( _ cr ,s) space.

From Table tab:result , we can see that the pionic gamma-ray luminosity calculated from our model agrees well with the phenomenological Fermi fits for the starburst galaxies M82, NGC 253, NGC 4945, NGC 1068 the Circinus galaxy, and ULIRG Arp 220. The best-fit pionic gamma-ray spectra can be seen in Figs. fig:spec-NGC253 through fig:spec-stb_2 . In left panels, the solid lines is our model's calculated differential spectral energy distribution of the five starburst galaxies with the best-fit parameters s and _ cr .

For M82 and NGC 253, we see that our best fit to GeV and TeV data is quite good and fairly well constrained thanks to the relatively large energy range. For NGC 1068, NGC 4945, Circinus and Arp 220, only GeV data is available and even our simple model is poorly constrained.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4945 as a nearby barred spiral starburst with an AGN nucleus and reports narrow, luminous 36.2 GHz methanol maser emission detected toward it.

quotes.quote: Here we report a search for the class I 36.2 GHz and class II 37.7 GHz transition towards the central region of NGC 4945. This nearby assumed distance of 3.7 0.3 Mpc; Tully+13 galaxy is classified as a barred spiral and starburst type. In addition to being classified as starburst, the nucleus of NGC 4945 is also classified as an AGN, and is the strongest source of hard x-rays observed from Earth Done&96 . Analysis of near- and far-infrared observations indicates that the AGN is heavily obscured, and that the starburst process is the primary source of energy for exciting photo-ionized gas Marconi+00, Spoon+00, Spoon+03, Perez-Beauputis+11 . NGC 4945 has a star-formation rate of 4.35 0.25 M _ yr ^ −1 Bendo+16 , almost a factor of 3 more than the 1.65 0.19 M _ yr ^ −1 observed in the Milky Way Licquia+15 .

For NGC 4945 the 36.2 GHz methanol emission is spread over a velocity range of 50 km s ^ 1 (from 660 to 710 km s ^ 1 ), dominated by a narrow (FWHM linewidth of 8 km s ^ 1 ) peak component at 674 km s ^ 1 . There also appears to be a secondary component redshifted by approximately 30 km s ^ 1 that is much weaker (factor of 4 dimmer), however, it shares a similar linewidth. Therefore, we observe similar emission characteristics in NGC 4945 as displayed by the 36.2 GHz emission in NGC 253 Ellingsen+14 .

sources.summary: The paper reports X-ray/gamma-ray correlation hints in NGC 4945 and argues that its gamma-ray emission is dominated by the active nucleus rather than starburst cosmic rays.

quotes.quote: We report hints for the correlation between the X-ray and ray emission in the nearby galaxy NGC 4945, which harbors both an active galactic nucleus and a nuclear starburst region. We have divided the Fermi /LAT observations of NGC 4945 into two datasets, comprising events detected during the low (L) and high (H) level of X-ray emission from the active nucleus of this galaxy, determined using the Swift /BAT light curve. Both datasets contain an equal amount of 3.8 years of LAT data and NGC 4945 is detected with a similar statistical significance of 15 in L and 14 in H. However, the slope of the ray spectrum hardens with the increase of the X-ray flux, with the photon index = 2.47 0.07 in L and 2.11 0.08 in H. The change is confirmed by a systematic variation of the spectral energy distribution as well as a substantial reversal of the ray signal in significance maps for low and high ray energies.

The X/ ray correlation indicates that the ray production is dominated by the active nucleus rather than by cosmic rays interacting with the interstellar medium. We discuss possible locations of the ray source. We also compare NGC 4945 with other starburst galaxies detected by LAT and we note similarities between those with active nuclei, e.g. \ unlikely high efficiencies of ray production in starburst scenario, which argues for a significant contribution of their active nuclei to the ray emission.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4945 rich in centimeter lines, with redshifted OH absorption suggesting nuclear inflow, complicated NH3 emission/absorption, likely NH3 maser activity, and anomalous absorption ratios.

quotes.quote: Discounting the RRLs in NGC 253, we detect the most lines in NGC 4945. In NGC 4945, the lines from different species are strikingly different from each other in morphology. The OH and C _3 H _2 lines are both detected solely in absorption, but with drastically different FWHMs of 55 km s ^ 1 and 220 km s ^ 1 , respectively.

The NH _3 in NGC 4945, detected in the (1,1) through (6,6) lines, shows a complicated superposition of emission and absorption. The profile is similar to that of several mm-wave tracers of dense gas. The (3,3) line displays the same general profile as the others, but superimposed on top of a much stronger emission base. This anomalous behavior is not seen in the other ortho-NH _3 (6,6) line, suggesting that spatially-extended maser activity may be the culprit.
Boltzmann diagrams created using the NH _3 absorption in NGC 4945 display depressed (1,1) and (4,4) lines and/or enhanced (2,2) and (5,5) lines.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4945 as a nearby edge-on Seyfert/starburst galaxy, testing cosmic-ray acceleration in its nuclear starburst and superwind regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 4945 is a nearly edge-on spiral galaxy classified as SB type and is located at distance of 3.82 0.31 Mpc 1990MNRAS.245..665W,2007AJ....133..504K . This galaxy is one of the brightest emitters in the infrared (IR) band, which originates due to the heating of dusty medium by ultraviolet (UV) and optical photons from the central SB activity. NGC 4945 has an obscured AGN/Seyfert type 2 nucleus, indicated by the variability of the order of hours, in hard X-ray emission 1993ApJ...409..155I .
In this paper, we investigate if the Seyfert/SBG NGC 4945 can produce the IceCube detected neutrino event IC35 via pp collisions in the nuclear SB region.

We analyzed if the nearby SBG NGC 4945 could be responsible for these observations. We considered two regions around this galaxy: the SB and the superwind or halo region. First, we estimated the maximum energy that CRs could reach in the nuclear SB and superwind regions. In the nuclear SB region, particles are accelerated by HN reaching energies of 100 Z PeV .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4945 has GeV spectral hardening beyond a one-zone model, weighs AGN versus star-forming origins, and says a two-zone hadronic SNR model explains it better.

quotes.quote: As can be seen in Figure Fig2 , we found that the one-zone model did not explain the hardening spectral components from NGC 1068 and NGC 4945. Therefore, we considered a two-zone model, which assumes that they have two kinds of different protonic compositions from the two different zones. All protons inside the two zones satisfy the ECPL energy distribution. The two zones contributed to the soft and hard spectral compositions in the 0.1 to 500 GeV band. As shown in Figure Fig3 , by fitting their SEDs, we found that this two-zone model can better explain the hardening components of the SEDs of NGC 1068 and NGC 4945 than the one-zone model.

The correlation of the X-ray and ray emission of NGC 4945 indicated that the ray emission was likely dominated by AGN Wojaczynski2017 . Moreover, the ray emission of NGC 1068 was believed to be likely from its AGN activity, considering that it exceeded the expectation of the star-forming process Lenain2010,Ackermann2012a,Eichmann2016 . However, The GeV flare is an important feature of AGN and has not been observed from NGC 4945 and NGC 1068 thus far Ackermann2012a,Peng2019,Ajello2020 . In addition, they well conformed to the two well-known luminosity relations; one is that of the total IR (8-1000 m) and the ray luminosities; the other is that of the ray and 1.4 GHz radio continuum luminosities. These results suggest that their ray emissions may originate from the star-forming process Yun2001,Thompson2007, Ackermann2012a, Guo2019, Xi2020, Ajello2020 . The previous research results provide strong evidence to support the hadronic origin of SNR for NGC 4945 and NGC 1068 in this analysis. For SNRs, Uchiyama2007 found the variable X-ray filaments (or knots) within the region of RX J1713.7-3946 with Suzaku and Chandra. These filaments themselves could contribute significantly to the ray emission from RX J1713.7-3946 Finke2012 . This result implies that the one-zone fit is inadequate to explain the overall SEDs of SNRs in the Milky Way. Therefore, Finke2012 considered a multi-zone scenario to well explain the multi-band SED of RX J1713.7-3946. Furthermore, Zhang2016 used a two-zone model to well fit the broadband spectrum of RX J1713.7-3946. In their model, the first zone was the cavity wall of SNR with the dense matter at the cavity boundary; the second zone was inside the SNR. Lu2020 considered a two-zone model with different diffusion processes from the extended region of SNR and the internal pulsar wind nebula region to explain the broadband SED of SNR G21.5-0.9. Similarly, Xiang2021b also used a simple two-zone model, irrespective of the acceleration process of CR particles, to well explain overall SED from composite SNR G327.1+1.1. If the GeV emissions of NGC 1068 and NGC 4945 are two-zone collective contributions from their internal SNRs, we found that the two-zone model can well explain the hardening phenomena of NGC 1068 and NGC 4945 at the end of their GeV spectra.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA CO observations of NGC 4945, identifying four molecular outflow plumes from its central starburst/AGN in a nearby edge-on galaxy.

quotes.quote: NGC 4945, located in the southern hemisphere, is one of the nearest massive galaxies 3.8 0.3 Mpc corresponding to a scale of 18.4 pc per arcsecond; Karachentsev2007 . The galaxy harbors a central starburst Bendo2016,Emig2020 as well as a Seyfert 2 Active Galactic Nucleus AGN, Schurch2002 . Similar to the nearby starburst galaxies M 82 and NGC 253, NGC 4945 is one of the brightest far-infrared sources outside the Local Group Sanders2003 . And like these two examples, it is one of the very few starbursts seen in gamma-ray emission by Fermi , although it is unclear if the emission is due to the star formation activity (and the ensuing supernovae) or to the central AGN Ackermann2012 . NGC 4945 is viewed almost edge-on, with an axis ratio of nearly 5, corresponding to an inclination i>80^ .

We detect four outflow plumes in CO \ at 0.3 resolution that appear to correspond to molecular gas located near the edges of the known ionized outflow cone and its (unobserved) counterpart behind the disk. The fastest and brightest of these plumes has emission reaching observed line-of-sight projected velocities of over 450 \ beyond systemic, equivalent to an estimated physical outflow velocity v 600 \ for the fastest emission. Most of these plumes have corresponding emission in HCN or HCO ^+ .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4945 as a nearby barred spiral with a starburst nucleus, reporting known and new 36.2-GHz methanol maser regions and variability evidence for an extragalactic maser process.

quotes.quote: NGC 253 and NGC 4945 are two nearby, southern, barred spiral galaxies with starburst nuclei. The single maser region in NGC 4945 appears to be located at the interface region between the galactic bar and the south-eastern spiral arm Henkel+18, McCarthy+18c . Project C3167: the methanol masers of NGC 253 and NGC 4945 NGC 4945 The previously reported bright 36.2-GHz methanol maser offset to the south-east from the nucleus of NGC 4945 was readily detected in the latest 2019 March epoch observation (M1 in Figure fig:ngc4945_mar2019 ).

New methanol emission toward NGC 4945 The new methanol emission from region M2 shares overlapping position and velocities with the south-western CS (1--0) and HC _3 N (4--3) emission reported in McCarthy+18c . This angular size corresponds to a linear size of 113 79 pc at the 3.7 Mpc distance of NGC 4945 (likely a vast over-estimate based on variability timescale, see Section sec:variability ). h 0.5 h 0.49 h 0.5 h 0.49 h 0.5 h 0.49 h 0.5 h 0.49 h 0.50 h 0.49 h Evidence for variability in extragalactic methanol masers We currently have five epochs of observations of the 36.2-GHz methanol transition toward NGC 4945, three of which were originally reported in McCarthy+17 and , with the remaining 2 reported in this paper. The detection of the new emission at M2 in NGC 4945 is the first strong evidence for variability in an extragalactic class I methanol maser source. M1 in NGC 4945 may be associated with the star-formation from Knot B, though even if this is correct, we do not observe class I maser emission from other regions in NGC 4945 with enhanced star-formation levels.

sources.summary: MeerKAT H I observations of nearby edge-on starburst NGC 4945 find a deeper map, 6.8 percent anomalous halo gas likely blown out by star formation, and a warped distribution.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we report high sensitivity H i observations of the nuclear starburst galaxy NGC 4945 with MeerKAT. Our H i map is about 2.5 times deeper in column density than previous ATCA mosaic imaging by LVHIS 2018MNRAS.478.1611K . NGC 4945 is a nearby edge-on starburst galaxy belonging to the Centaurus A group.

Observations of the neutral atomic hydrogen ( ) in the nuclear starburst galaxy NGC 4945 with MeerKAT are presented. We find a large amount of halo gas, previously missed by \ observations, accounting for 6.8 of the total \ mass. This is most likely gas blown into the halo by star formation. Our maps go down to a 3 column density level of 5e+18 cm^ 2 . We model the \ distribution using tilted-ring fitting techniques and find a warp on the galaxy's approaching and receding sides.

sources.summary: The paper studies Chandra spectra of NGC 4945, finding hot gas temperatures and densities that peak centrally, decline along the outflows, require non-spherical or mass-loaded wind geometry, and imply charge-exchange emission.

quotes.quote: We analyze 330 ks of Chandra X-ray imaging and spectra of the nearby, edge-on starburst and Seyfert Type 2 galaxy NGC 4945 to measure the hot gas properties along the galactic outflows. We extract and model spectra from 15 regions extending from 0.55 kpc to + 0.85 kpc above and below the galactic disk to determine the best-fit parameters and metal abundances. We find that the hot gas temperatures and number densities peak in the central regions and decrease along the outflows. These profiles are inconsistent with a spherical, adiabatically-expanding wind model, suggesting the need to include mass loading and/or a non-spherical outflow geometry. We estimate the mass outflow rate of the hot wind to be 2.1 M_   yr ^ 1 .

NGC 4945 is a nearby ( D = 3.72  Mpc; Tully2016 ) edge-on ( i=78^ ; Ott2001 ) galaxy that lies in the Centaurus A/M83 group and is known to host a central starburst Schurch2002,Emig_2020 as well as a Seyfert Type 2 AGN Iwasawa1993 .

Similar to M82 and NGC 253, NGC 4945 is one of the nearest far-IR galaxies (FIRGs; Heckman1990 ) producing large-scale outflows along the galaxy's minor axis. The outflow has been observed at mm Bolatto2021 , optical Venturi2017,Mingozzi2019 , and X-ray wavelengths Schurch2002,Done2003,Marinucci2017 .

sources.summary: The paper detects radio recombination lines from NGC 4945, modeling its edge-on spiral Seyfert/starburst nucleus, ionized gas, rotation curve, enclosed mass, and possible recombination-line maser emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 4945 is a nearby (3.9 Mpc ) edge-on spiral galaxy containing an obscured nuclear starburst and is the second-brightest Seyfert 2 galaxy in the X-ray sky at 100 keV . Its nucleus hosts water masers with up to 10 Jy .

We used the integrated line strength (11.0 mJy), line width (280 km s ^ 1 ), size of the line-emitting region (70 pc), continuum emission (1439 mJy), and spectral index (-0.75) to constrain conditions in the ionized gas. Using the collection of H II regions model, models with 10 to 300 H II regions, all with T_ e 5000  K, n_ e 10^ 3  cm ^ 3 to 10^ 4  cm ^ 3 and size of (2 to 100) pc produced good matches to the line and continuum emission.

The rotation velocity of 120 km s ^ 1 within the central 1'' (19 pc) radius infers an enclosed mass of 3 10^7 M_ . The water masers ( ) infer an enclosed mass of 1 10^6 M_ within 0.3 pc radius, and so most of the 3 10^7 M_ is extended between 0.3 pc and 19 pc radius of the nucleus. The average surface density within the central 19 pc is 25000 M_   pc ^ 2 , which exceeds the threshold gas surface density for star-formation of (3 to 10)  M_   pc ^ 2 ( ) by four orders of magnitude. The 50 pc ring might be a Lindblad resonance, which would infer a bar. The bright peak in the line emission at the nucleus might be recombination-line maser emission occuring along the line of sight towards the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4945 among the four nearby star-forming galaxies best suited for BATSE giant-flare searches, with distance, supernova-rate, and expected-flare estimates.

quotes.quote: As it is discussed by h1998 , inside the 10 Mpc radius, 25 of star formation is due to just four well-known galaxies: M82 (d =3.4 Mpc), NGC 253 (2.5 Mpc), NGC 4945 (3.7 Mpc), and M83 (3.7 Mpc). Obviously, inside 4 -- 5 Mpc (this is the limiting distance for the BATSE detection of a GF, see Fig. 3 below and the discussion in the text) their contribution is even higher.
As a simple estimate, let us use the following values: 0.4, 0.2, 0.3, and 0.1 SN per year for M82, NGC 253, NGC 4945, and M83, correspondently.

In comparison with the Galactic SN rate, these galaxies have significant enhancement (roughly a factor of 12, 6, 9, and 3 correspondently). It total, the SN rate in the four galaxies is 30 times higher than the one in the Milky Way. We can expect proportionally higher number of SGRs (and GFs) from them. With the Galactic rate of 3 flares in 25 years, for BATSE (4.75 years equivalent of all-sky coverage) we can expect roughly 6-7 GFs from M82, 3-4 GFs from NGC 253, 5-6 from NGC 4945, and 1-2 GFs from M83 (in total about 15-20 GFs from four galaxies during the BATSE life cycle).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4945 as a dusty edge-on starburst galaxy with a superwind and a wide, well populated halo red giant branch.

quotes.quote: NGC 4945 is an edge-on Sb galaxy, hosting a powerful nuclear starburst region, with a ring morphology (Moorwood et al. 1996), powering a galactic superwind (Heckman, Armus, Miley 1990). Similar to NGC 5128, the prominent galaxy of the group to which NGC 4945 is believed to belong, its optical image is marked by dust extinction in the nuclear regions (Marconi et al. 2000; Lipari et al. 1997). The left panel of Fig. ngc4945 shows the color-magnitude diagram of the observed halo field stellar population.

The red giant branch is wide and well populated, indicating a large spread of the halo stellar metallicities. Also shown is the output of the edge detection filtering of the I-band luminosity function. The right panel of Fig. ngc4945 shows the logarithmic I-band luminosity function, compared to the maximum-likelihood best model derived by fitting the data in the range 23<I<24.2 . Again the upper red giant branch power law distribution is evident with a break around I 23.5-23.6 . The edge detection method and the maximum likelihood model disagree slightly on the location of the I-band TRGB.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4945 as a nearby edge-on Seyfert galaxy, constraining its Compton-thick AGN reflector to a resolved, clumpy circumnuclear structure tens of parsecs from the nucleus.

quotes.quote: We present a time, spectral and imaging analysis of the X-ray reflector in NGC 4945, which reveals its geometrical and physical structure with unprecedented detail. NGC 4945 hosts one of the brightest AGN in the sky above 10 keV, but it is only visible through its reflected/scattered emission below 10 keV, due to absorption by a column density of 4 10 ^ 24  cm ^ 2 . A new Suzaku campaign of 5 observations spanning 6 months, together with past XMM-Newton and Chandra observations, show a remarkable constancy (within < 10 ) of the reflected component.

NGC 4945 is an almost edge-on (inclination angle 80^ ) spiral galaxy hosting one of the nearest AGN (D 3.7 Mpc, 1''=18 pc, Mauersberger et al. 1996). NGC 4945 has been widely studied in the past, both in the soft and in the hard X-rays.

The time, spectral and imaging analysis of the X-ray emission of NGC 4945 based on XMM-Newton , Suzaku , Swift/BAT and Chandra data, characterizes of the AGN circumnuclear reflector with unprecedented detail. 1) The comparison between the strong intrinsic variability measured by Swift/BAT and the constant reflection spectra from XMM-Newton and Suzaku observations over more than ten years implies a distance of the reflector D > 35 pc.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4945 as one of the two nearest AGN in its UHECR catalog, with nearby cosmic-ray directions and candidate associations sensitive to deflection scale.
