quotes.quote: NGC 5005 (late-type LINER, Sakamoto et al 2000), mapped with OVRO, has a molecular gas distribution concentrated in a nuclear disk and in an outside ring. Non-circular motions show that the gas is following x1 orbits in the nuclear bar. A light stream of gas reveals epidodic accretion from the ring towards the center. This transient (intermittent) gas flow towards the center could be the way fueling is regulated towards central starburst and active nuckei.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5005 has background continuum sources removed, only slightly resolved low-resolution CO and radio emission, an unresolved AGN, and excellent extranuclear CO-radio agreement.

quotes.quote: NGC 5005 . Two background continuum sources are apparent in the low-resolution RC images of NGC 5005; these have been omitted from all analysis. Like NGC 4414, the 55 \ observations show that the CO and RC is only slightly resolved. At 6 \ resolution, the central unresolved RC emission is associated with a nonstellar active galactic nucleus (AGN). Outside the nucleus, the agreement between the RC and CO emission is excellent, as can be seen both in Figure 1( e ) as well as in Figure figuraq ; the \ image is nearly featureless and has low dispersion.

sources.summary: The paper notes that NGC 5005 had been claimed Compton-thick from ASCA FeK evidence, but that later work questioned that interpretation.

quotes.quote: UGC 05101 and NGC 5005 have been also claimed to be Compton-thick by using an indirect method. Based on the detection of a high upper limit for the EW(FeK ) on ASCA data risaliti_distribution_1999 claimed a column density for NGC 5005 larger than 10 ^ 24 cm ^ 3 . With more recent data, guainazzi_x-ray_2005 questioned its Compton-thick nature.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 5005's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 5005 is an inclined barred spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 16.9 6.7 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) with a broad-line LINER nucleus ho_search_1997-1 . In X-rays, it appears as a compact source embedded within extended emission dudik_chandra_2005,gonzalez-martin_x-ray_2009 . An elongated radio nucleus was detected in arcsecond-scale radio observations (PA 135 ; vila_compact_1990 ; not detected at nagar_radio_2000 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5005 as a weakly barred LINER paired with NGC 5033, with intermediate-age nuclear stars, compact hard X-rays, and shock-indicating H2 and [Fe II] lines.

quotes.quote: NGC 5005 (L1.9) NGC 5005 is a weakly barred spiral galaxy and part of a pair that also includes NGC 5033 Helou82 . It is optically classified as a type 1.9 LINER Ho97 , and it has an intermediate-age stellar population contributing about 45 of the nuclear optical light CidFernandes04 . The presence of a weak AGN is confirmed by the detection of a compact hard X-ray source Gonzalez-Martin06,Dudik09 .

gmw_00000228.name: NGC 5033
gmw_00000228.overview: NGC 5033 is an inclined spiral galaxy [arXiv:1605.01638, arXiv:1503.01836] hosting a Seyfert nucleus [arXiv:2210.15376, arXiv:2207.06524] that exhibits X-ray variability [arXiv:0809.1750, arXiv:2210.15376]. The galaxy features a strongly warped neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:1605.01638, arXiv:astro-ph/0610114] and is paired with the neighboring galaxy NGC 5005 [arXiv:2210.15376, arXiv:1503.01836].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5033 has significant X-ray variability, a quasar-like near-IR/optical SED, and an X-ray-to-optical luminosity ratio similar to quasars.

quotes.quote: We chose four from these sources, namely NGC 4235, NGC 4639, NGC 5033 and NGC 5273, because there exist estimates of their central black hole mass (hence we can estimate their L _ Edd ) and their nuclear luminosity is small: their L _ X /L _ Edd ratio is smaller than 10^ 3 . Therefore, it is possible that accretion in these objects operates in a radiatively inefficient mode. First, we used archival \ and \ data to measure their X-ray variability amplitude and we compared it with the variability amplitude of more luminous Seyferts.

The near IR/optical SEDs of NGC 5033 and NGC 5273 show a concave spectrum, which is almost identical, in shape, to the mean quasar SED. This spectral shape is often interpreted as the signature of the presence of the ``standard", geometrically thin/optically thick disc emission and of dust heated by the central engine. In NGC 4639 we observe the optical part of the spectrum only, which is rising towards the UV (as expected if we observe thermal emission from the accretion disc). In the case of NGC 4235 and NGC 5033, the X--ray to optical luminosity ratio is almost identical to what we observe in quasars.

We detect significant X--ray variations in the light curves of NGC 5033 and NGC 5273, and in the light curve of NGC 4235. The variability amplitude is 5--15 of the light curve's mean, and is exactly of the ``right" order, given their BH mass.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5033 as a nearby spiral with a low-luminosity Seyfert nucleus, compact core, jet-like radio features, X-ray variability, and Compton-thin torus fit.

quotes.quote: NGC 5033 NGC 5033 is a nearby spiral galaxy with a low-luminosity Seyfert 1.8 type nucleus 2010veroncetti with a point-like central X-ray source 1999terashima . This galaxy has been alternatively classified as a Seyfert of type 1.5 1997Ho . This object has a giant neighbor within a short distance: NGC 5005, a Seyfert SAB(rs) galaxy. In the radio, it is predominated by a compact core 2001ho , and showing extended jet-like features to the East-West 2007perestorres .

In the X-rays, 1999terashima using ASCA observations found a point-like X-ray source in the 2–10 a keV band. Their X-ray light curve showed variability on a timescale of 10 ^ 4 s with an amplitude of 20 . In this work, we combine the observations and perform a simultaneous fit of the , and /BAT data.
Using borus02 , we found that the data is consistent with a Compton thin torus ( (N_ H,refl ) =23.49 _ 23.34 ^ 23.69 ) covering 77 of the sky.

sources.summary: NGC 5033 is presented as a nearby Seyfert spiral whose radio and FIR emission are dominated by recent starburst activity, with low cosmic-ray escape and a possible radio spur.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: NGC 5033*^ & 13 13 27 4430 & 36 35 37 880 ... The coordinates correspond to the putative nucleus of NGC 5033

quotes.quote: This latter spectrum is typical of galaxies where energy losses are high, indicating that the escape rate of cosmic ray electrons in NGC 5033 is low. The nucleus contributes little to the total 1.4 GHz radio power of NGC 5033 and, based on the radio to far-infrared (FIR) relation, it appears that the radio and far-infrared emission from NGC 5033 are dominated by a starburst that during the last 10 Myr produced stars at a rate of 2.8 yielding a supernova (type Ib/ and II) rate of 0.045 yr ^ 1 . This supernova rate corresponds to about 1 SN event every 22 yr. Finally, from our deep 8.4 GHz VLA-D image, we suggest the existence of a radio spur in NGC 5033, which could have been due to a hot superbubble formed as a consequence of sequential supernova explosions occurring during the lifetime of a giant molecular cloud.

NGC 5033 is a nearby ( D =13 Mpc) spiral galaxy that exhibits strong CO emission out to at least R 50 , and contains a Seyfert 1.5 nucleus ho97 . NGC 5033 also displays strong far infrared luminosity ( L_ 8-1000 m = 1.20 10 ; sanders03 ), and has been host to at least three supernovae in the last 60 yr (SN 1950C, SN 1985L, and SN 2001gd). Here, we present and discuss continuum VLA-D observations of NGC 5033 at 1.4, 4.9 and 8.4 GHz taken on April 8, 2003, complemented with archival VLA data taken on August 29, 1999 (1.4 GHz, A-configuration) and October 31, 1999 (4.9 GHz, B-configuration), respectively.

sources.summary: The paper measures compact continuum and extended dense-gas emission in NGC 5033, but its kinematic modeling favors a thin disk rather than the thick disks seen in most of the sample.

quotes.quote: NGC 5033 The 3 mm continuum for NGC 5033 is presented in the top left panel of Figure fig:conhcnhco , and has a flux density within a 3 \ aperture of 0.7 mJy. A Gaussian fit gives a size of 1.01 0.79 at PA 41 . As for the other 2 galaxies, this is consistent with the continuum being spatially unresolved. The HCN (1-0) and HCO ^+ (1-0) maps are shown in the top middle and right panels of Figure fig:conhcnhco respectively. The measured sizes are 2.0 1.1 at PA 17 and 1.7 1.2 at PA 52 respectively.
For this object there is no convincing evidence for a thick disk. We conclude that our kinematic analysis favours the thin disk solution, for which the corresponding intrinsic /v ratio is <0.1 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5033 as a nearby barred Seyfert with changing optical classification, an off-center black hole, merger signs, low Chandra obscuration, and persistently strong Fe K emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 5033 is an intriguing Seyfert galaxy because its sub-classification may change with time, and because optical and sub-mm observations find that the massive black hole does not sit at the dynamical center of the galaxy, pointing to a past merger. We obtained a new optical spectrum of NGC 5033 using the 200'' Hale telescope at Palomar that clearly reveals a broad H line (FWHM =5400 300 km s ^ 1 ). This signals a clear view of the optical broad line region (BLR) and requires Seyfert-1.5 designation.

Our analysis of a 2019 Chandra spectrum of the massive black hole reveals very little obscuration, also suggesting a clean view of the central engine. However, the narrow Fe K emission line is measured to have an equivalent with of EW =460^ +100 _ 90 eV. This value is extremely high compared to typical values in unobscured AGN. Indeed, the line is persistently strong in NGC 5033: the line equivalent width in a 2002 XMM-Newton snapshot is EW =250^ +40 _ 40 eV, similar to the EW =290^ +100 _ 100 eV equivalent width measured using ASCA in 1999.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5033 as a piled-up Seyfert nucleus whose hard flux is nucleus-dominated despite several off-nuclear sources, with only small expected contamination.

quotes.quote: NGC 5033 (S1.5) . --- A result on the same data set is presented in Ho et al. (2001). The nucleus is significantly piled up in the observation. The five off-nuclear sources shown in Table 3 are located within the beam. The sum of the counts from these sources is less than 44 counts in the 2--8 keV band, while 380 counts are detected from the nucleus before correction for pileup.

Therefore, the flux ( 5.5 10^ 12 ; Terashima et al. 1999, 2002b) is probably larger than the true nuclear flux by 10 or less, unless the off-nuclear sources show drastic time variability. We used the flux without any correction for the off-nuclear source contribution. The 10 uncertainty does not affect any of the conclusions.

sources.summary: The paper finds possible warp signatures in NGC 5033, no detected inner radial inflow above five percent of circular speed, and possible weak-interaction or spiral-streaming effects.

quotes.quote: The CO data show some evidence for a warp based on the s_1 s_3 plot [ Fig. fig:s13:5033 () ] , although the uncertainties are large. No indication of radial inflow at speeds of > 5 of the circular speed ( v_c 200 ) is seen for R<55 . For the \ data, the s_1 and s_3 terms are positively correlated at radii beyond 90 , raising the possibility that the galaxy's warp, which only becomes prominent around R_ 25 =320 , is pervasive throughout the galaxy, as suggested for NGC 4414.

Such a disturbance may be related to the weak interaction of NGC 5033 with Holmberg VIII, as evidenced by the tidal \ plume on the southern side of the galaxy Thean:97 . However, the peak in s_1 and s_3 near R =110 \ [ Fig. fig:s13:5033 (a) ] might instead be the result of spiral streaming. The morphological inclination also falls with radius beyond R =30 -100 , although it does not closely follow the kinematic inclination [ Fig. fig:s13:5033 (b) ] .

sources.summary: The paper associates NGC 5033's polarized emission with its bright inner continuum disk, giving it an X-shaped appearance and increasing polarized fraction at larger radii.

quotes.quote: NGC 5033 The polarized emission in this galaxy is associated with the bright inner continuum disk reported by braun_etal_2007 . It has a roughly X-shaped appearance, which may be indicative of minor axis outflows, as seen elsewhere in our sample. The polarized brightness declines to a minimum in the direction of the receding major axis (PA = 352 ^ , as tabulated in Table table:summary ). In the central parts the polarized fraction is of the order of 1 . At larger radii the polarized fraction increases to about 5-7 .

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 5033 has a poor MOND fit, with velocities overestimated inside and underestimated outside, and that including the EFE improves chi-squared to 3.1.

quotes.quote: (14) NGC 5033 : With a luminosity of about L_V=3.91 10^ 10 L_ n3521-lu , NGC 5033 is located at ( 13^h13^m27.4^s, 36^d35^m38^s n4395-p ) and at a distance of about 19.6 Mpc from us with a large uncertainty such that the minimum and maximum distance estimation is 15.2 and 42.5 Mpc n3198-dm, n5033-md , respectively. This is one of the galaxies that MOND could not reproduce acceptably with a reduced ^2 of 7.04. MOND overestimates the rotation velocities in the inner parts and underestimates them in the outer parts. A better fit with ^2=3.1 is obtained by including the EFE. The required external field is a_e=0.335^ +0.070 _ 0.080 a_0 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5033 as a massive SAc galaxy with structured HI, two dwarf companions, ordered central kinematics, an outer disc warp, and spiral-arm streaming motions.

quotes.quote: NGC 5033 NGC 5033 is a massive SAc galaxy whose kinematics have been well-studied in the literature. The HI data presented in Fig.  fig:n5033sum were processed from archival VLA observations which were originally published in . Two dwarf companion galaxies, UGC 08314 and UGC 08303, are also detected in these observations (see Table  tab:n5033comps ). The distribution of HI in NGC 5033 displays a lot of structure. In general, regions of greater HI surface density correspond to regions with strong H emission, which is mostly in the centre and along the spiral arms.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5033 as having a featureless exponential CO profile, a high extinction-corrected molecular Schmidt index, gas close to the critical relation, and a flat inner atomic profile.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, two of the seven galaxies (NGC 4501 and 5033) show featureless, roughly exponential CO profiles. Most galaxies exhibit an excellent correlation between azimuthally averaged H and CO emission that can be parametrized using a Schmidt (power) law: n_ mol = d d _ mol , For the N_ H dependent extinction corrections, the inferred power-law indices range from n_ mol 1 for NGC 4414 to n_ mol 2 for NGC 5033 (see Table tbl:nschmidt ).

At first glance, it is impressive how well the relation _ gas _ crit holds for some of the galaxies (NGC 4501, 5033, and 5055). On the other hand, there are two galaxies (NGC 4321 and 4414) where _ gas > _ crit over a significant range of radius, while in NGC 4736 and 5457, _ gas < _ crit across most of the disk.
In NGC 4414, 5033, and 5055, the \ profile remains relatively flat across the inner disk, while in NGC 4321, 4501, 4736, and 5457, the \ profile declines toward the center.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 5033's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 5033 is an inclined spiral galaxy at a distance of D= 18.1 7.4 Mpc (NED redshift-independent median) hosting an AGN, which has optically been classified either as a Sy 1.2, 1.5, 1.8 or 1.9 (see discussion in trippe_multi-wavelength_2010 ). It features a compact radio core with jet-like structure extending 0.5 45 pc to the east (PA 95 ; e.g., ho_radio_2001 ) and a one-sided very wide kiloparsec-scale cone towards PA 80 mediavilla_asymmetrical_2005 . The first MIR observations of NGC 5033 were performed by , but the nucleus remained undetected.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5033 as an inclined spiral paired with NGC 5005, hosting a dust-obscured Seyfert 1.5 nucleus that is brighter in the near-infrared.

quotes.quote: NGC 5033 (S1.5) This highly inclined spiral galaxy forms a pair with NGC 5005 Helou82 . It hosts a Seyfert 1.5 nucleus, obscured by strong spiral dust lanes revealed by HST/NICMOS and WFPC2 images Martini03,Erwin04 . The galaxy nucleus is conspicuously brighter in the near-infrared than in the optical, confirming the presence of nuclear dust lanes that are probably responsible for the flattening of the continuum at short wavelengths in the GNIRS spectrum (Fig.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5033 as having an unresolved AGN, a nearly featureless ratio image, possible weak southern CO enhancement, and background continuum sources.

quotes.quote: NGC 5033 . Like NGC 5005, NGC 5033 has an unresolved AGN at its nucleus, and its \ image is nearly featureless. An enhancement in \ is seen at the southern end of the CO emission; however, this CO emission is detected only at a 3 level and might be spurious. Background continuum sources are apparent in both the high- and low-resolution data sets.

sources.summary: NGC 5033 is described as a tidally distorted Seyfert I galaxy with an asymmetric, strongly warped H i disk and a radio continuum image showing bright inner and faint outer disks.

quotes.quote: This tidally distorted Seyfert-I galaxy has seen numerous kinematic studies (e.g. Thean et al. ). Our H i map shows substantial asymmetry, the gaseous disk being strongly warped. The radio continuum image reveals a bright inner- and faint outer disk.

gmw_00000229.name: NGC 5135
gmw_00000229.overview: NGC 5135 is a face-on barred spiral luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1804.07311, arXiv:1809.06283] that hosts a Compton-thick Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1109.0921, arXiv:1809.06283]. The nucleus is surrounded by intense circumnuclear starburst activity [arXiv:1310.2770] and a large, high-excitation ionization cone driven by an AGN outflow [arXiv:0904.3324, arXiv:1102.0012].

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 5135 with ALMA, resolving circumnuclear CO and dust clumps along bar dust lanes, finding shock-enhanced CO ratios and at most a 1 percent AGN contribution to CO(6-5).

quotes.quote: We present high-resolution (0.17 0.14 ) Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) observations of the 65 \ line, and 435 \ dust continuum emission within a 9 9 \ area centered on the nucleus of the galaxy NGC 5135. NGC 5135 is a well-studied luminous infrared galaxy that also harbors a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus (AGN).

At the achieved resolution of 48 40 pc, the 65 \ and dust emissions are resolved into gas ``clumps'' along the symmetrical dust lanes associated with the inner stellar bar. The clumps have radii between 45-180 pc and 65 \ line widths of 60-88 . The 65 \ to dust continuum flux ratios vary among the clumps and show an increasing trend with the /Br- ratios, which we interpret as evidence for supernova-driven shocked gas providing a significant contribution to the 65 \ emission. The central AGN is undetected in continuum, nor in CO (6-5) if its line velocity width is no less than 40 . We estimate that the AGN contributes at most 1 of the integrated 65 \ flux of 512 24 Jy \ within the ALMA field of view, which in turn accounts for 32 of the 65 \ flux of the whole galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5135 has extra-nuclear mid-infrared star-forming blobs, weak diffuse emission, and possible star-formation contamination of the central point-source flux.

quotes.quote: In addition to the central point source, in three sources -- NGC 1097 , NGC 5135 , NGC 7469 - we observed clear extra-nuclear MIR emission. In all of these objects we find distinct blobs of star formation (SF), in addition NGC 5135 and NGC 7469 also exhibit a weak diffuse component. Typical distances between SF blobs and the AGN are 8 2 ( 700 pc) in NGC 1097, 1 5 ( 400 pc) in NGC 5135 and 1 3 ( 400 pc) in NGC 7469.

In the latter two objects the proximity of the SF components to the AGN does not allow to rule out a significant contribution of SF to the measured flux of the central point source. By comparing the fluxes of individual SF blobs and of the AGN, we can estimate upper limits for such contamination. This yields 15 for NGC 5135 and 10 for NGC 7469.

sources.summary: The paper studies the central 2.3 kpc of NGC 5135, a nearby LIRG with an AGN and circumnuclear starburst, mapping gas ionization and a 600 pc [SiVI] ionization cone.

quotes.quote: We present a study of the central 2.3 kpc of NGC 5135, a nearby Luminous Infrared Galaxy (LIRG) with an AGN and circumnuclear starburst. Our main results are based on intermediate spectral resolution ( 3000 4000 ) near infrared data taken with the SINFONI integral field spectrograph at the ESO VLT.

For the first time in this galaxy, we have detected the presence of a high excitation ionization cone centered on the AGN by using the [ SiVI ] 1.96 m line. So far, this structure is the largest reported in the literature for this coronal line, extending (in projection) as far as 600 pc from the galaxy nucleus.

In a complex spatial distribution, a variety of mechanisms are driving the gas ionization, including SNe remnant shocks, young stars and AGN photoionization. The excitation of the molecular gas, however, is mainly produced by X-rays and SNe remnant shocks.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5135 as a Seyfert 2 LIRG with small soft X-ray excess, powerful starburst contribution, and Compton-thick classification from large Fe K equivalent width.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert 2 galaxies in our sample of LIRGs lie on the correlation (NGC 3690 and IC 4518W), or have a small (less than a factor of 3) soft X-ray emission excess (MCG 03-34-064, NGC 5135, and NGC 7130). In Type 2 Seyferts the absorbing hydrogen column density towards the AGN is high and thus most of the soft X-ray emission coming from the AGN is absorbed. Therefore we conclude that when a sufficiently powerful starburst is present it may contribute significantly to the observed soft X-ray emission.
The 3 Seyferts (NGC 3690, NGC 5135, and NGC 7130) that lie within 2 of the expected relation for star-formation are those with powerful starbursts.

The other 3 Seyfert 2s in our LIRGs sample (NGC 3690, NGC 5135, and NGC 7130) might be Compton thick AGN. In fact, NGC 5135 and NGC 7130 have been classified as Compton thick based on their large \ EW (see Levenson2004, Levenson2005 ).

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 5135 as a local LIRG with AGN and supernova activity, reporting a large coronal-line outflow, ionizing cones, and star-forming knots.

quotes.quote: By observing the local luminous infrared galaxy NGC 5135 with the near-IR IFU spectrograph SINFONI (ESO VLT), we report a 600 pc (in projection) AGN outflow traced by [ SiVI ] 1.96 m emission. This is the largest outflow traced by a coronal line ever reported. Its large spatial scale suggests that shocks, in addition to AGN continuum emission, are needed to locally produce [ SiVI ] emission.

We use the [ FeII ] 1.64 m emission, a supernovae tracer, to estimate the supernovae rate in different star-forming knots ( 250 pc across) within the central 2.3 kpc of NGC 5135. This finding, together with evidence for a similar (weaker) structure to the South-West (SW), suggest that we have detected, for the first time, the presence of ionizing cones in NGC 5135.

sources.summary: NGC 5135 is one of the spiral Seyfert LIRGs; its mapping aperture covers bright nuclear mid-infrared emission, and shock/PDR modeling is needed for its CO SLED.

quotes.quote: The other three galaxies (NGC 5135, NGC 7130, and NGC 7469) are spirals, although NGC 7130 and NGC 7469 have peculiar morphologies suggesting recent minor interactions Genzel1995, Bellocchi2012 .

For the more nearby NGC 5135 and NGC 7130, IRS spectral mapping observations are available and it is possible to extract their mid-IR spectra from a 15 15 \ aperture (5 5 kpc) comparable to the \ beam size and large enough to include most of their bright nuclear mid-IR emission.

The CO emission is compatible with that predicted by shock models only for NGC 5135 We do not consider UGC 05101 because its _1 uncertainty, 2.4, is very large. , whose best-fit model has n_ H_2 =10^ 4.5 cm ^ 3 . This result, however, strongly depends on the adopted density. For instance, for the model with n_ H_2 =10^ 6 cm ^ 3 , which provides only a slightly worse fit to the CO SLED of NGC 5135, the CO emission would need a PDR component.

sources.summary: NGC 5135 is an optical Seyfert 2 with [Ne v], hard X-ray excess, Fe K emission, and modeling that favors a reflection-dominated AGN.

quotes.quote: [ 142 ] NGC 5135 : This galaxy is classified as an optical Seyfert 2 e.g. YuaKew2010 , and meets our infrared [ Ne v ] line criterion for AGN selection PetArm2011 . It shows an excess in hard X-rays, with a Fe K_ line at 6.4 keV with a 2.9 significance, which could be the result of either absorption or a reflection component. Fitting an absorbed power-law with fixed photon index of 1.8 yields an absorbing column density of N_H 4 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , which is not large enough to produce the equivalent width of the iron line of EW 0.9 keV obtained through the same model. Fitting a pexrav model MagZdz1995 with a fixed photon index of 2.0 yields a believable EW 1.1 keV, which means our data favour a reflection dominated AGN.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5135 as a Seyfert 2 barred spiral with intense nuclear star-forming clumps, an AGN-position dispersion peak, star-formation arc, and outflow-coincident dispersion drop.

quotes.quote: NGC 5135 : This Seyfert 2 galaxy Phillips+83 , classified as SB(s)ab, presents a nuclear region with intense SF clumps along with its AGN see and references therein Busko+90,Levenson+04,Bedregal+09,Veron-Cetty+06,Colina+12 , which made the photometric analysis challenging. Although the innermost part displays low inclination values, the wider FoV covered by the imagining data reveals an inclination of 50 . The kinematic analysis reveals a peak in the stellar velocity dispersion map coincident with the AGN position, and a low-value ( 50 km s ^ 1 ) arc following the SF regions. In addition, a strong diminution in the velocity dispersion can be observed to the SW, spatially coincident with the presence of an outflow Colina+05 . The complex photometric structure of the innermost regions along with the small velocity amplitude are most likely contributing to the differences between the photometrically and kinematically derived PA and inclination.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5135 as one of the UV-bright Seyfert 2 nuclei whose resolved UV continuum and compact emission support a nuclear starburst interpretation.

quotes.quote: In four of the targets (Mrk 477, NGC 7130, NGC 5135 and IC 3639) the morphology suggests that they harbor a nuclear starburst (Figure 1). In these cases, the UV continuum is spatially resolved, showing sub-arcsec structure. The effective radius (50-200 pc) of the UV emission is similar to the size of the NLR. At the position where HST+WFPC2 (F606W) suggests that the nucleus is located, the UV images show very little emission (less than 15 of the total). This result suggests that even at UV wavelengths the S1 nucleus is still obscured.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5135 as a nearby Seyfert 2/starburst composite whose obscured AGN dominates above 4 keV while extended soft X-rays trace stellar processes.

quotes.quote: Observations of the Seyfert 2 and starburst galaxy NGC 5135 with the Chandra X-ray Observatory demonstrate that both of these phenomena contribute significantly to its X-ray emission. We spatially isolate the active galactic nucleus (AGN) and demonstrate that it is entirely obscured by column density N_H > 10^ 24 , detectable in the bandpass only as a strongly reprocessed, weak continuum and a prominent iron K emission line with equivalent width of 2.4 keV. Most of the soft X-ray emission, both near the AGN and extending over several-kpc spatial scales, is collisionally-excited plasma. We attribute this thermal emission to stellar processes. The AGN dominates the X-ray emission only at energies above 4 keV.

sources.summary: The paper observes NGC 5135 as a local LIRG/Seyfert 2, detecting compact nuclear mid-infrared emission, central star-forming regions, silicate absorption, and nuclear high-ionization line emission.

quotes.quote: The 3 LIRGs host an optically identified AGN (NGC 5135 and IC 4518W are Seyfert (Sy) 2s, whereas the NGC 7130 has been classified as Sy 2 or LINER). From our T-ReCS and NICMOS imaging data we have detected unresolved nuclear emission in NGC 5135 and IC 4518W, implying sizes of < 40-51 pc, respectively, whereas the mid-IR nuclear emission of NGC 7130 appears resolved with a size of 190 pc (see Alonso-Herrero et al. 2006b). In addition we have detected in NGC 5135 and NGC 7130 bright regions within the central 1-2 kpc.

In NGC 5135 the T-ReCS slit was placed such (Fig. 2) we could observe the Sy2 and a bright H ii region (with mid-IR and Pa emission) located 2.5 600 pc away, as well as the region with diffuse emission between them. The Spitzer /IRS spectrum of the central 4 4 ( 1 kpc 1 kpc ) includes emission from the Sy nucleus and a number of H ii regions, as well as diffuse emission. The emission line arises only from the nuclear regions (Figs. 2 and 3), and it is not detected in the H ii region, whereas the lower ionization [ Ne ii ] 12.8 m line is detected in both (Fig. 2).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5135 as a Sy2 galaxy with intense surrounding star formation, asymmetric line ratios, little PAH variation from AGN activity, and off-nuclear shallow silicate absorption.

quotes.quote: NGC 5135 This galaxy hosts a Sy2 nucleus surrounded by intense star formation ( Bedregal2009 and references therein), that we cannot separate due the limited spatial resolution of the IRS spectral maps. However the line ratios (e.g \ ratio) show some asymmetries that may be associated with the truncated ring-like structure of this galaxy. At our scale ( 0.5 kpc), we do not see any noticeable variations in the PAH emission due to the AGN activity. The silicate absorption feature is shallow (-0.47) and the maximum value is not located at the nucleus.

sources.summary: NGC 5135 is presented as a composite LIRG with spatially separated Seyfert 2, young-star, supernova, diffuse-ISM, and X-ray-emitting circumnuclear components within about 1-2 kpc.

quotes.quote: NGC 5135: prototype of composite LIRG Out of our sample of LIRGs, NGC 5135 is the only galaxy where clear evidence for spatially resolved and well-separated regions, dominated by either a Seyfert 2 nucleus, young massive stars, or supernovae, is well established ( Bedregal:2009p2426 ; Colina:2012go ). Imaging of the different emission lines for the entire emitting region of about 1.5 kpc across, for the regions dominated by the AGN, young stars, supernovae, and diffuse ISM, and the specific integrated values for these regions are presented in Fig. figure:NGC5135 .

NGC 5135 represents therefore a prototype of the so-called composite galaxies where AGN, young stars, and supernovae clumps, as well as AGN-contaminated and general diffuse ionized medium, are all within about 1-2 kpc, therefore unresolved (even with AO) in intermediate and high-redshift galaxies.

Detailed studies of the X-ray emission in NGC 5135 Colina:2012go established the presence of hard X-ray emitting regions associated with the Seyfert 2 nucleus and the bright SNe-dominated [ FeII ] emission peak, with a diffuse soft X-ray component covering the entire circumnuclear region.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5135 is the sample exception without clear core-subtracted extended MIR emission, though weak structures may exist; prior ALMA non-detection suggests a depleted or past-active nucleus.

quotes.quote: After the core subtraction, all objects, except NGC 5135, show clearly extended emission. Even in NGC 5135 extended emission might be present as indicated by the low significance structures in the image ( 3 ). Interestingly, the nuclear region of NGC 5135 remained undetected also in recent high resolution ALMA observations tracing cold dust and molecular gas cao_alma_2018 , implying that the AGN in this object might have used up most of its fuel. In that case, the bright emission might be coming predominantly from the kiloparsec scales, i.e, the outer NLR, as a relict of its more active past.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5135 as a Seyfert galaxy with starburst spiral arms, fit by an absorbed single-component star-forming radio SED with measured turnover, synchrotron slope, SFR, dust luminosity, and AGN fraction.

quotes.quote: NGC 5135 : classified as having a Seyfert-type AGN nucleus Condon96 with strong starburst along the spiral arms Marin07 , its radio SED covering 2.0 decades in frequency (70 MHz-6.7 GHz) is fitted with single-component star-forming region properties (including absorption) with a turnover at 0.130 GHz. The synchrotron spectral index, = 0.88. The TF is 3.6 . The CIGALE-estimated SFR _ IR , L_ dust , AGN fraction are 11.22 M_ yr ^ 1 , 10 ^ 11.21 L_ , 6 , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5135's nucleus has a young starburst contributing over half the 4020 flux, an intermediate-age 640 Myr component, and stellar-population fractions consistent with previous Balmer-line modeling.

quotes.quote: In Mrk 1210, NGC 5135 and NGC 7410 they account for more than half of the 4020 flux, and in several other Seyfert 2s their contribution exceeds 20 .

Our spectral synthesis find null contributions of the 100 and 290 Myr components, but for NGC 5135 it fits 27 of the continuum at 4861 \ as due to the 640 Myr component, while for IC 5135 18 and 21 are associated with 640 and 900 Myr-old bursts respectively.

For both IC 5135 and NGC 5135 they find 50 , 40 and 10 contributions (at 4800 ) of young, intermediate age and old populations respectively, where the intermediate age component is modeled as a combination of 200, 500 and 1000 Myr bursts. Our condensed population vector (at 4861 ) for these galaxies are (x_ Y/FC ,x_I,x_O) = (60,40,0) and (56,27,17) respectively.

sources.summary: The paper reports T-ReCS imaging of NGC 5135, treats its nucleus as an unresolved Seyfert 2 source, and estimates that it contributes about 20% of the central N-band emission.

quotes.quote: For the unresolved sources (nuclei of NGC 5135 and IC 4518W) the aperture corrections to include all the flux from the point source were computed from standard star observations. To compare our T-ReCS data with other galaxies (see 3.3), we converted the T-ReCS N band flux densities to IRAC 8 m ones, convolving Spitzer /IRS spectra with the appropriate filter transmissions. For NGC 5135 and NGC 7130 we found f_ ( IRAC 8 m )/f_ ( N-band ) = 0.86 and 0.76, respectively. These might be average values as spatial variations of the MIR spectral properties of the central regions have been observed in nearby galaxies (e.g., Smith et al. 2004). For the Seyfert (Sy) nuclei of NGC 5135 and IC 4518W, and using a 'generic' IRS Sy2 template (Mrk 3, Weedman et al. 2005), we obtained f_ ( IRAC 8 m )/f_ ( N-band ) = 0.56 .
The Sy2 nucleus of NGC 5135 contributes 20 of the N band emission within the central 10 , and even less to the IRAS integrated galaxy emission (Table 1).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of NGC 5135, describing its best-fit components, Fe K emission or flux behavior, and comparison with previous modeling.

quotes.quote: NGC 5135 Chandra observations of NGC 5135 reveal two X-ray point sources near the nucleus of the galaxy. The northern source was identified by Levenson et al. (2004) as the active nucleus, so we restrict our analysis to this source, using an extraction region of 1.2.'' They find the AGN spectrum to be best fit by a model consisting of two thermal components, a Gaussian component at 2 keV and at the Fe K energy, and an absorbed power law.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5135 as a local LIRG with an AGN detected in the IRS spectral decomposition, dominating the 6 micron slit emission while starburst emission dominates at longer wavelengths.

quotes.quote: NGC 5135 and NGC 7469 are intermediate cases. The AGN is clearly detected in NGC 5135, and dominates the 6 m emission within the IRS slit, but the SB component accounts for most of the continuum emission at > 15 m.
The largest discrepancy is for NGC 5135 for which we find a clear AGN component (see Figure 1) at 6 , whereas the upper limit is well below our estimate.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5135 has core-dominated [O III] emission plus broad coronal and blueshifted line components interpreted as possible AGN-driven outflow kinematics.

quotes.quote: The total [ O iii ] emission is dominated by the broad component in MCG-03-34-064 and in NGC 7130, whereas in NGC 5135 and NGC 7469 the core component dominates the emission. In NGC 5135 based on both the relatively broad [ Si vi ] 1.96 m coronal line and its spatial extent suggested the existence of different kinematical components and the possible presence of AGN induced outflows. found blueshifted components in the Ly and [ O iii ] 5007 lines of both NGC 7130 and NGC 5135.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 5135's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 5135 is an infrared-luminous face-on barred spiral galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0137 ( D Mpc). It harbours a Sy 2 nucleus veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 and a circum-nuclear starburst gonzalez_delgado_ultraviolet-optical_1998,bedregal_near-ir_2009 . The latter has a knotty morphology and extends over 3 0.9 kpc.

sources.summary: The appendix describes NGC 5135 as an infrared-luminous face-on barred spiral with a Sy2 nucleus and banana-shaped circumnuclear star formation extending from 300 to 750 pc.

quotes.quote: NGC 5135 is an infrared--luminosity, face-on barred spiral galaxy. The nucleus is classified as a Sy2 Veron10 and it is surrounded by a circumnuclear SF in banana-shaped Gonzalez98, Bedregal09 . The inner and outer radius of the star formation emission, are located at 300 pc and 750 pc from the nucleus, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 5135 as a barred group galaxy with variable, extended X-ray emission, distinct soft and hard PSPC morphologies, and a scattered-plus-thermal best-fit model.

quotes.quote: Several of the galaxies are variable (NGC 5135, Mrk 463, NGC 6221, and NGC 7582), typically showing flux changing by factors of less than 50 on timescales on the order of 10^4 s.
The hard emission from NGC 5135 is symmetric and centrally concentrated, while in this galaxy the soft emission is more diffuse and irregular (Figure fig:phsn5135 ).
NGC 5135 is best fit with the three-component model of a scattered power law and thermal emission.

sources.summary: NGC 5135 is presented as a spatially extended, heavily absorbed composite whose spectra are dominated by galaxy/starburst emission rather than an exposed AGN.

quotes.quote: As an example, Figure fig:spatial illustrates the spatial extent of NGC 5135 with a \ HRI map and radial profiles from both HRI and PSPC observations, which represent the conservative measurement method.

\ observations are not sensitive to such high column densities, and higher-energy data are required to measure the intrinsic power law and absorption accurately in these instances, as well as in NGC 5135.
When N_H is small, we see the AGN shining through (e.g., NGC 7582), while for the heavily absorbed cases (e.g., NGC 5135) the galaxies dominate the spectra.

sources.summary: The paper observes NGC 5135 as a local LIRG whose unresolved Sy2 nucleus is a bright mid-infrared source but contributes no more than about 20 percent of the central N-band emission.

quotes.quote: Three LIRGs (NGC 5135, IC 4518W, and NGC 7130) were observed with the N broad-band filter (central wavelength _ =10.36 m) and NGC 3256 with the Si-2 narrow-band filter ( _ =8.74 m).

The Sy2 nuclei of NGC 5135 and IC 4518W appear unresolved in the T-ReCs images, in agreement with the detection of nuclear point sources in the higher spatial resolution ( 0.15 ) NICMOS images (see AAH06).
The Seyfert nucleus of IC 4518W appears to be dominate the mid-IR emission, whereas for NGC 5135 and NGC 7130 the Seyfert nuclei contribute 20 or less of the N band emission within the central 10 .

gmw_00000230.name: NGC 5252
gmw_00000230.overview: NGC 5252 is a lenticular Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:2309.00051, arXiv:2001.07927] featuring a spectacular biconical ionization structure [arXiv:1708.02683] and a compact radio core with jet-like extended emission [arXiv:1310.2770]. The galaxy exhibits both intrinsic and absorption variability [arXiv:2007.02616], and it likely underwent a past merger event [arXiv:1807.03792, arXiv:2401.09172].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5252 as the lenticular Seyfert host and JVLA target and places CXO J1338+04 about 10 kpc from its center.

quotes.quote: The object CXO J1338+04 was observed in the same integration as its host galaxy NGC 5252, as part of our JVLA 22 GHz imaging survey of radio-quiet AGN host galaxies from the Swift BAT ultra-hard X-ray survey Baumgartner2013 . The observations were taken in the K-band while the array was in C-configuration, so the resulting images have a beam size of 1 .

Our observations of NGC 5252 occurred on March 2, 2020 as part of an execution block including two other science targets. The block began with X- and K-band attenuation scans and an X-band reference pointing scan on 3C 286. The science observation of NGC 5252 had an on-source integration time of 8 minutes and 50 seconds, and was bracketed on either side by gain calibration scans of J1347+1217 and preceded by an X-band pointing calibration scan of this same target.

In the case of NGC 5252, neither effect was found. Finally, each science target was split from the main measurement set and averaged over all 64 channels within each spectral window, and the data were cleaned to a 0.03 mJy threshold with Briggs weighting. The image obtained by this observation is shown in Figure  fig:images with an inset of the VLBI image of Mezcua2018 and alongside the g band image of NGC 5252 from the Pan-STARRS survey Chambers2016 . The ULX is approximately 10 kpc from the galaxy center.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 5252's nuclear gas kinematics, deriving a very massive black hole and identifying the Seyfert host as unusually bright and massive for its class.

quotes.quote: Two different gas systems are present in the nuclear regions of NGC 5252: the first shows a symmetric velocity field, with decreasing line width and can be interpreted as being produced by gas rotating around the nucleus. The second component, showing significant non circular motions, is found to be associated exclusively with the off-nuclear blobs. Following the fitting procedure described earlier, the best fit to our data is obtained for a black hole mass M_ BH = 9.5 10^8 M _ . The model fitting of the nuclear rotation curve of NGC 5252 shows that the kinematics of gas in its innermost regions can be successfully accounted for by circular motions in a thin disk when a point-like dark mass (presumably a supermassive black hole) is added to the galaxy potential.

Therefore, the black hole mass we derived for NGC 5252 is larger by a factor 10 than the value expected from this correlation(see Fig. corr ). This value however is in good agreement with the correlation between bulge and BH mass. As for its active nucleus, NGC 5252 is an outlier when compared to the available data for Seyfert galaxies, not only as it harbours a black hole larger than typical for these objects, but also as its host galaxy is substantially brighter than average for Seyfert galaxies.

sources.summary: NGC 5252 is treated as a known EELR and archetypal Seyfert ionization-cone galaxy whose gas ring and morphology inform the tidal-debris interpretation.

quotes.quote: This search recovered known EELRs (such as Mkn 78, Mkn 266, and NGC 5252) and identified additional previously unknown cases, one with detected emission to =37 kpc.

Some of these, such as Mkn 1498 and the Teacup 1430+13, show a phenomenon remarked earlier in, for example, NGC 5252 Dadina the ionization balance stays roughly constant with radius, which is naturally explained if the characteristic density n_e ^ 2 .

The examples of Hanny's Voorwerp Josza2009 and NGC 5252 Prieto suggest that a common source of extraplanar gas at large radii is tidal debris. The host morphologies of the galaxies where we find extended ionized clouds support this notion. Table tbl-morphology lists morphological information on these galaxies, including warps, close companions, asymmetries, or ongoing mergers. The actual incidence of tidal structures will be higher - for example, the inclined ring of gas with distinct kinematics in NGC 5252 has no optical counterpart.

sources.summary: The paper presents GMOS observations of NGC 5252 showing gas around its ULX is kinematically associated with the Seyfert galaxy, rotates around the ULX, has low metallicity, and may mark an accreted low-mass galaxy nucleus.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 5252 contains a recently identified ultra-luminous X-ray (ULX) source that has been suggested to be a possible candidate off-nuclear low-mass active galactic nucleus. We present follow-up optical integral-field unit observations obtained using GMOS on the Gemini-North telescope. In addition to confirming that the ionized gas in the vicinity of the ULX is kinematically associated with NGC 5252, the new observations reveal ordered motions consistent with rotation around the ULX.

The close coincidence of the excitation source of the line-emitting gas with the position of the ULX further suggests that ULX itself is directly responsible for the ionization of the gas. The spatially resolved measurements of \ 6584/ \ surrounding the ULX indicate a low gas-phase metallicity, consistent with those of other known low-mass active galaxies but not that of its more massive host galaxy. These findings strengthen the proposition that the ULX is not a background source, but rather that it is the nucleus of a small, low-mass galaxy accreted by NGC 5252.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC5252 as a Sy1.9 with ionisation cones, a resolved steep-spectrum nuclear component, and a northern component detected at 22 GHz.

quotes.quote: NGC5252 It is a Sy1.9 galaxy which is known to exhibit pair of ionisation cones in optical emission lines images e.g. TadhunterTsvetanov1989 . Previous VLA studies revealed two components 10 kpc far, one (the southern) coincident with optical nucleus, the other (northern) which can be associated with emission line features, both having steep spectra e.g. WilsonTsvetanov1994,Tsvetanov1996,Kukula1995 . YangYangParagi2017 performed EVN observations focusing on the off-nuclear component, finding that it is compact on pc scale, it has a flat spectrum and a high brightness temperature ( T_ B 10 ^7 K), which is interpreted as signature of a background LLAGN.

Our radio maps exhibit a compact, resolved core and a northern component 22 arcsec far ( 10 kpc) which is however detected only at 22 GHz. The flux densities for the nuclear component are 8.8 and 2.7 at 22 and 45 GHz, while the 22 GHz flux of the northern component is 0.6 . The nuclear component has a steep spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 5252 as an observational comparison for AGN ionization-cone simulations, linking its velocity field, [OIII] image, wave-pattern morphology, and Doppler shifts to helical and pinch jet modes.

quotes.quote: We show that the shocks produced by this instability in the ambient medium of the jet are localized inside a cone with a large opening angle and are capable of producing features that are morphologically similar to those observed in galaxies with active nuclei (NGC 5252 for example).

A good example is provided by the NGC 5252 galaxy whose velocity field was published by Morse et al. (1998) and Moiseev et al. (2000). Below we show that such a behavior can be explained by the development of an axisymmetric wave mode. Indeed if the tilt angle of the radio-jet axis to the line of sight 30-60^ , then the cone nearest to the observer should be seen through shock fronts propagating virtually toward the observer and the farthest cone, against receding shock fronts (see Fig. f12 ).

In Fig. f13 we compare the [ OIII ] line image of the NGC 5252 galaxy (contour image in Fig. 1 from Paper I) and examples of arbitrarily scaled simulated images for the modes with m=0 and m=1 . Note that our simulations were either axisymmetric or nonaxisymmetric. However, in real situations pinch perturbations and helical modes in the jet develop simultaneously. Nonlinear superposition of such modes can explain the morphology of the wave pattern and the velocity field observed in the NGC 5252 galaxy if we assume that the helical and axisymmetric (pinch) mode dominate in the inner and outer parts, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5252 as the S0 Seyfert host associated with the off-nuclear ULX and notes evidence for a past interaction and possible dwarf-galaxy accretion.

quotes.quote: The ULX CXO J133815.6+043255 is located at a projected separation of 22 arcsec ( 10 kpc) from the nucleus of the S0 Seyfert galaxy NGC 5252 (redshift z = 0.0229; 2015ApJ...814....8K ). It has a Chandra 0.5-8 keV X-ray luminosity of 1.5 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 , which does not qualify it as an extreme ULX, and no significant signs of X-ray variability. However, it has some peculiar properties compared to other ULXs: (i) it has an optical counterpart that is clearly detected in ultraviolet and optical images obtained with the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) and the Hubble Space Telescope ( m_ 22 mag; 2015ApJ...814....8K ); (ii) its optical spectrum shows strong emission lines with a fairly small ( 13 km s ^ 1 ) systematic velocity offset from that of the nucleus of the galaxy, revealing that the ULX is likely associated with NGC 5252 ( 2015ApJ...814....8K );

NGC 5252 seems to have undergone a past interaction, as evidenced by the finding of a small-scale half-spiral of dust near the nucleus of the galaxy and of a kinematical decoupling between the stars and the gas ( 1998ApJ...505..159M ; 2015AJ....149..155K ). This, together with the size of the optical counterpart of CXO J133815.6+043255 ( 46 pc; 2015ApJ...814....8K ) being consistent with that of ultracompact dwarf galaxies, suggests that CXO J133815.6+043255 is the nucleus of a dwarf galaxy that was accreted by NGC 5252 ( 2015ApJ...814....8K,2017ApJ...844L..21K ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5252 as a type 2 Seyfert lenticular hosting an off-nuclear ULX whose stellar component may be a merging dwarf-galaxy remnant.

quotes.quote: The interesting ULX (CXO J133815.6 + 043255) was identified in NGC 5252, a type 2 Seyfert lenticular galaxy. The ULX is 22 \ ( 10 kpc) away from the centre of NGC 5252 and has an X-ray luminosity L_ X-ray 1.2 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 kim_2015 . Previous studies using spectroscopic data suggest the ULX is kinematically associated with the host with velocity offset of 13 kim_2015, kim_2017 .

The host galaxy does not show morphological signs of an ongoing merger from optical images, although the complex gas distribution could suggest the host may have undergone a recent minor merger prieto_1996 . Therefore, it is natural to suggest that the ULX is the remnant core of a dwarf galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC5252, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC5252 (Fig. fig:NGC5252 ) is a lenticular galaxy that harbours a prominent AGN. If unaccounted for, the AGN significantly biases the parameters of the standard bulge+disk models, to the point that the \ index of the bulge becomes larger than 10. For this reason, in the standard model we fix the \ index of the bulge to n=4 , although this results in a best-fit with an unrealistically small bulge component.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 5252 spectra and finds intrinsic and absorption variability requiring both ionized and neutral partial-covering absorption components.

quotes.quote: NGC 5252: We analyzed the \ and \ spectra of the source. The source showed intrinsic and absorption variability, requiring both ionized and neutral partial covering absorption. The best-fit model, in XSPEC parlance, can be written as: zpcfabs zxipcf zcutoffpl + pexmon + Apec + Apec . For the \ observation, due to the lack of good-quality simultaneous soft X-ray observations, we cannot constrain the parameters of both absorption components.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 5252 as a low-obscuration, low-equivalent-width source with a two-sided cone and Eddington rate consistent with torus disappearance at low accretion rates.

quotes.quote: We find that 12 objects do not present any signs of reflection (namely UGC 5101, NGC 3628, NGC 3718, M 106, NGC 7331, NGC 612, 2MXJ 1105+5856, NGC 3621, NGC 3786, M 88, NGC 5252 and 2MXJ 2325-3826).

As for NGC 5252 is greatly known for having a two-sided cone Tadhunter-89 . In agreement with our results, finds that this source is low-obscured with a low EW. Moreover, finds for this source a _ Edd 0.005 , which is in agreement with the idea of the torus disappearance at low Eddington rates.

sources.summary: The recurrence analysis identifies NGC 5252 as non-stationary in Swift/BAT light curves and also finds recurrence-plot evidence for non-linearity in the source.

quotes.quote: We find that several of the RPs show evidence for non-stationarity in which large swaths of white in the RP appear far from the main diagonal. Non-stationarity in this context is defined as deviant sections of the phase-space projection of the light curve, indicating times that the source is undergoing a state change or the underlying dynamics have deviated strongly from the rest of the observations. This is most evident in Cen A (Fig. fig:AGN_RPs_select ) and NGC 5252 (in the supplemental figures available online).

Only one object was determined to be both difference and trend non-stationary (NGC 5252) and 17 sources were determined to be trend non-stationary (identified in Table tab:table2 ). Non-stationarity in the underlying dynamical process can also be seen in the RPs of some sources, as noted in Cen A and NGC 5252.
From the recurrence plot analysis we additionally found evidence for non-linearity in 3C 111, ESO 297-018, NGC 3516, NGC 4388, NGC 5252, and NGC 6814.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5252 as an early-type Seyfert with large ionization cones, possible merger debris, and Chandra evidence for photoionized extended soft X-rays.

quotes.quote: One of the most extended ionization cones ( 40 kpc across) in the optical ever observed in nearby galaxies is harbored in NGC 5252 1989Natur.341..422T , an early type (S0) Seyfert 1.9 galaxy 1993ApJ...414..552O at redshift z =0.023 (corresponding to a luminosity distance D_L =100.8 Mpc and a scale of 0.467 kpc per arcsec; NED http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ ).

The significant misalignment between one circumnuclear gas disk and the stellar disk leads to the conclusion that NGC 5252 possibly went through a galaxy merger 1998ApJ...505..159M . The lack of close companion galaxies to NGC 5252 1996A&A...306...39F and absence of disturbed velocity field of the gas indicate that it is unlikely that the merger event occurred in the recent past, instead the observed emission line arcs are likely part of the debris gas from this putative galaxy-galaxy encounter illuminated by the AGN 1998ApJ...505..159M .

The spectral analysis with Chandra data and lacking of kinematical evidence for outflow supports the previous conclusion that the extended soft X-rays are due to photoionization and the ionizing source is the active nucleus of NGC 5252.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 5252's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 5252 is a lenticular galaxy at a redshift of z= 0.0230 ( D 109 Mpc) with an AGN optically classified as either a Sy 1.9 osterbrock_spectroscopic_1993,trippe_multi-wavelength_2010 or a Sy 2.0 veron-cetty_catalogue_2010 . It is listed in the nine-month BAT AGN sample. The galaxy features a compact radio core with jet-like extended emission (diameter 4 2 kpc, PA 175 ) wilson_ionization_1994 and very prominent, kiloparsec-scale, shell-like ionization cones along a PA 167 tadhunter_anisotropic_1989 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5252 as one of five objects with very large iron-K equivalent width because the reflection continuum is weak, interpreting its torus as Compton-thin and reproducible with a Monte-Carlo torus model.

quotes.quote: In the rest of objects (NGC 4388, NGC 5252, LEDA 170194, Mrk 520, and Mrk 915), the EW is very large ( >3 keV) because the reflection continuum (i.e., R ) is apparently very weak. We interpret that the tori in these objects are Compton-thin, thus producing a much weaker hump at 30 keV and absorption iron-K edge features than what the pexrav model predicts. In fact, we confirm that a Monte-Carlo based torus model by can well reproduce the spectra of these objects including the continuum and iron-K emission line, as done in , , and .

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 5252 as a fading-AGN sample galaxy with well-documented ionization cones, includes archival WFPC2 emission-line imaging, and gives a 64-degree opening angle for its outer structures.

quotes.quote: These include NGC 5252 with its well-documented set of ionization cones ( PrietoFreudling , morse ), and the large-scale double radio source hosts NGC 5972 VCV2001 and Mkn 1498 Huub .

HST imaging Central to our analysis is a set of emission-line images of our fading-AGN candidates obtained with the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) and narrowband filters in the Wide Field Camera 3 (WFC3) or tunable ramp filters on the Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS), using archival WFPC2 data for NGC 5252.
The rest are broader, with NGC 5252 at 64^ , UGC 7342 at 75^ , and NGC 5972 at 96^ to encompass the outer structures.

sources.summary: The S7 notes describe NGC 5252 as having a spectacular biconical ionization structure with arcs, strong inner-region line splitting, and evidence for broad nuclear emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 5252: This galaxy displays a spectacular bi-conical ionization structure with arcs, first described by Tadhunter89 . In the bright inner region, strong line splitting is visible. A spectral analysis of this galaxy was performed by Acosta96 ; both their data and our data provides evidence for the presence of a broad component to \ in the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5252 as a Seyfert galaxy with a symmetric Z-like NLR, large ionized arcs, and one-sided velocity patterns between opposite ionization cones.

quotes.quote: Figure gal shows, as an example, two [ OIII ] emission-line images of ionization cones in the galaxies NGC 3516 (Sy 1 type) and NGC 5252 (Sy 1.9 type) and the orientation of the axes of their radio jets.

The NLR in the NGC 5252 galaxy (Fig. gal b and ) also has a symmetric Z-like shape in the central region <10-15'' ( 5-7 ) kpc, whereas the biconical structure itself, which contains regular emission-line ``arcs'' can be observed out to 18 kpc from the center.

Moreover, in some galaxies, like, e.g., in NGC 5252, the velocity fields in the region of ``arcs'' at large distances from the nucleus exhibit emission-line filaments that are blueshifted exclusively in one cone and redshifted exclusively in the diametrically opposite cone.

gmw_00000231.name: NGC 5258
gmw_00000231.overview: NGC 5258 is a massive spiral galaxy [arXiv:2006.12523] interacting with NGC 5257 in the early stages of a merger [arXiv:2006.12523, arXiv:1310.2770]. Its star formation is primarily concentrated in the southern arm [arXiv:2006.12523, arXiv:1811.04158], while its nuclear region is dominated by older stars [arXiv:1204.5520].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5258 is the other Arp 240 spiral, with molecular gas in the south arm and strongly varying line ratios across its regions.

quotes.quote: Arp 240 is composed two massive spiral galaxies, NGC 5257 and NGC 5258. The two galaxies are intertwined in HI Iono_2005 but well separated in optical images. The projected distance of the two galaxies is about 40 kpc, which is larger than the separation of Arp 55 ( 9 kpc). Simulations Privon_2013 show the two galaxies are in the early stage of the merging process and have just been through first passage.

For NGC 5258, different regions have very different ratio values. The central region particularly has low ratio values compared to normal spiral galaxies. This suggests that \ in this region is moderately optically thick. Since the central region also has low \ intensity, the low / \ ratio suggests a low \ abundance ratio there.

sources.summary: NGC 5258 is the other spiral in Arp 240; the paper finds it contributes to the 100 Mpc interacting pair, has star formation mainly in its southern arm, and shows non-circular motions linked to bars, HII regions, and tidal response.

quotes.quote: The pair of galaxies Arp 240 is an interacting galaxy pair consisting of two spiral galaxies: NGC 5257 and NGC 5258 (Figure arp240_img ). Using the line-of-sight (LOS) radial velocities of the galaxies, corrected for the Local Group infall onto Virgo (6798 \ km \ s ^ 1 and 6757 \ km \ s ^ 1 , for NGC 5257 and NGC 5258, respectively Values taken from the LEDA database. ), the galaxy pair is located at a distance of 100.0 \ Mpc. This implies linear diameters of 51.7 kpc (106.6 ) and 48.3 kpc (99.6 ), for NGC 5257 and NGC 5258, respectively, considering the D_ 25 values by 1991rc3..book.....D , and a projected separation between the two galaxies of 37.8 kpc (78 ).

In the case of NGC 5257, the SF is spread over the whole disc of the galaxy, while for NGC 5258, the SF is mainly confined to a particular region in the southern arm where gas is being fueled towards the centre. Non-circular motions are detected in both galaxies. For both galaxies, in the inner parts of the discs, these motions are typically associated to spiral arms, and HII regions, and to streaming motion along the bar. For the outer parts of each galaxy, they are associated with the presence of the companion and the tidal response of the disc.

sources.summary: NGC 5258 is described as a near twin of NGC 5257 with higher extinction, a flatter metallicity gradient, old nuclear stars, post-starburst populations, and southwest star formation.

quotes.quote: IRAS F13373+0105 E (NGC 5258) NGC 5258 is a near-twin to NGC 5257 in mass and luminosity. Our spectra indicate higher extinction and a flatter gradient, though the extent of measurable HII-region metallicities is smaller than in NGC 5257. Again similar to NGC 5257 the nuclear region of NGC 5258 is dominated by older stars and very little line-emission and there is some evidence of younger post-starburst populations away from the nucleus. The strongest line emission is associated with the knot of star formation to the southwest of the nuclear region.

sources.summary: The atlas gives NGC 5258's AGN or host classification, redshift context, and object-specific MIR or multiwavelength observational properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 5258 is a disturbed spiral galaxy interacting with NGC 5257 (1.3 \ towards the north-west) at a redshift of z= 0.0225 ( D 173 Mpc). It possibly harbours an AGN (according to NED) classified as a borderline LINER/H II nucleus (no reference could be retrieved), while classify it as H II nucleus. No X-ray detection or subarcsecond-resolution radio observation are reported in the literature.

gmw_00000232.name: NGC 5273
gmw_00000232.overview: NGC 5273 is a nearby lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1912.06196, arXiv:2212.02484] that hosts a low-luminosity Seyfert active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1706.02489, arXiv:1912.06196]. The galaxy contains a dust absorption pattern near its nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0411026] and exhibits long-term variability, including multiple optical and near-infrared changing-look events [arXiv:2211.03801].

sources.summary: The paper focuses on NGC 5273 as a nearby low-luminosity Seyfert 1.5 galaxy, measuring its hard X-ray cut-off, absorption, coronal properties, and black-hole context.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage 2017 We perform the and /XRT joint energy spectral fitting of simultaneous observations from the broad-line Seyfert 1.5 galaxy NGC 5273. When fitted with the combination of an exponential cut-off power-law and a reflection model, a high energy cut-off is detected at 143 ^ +96 _ 40 keV with 2 significance. Existence of such cut-off is also consistent with the observed Comptonizing electron temperature when fitted with a Comptonization model independently.

Continuing the efforts to understand the hard X-ray spectral properties of AGN, we present the spectral analysis of a low-luminosity, Seyfert 1.5 galaxy NGC 5273 in the energy range 0.8-75 keV. NGC 5273 is a broad-line, nearby AGN at a redshift of 0.00362 be14 . The AGN is hosted by a faint-spiral galaxy (S0 morphology) at a distance of 16.5 1.6 Mpc to01,tu08 . Using the weighted average of the time-delay between the broad, optical recombination lines with respect to the changes in the continuum flux, be14 measured the mass of the central black hole of (4.7 1.6) 10 ^ 6 M . Subtracting M32 template spectrum from the optical spectrum of NGC 5273, os93 found a weak H emission line.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 5273 as a nearby lenticular low-luminosity Seyfert AGN, measuring X-ray reflection, iron-line reverberation, variability, black-hole mass, and inclination constraints.

quotes.quote: NGC 5273 is a nearby (z=0.00362) bentz2014 low luminosity AGN (LLAGN) hosted by a lenticular galaxy (S0 morphology) at a distance of 16. 1.6 Mpc tonry2001 . First known as a 1.9 Seyfert Galaxy osterbock , it has recently been re-classifed as 1.5 Seyfert galaxy after a re-analysis of the contribution of the host galaxy to its spectrum revealed the presence of broad components to H_ and H_ trippe2010 . Through optical reverberation mapping measurements, bentz2014 measured a black hole mass of 4.7 1.1 10^6 M_ . X-ray observations with Suzaku showed that NGC 5273 was significantly variable in X-rays kawamuro2016 . Recent analysis of a Swift+NuSTAR observation by pahari2017 found clear evidence for non-relativistic reflection in the X-ray spectrum, with also a marginal detection of a high energy cut-off at 140 keV.

We present the results of X-ray spectral-timing analysis of a 90ks XMM-Newton observation of the nearby, broad line, early-type AGN NGC5273. The X-ray spectrum revealed the clear presence of a reflection component at high energies, with a clear signature of a narrow iron line at 6.4 keV, consistent with distant reflection. Applying a relativistic reflection model, we found only marginal evidence for a broader relativistic line component.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 5273 as a variable Seyfert AGN with repeated optical and near-IR changing-look events tied to changing luminosity and BLR response.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage NGC 5273 is a known optical and X-ray variable AGN. We analyze new and archival IR, optical, UV, and X-ray data in order to characterize its long-term variability from 2000 to 2022. At least one optical changing-look event occurred between 2011 and 2014, when the AGN changed from a Type 1.8/1.9 Seyfert to a Type 1. It then faded considerably at all wavelengths, followed by a dramatic but slow increase in UV/optical brightness between 2021 and 2022. Near-IR (NIR) spectra in 2022 show prominent broad Paschen lines that are absent in an archival spectrum from 2010, making NGC 5273 one of the few AGNs to be observed changing-look in the NIR.

We propose that NGC 5273 underwent multiple changing-look events between 2000 and 2022 -- starting as a Type 1.8/1.9, NGC 5273 changes-look to a Type 1 temporarily in 2002 and again in 2014, reverting back to a Type 1.8/1.9 by 2005 and 2017, respectively. In 2022, it is again a Type 1 Seyfert. We characterize the changing-look events and their connection to the dynamic accretion and radiative processes in NGC 5273, and propose that the variable luminosity (and thus, Eddington ratio) of the source is changing how the broad line region (BLR) reprocesses the continuum emission.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5273 has the strongest variability in the sample, a small black hole mass, quasar-like near-IR/optical SED, and a weak optical-UV bump.

quotes.quote: We chose four from these sources, namely NGC 4235, NGC 4639, NGC 5033 and NGC 5273, because there exist estimates of their central black hole mass (hence we can estimate their L _ Edd ) and their nuclear luminosity is small: their L _ X /L _ Edd ratio is smaller than 10^ 3 . Therefore, it is possible that accretion in these objects operates in a radiatively inefficient mode. First, we used archival \ and \ data to measure their X-ray variability amplitude and we compared it with the variability amplitude of more luminous Seyferts.

The near IR/optical SEDs of NGC 5033 and NGC 5273 show a concave spectrum, which is almost identical, in shape, to the mean quasar SED. This spectral shape is often interpreted as the signature of the presence of the ``standard", geometrically thin/optically thick disc emission and of dust heated by the central engine. In NGC 4639 we observe the optical part of the spectrum only, which is rising towards the UV (as expected if we observe thermal emission from the accretion disc). In the case of NGC 4235 and NGC 5033, the X--ray to optical luminosity ratio is almost identical to what we observe in quasars. In NGC 4639 and NGC 5273, the optical-UV bump appears to be weaker than the X--rays by a factor of a few.
The fact that NGC 5273 shows the largest variability amplitude among the four objects in our sample is in agreement with its small black hole mass.

sources.summary: The paper reports H2 behavior unlike most galaxies and a centrally peaked [ FeII ] profile in NGC 5273, co-rotating disk gas, and a centrally peaked velocity-dispersion profile across its central kiloparsec.

quotes.quote: The H _2 line-to-continuum ratio is constant or drops significantly towards the nucleus in most galaxies, except NGC 2273, NGC 5273, and NGC 7450. For Br , in contrast, the ratio peaks towards the nucleus in many galaxies, consistent with stronger ionization mechanisms towards the active nucleus. These two results both explain the observed central drop of the H _2 /Br ratio. Of the three emission lines, the [ FeII ] line-to-continuum ratio shows the most distinct radial variations. Its profile is centrally peaked in Mrk 1, Mrk 3, NGC 262, NGC 1320, NGC 2273, NGC 3081, NGC 5273, and NGC 7450. Several sources show two maxima (or a central drop, e.g. NGC 1667, NGC 3185, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, NGC 4388, and NGC 5506). Finally, Mrk 1066, NGC 931, NGC 2110, NGC 2992, and NGC 4051 show peculiar distributions, e.g. the distribution in NGC 2110 shows three separate peaks.

However, the gas velocity curves are similar to those of the stars, which define the galaxy disk. This is the case in Mrk 1, Mrk 1066, NGC 262, NGC 931, NGC 1667, NGC 2110, NGC 2273, NGC 3081, NGC 3185, NGC 4388, NGC 5273, and NGC 5506. In these galaxies, all three emission lines display a velocity curve that follows that of the stars and is reminiscent of rotation, thus strongly implying that the line-emitting gas is located in the disk of the galaxy and rotating with the disk. Other galaxies (Mrk 3, NGC 1320, NGC 2992, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, and NGC 7450) display velocity curves different from the stellar velocity curve in one or more emission lines, which may be a signature of non-rotational motion, either in- or outflows or other non-planar motion of the gas.

The radial dispersion profiles in Mrk 3, Mrk 1066, NGC 2110, NGC 2273, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, NGC 5273, and NGC 5506 have a central peak, which indicates a higher kinetic gas temperature in the nucleus. Either the ISM is more turbulent (possibly caused by a jet- or wind-driven outflow), and/or more intensely heated by the incident radiation field. In either case, a peaked dispersion distribution is a strong indication for the AGN governing the physical state of the gas at the nucleus. The dispersion peak is most often present in [ FeII ] (indicating dust destruction close to the nucleus), and/or in Br (indicating a strong radiation field), while the H _2 line shows the least-pronounced central rise. Examples for flat velocity dispersion profiles are NGC 3081, NGC 3185, and NGC 4388, which show no significant change over up to 1.2 kpc in diameter. The velocity dispersion in all three sources is of order 100 km/s, well within the range observed for star forming regions, which is consistent with star formation being the dominant the gas excitation mechanism. Finally, the dispersion profiles in Mrk 1, NGC 262, NGC 931, NGC 1320, NGC 1667, NGC 2992, and NGC 7450 show marked differences between the three emission lines, with variations of 100 km/s or more at a given radius. This indicates that the ionized atomic and molecular gas is responding differently to the central AGN in these sources, perhaps due to the presences of asymmetric energy input (a jet or shocks) together with a stratified medium, e.g. a thin molecular disk in a thick ionized medium.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 5273 with an HST-based nuclear profile, gives a black-hole upper limit, and notes a TripleSpec helium line showing circumnuclear gas outflow.

quotes.quote: Dust has been detected with Spitzer in our third S0 galaxy, NGC 5273, totalling 2.5 10 ^5 M _ by Martini et al (2013).

NGC 5273 We fitted a nuker profile to archival HST WFPC2 PC data (Figure 5), obtaining ( , , ) = (1.8, 1.8, 0.75) and normalized the profile to the surface photometry of Mu noz Marin et al (2007) with _b = 50 pc. Figure 6 is the model fit, and ^2 per degree of freedom implies that M _ > 10 ^8 with less than 25 probability. We assumed the Tonry et al (2001) surface brightness fluctuations distance of m-M = 31.09 0.26. The TripleSpec spectrum also shows an interesting He I 10830 line (Figure 10). Silhouetted against the broad line region helium emission and its luminous (10 ^8 L _ ) x-ray gas (Liu 2011) is a P-Cyg profile of cooler (kT 30 eV) neutral gas with a terminal outflow velocity of 750 km/sec.
This object will repay IFU study of its circumnuclear gas and modelling to determine the outflow rate.

sources.summary: The paper reports a high cutoff lower limit for NGC 5273, compares several literature estimates, and says discrepant results may stem from Swift-XRT inclusion, model complexity, or unclear NuSTAR-only differences.
